<file path="models/staging/stg_accounts.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT customer_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Accounts') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_customers.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    customer_id,
    customer_first_name,
    customer_last_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Customers') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Accounts
      - name: Customers
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_accounts

      - name: customer_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_customers

      - name: customer_last_name

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_customers_with_most_accounts.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH joined_data AS (
    SELECT
        a.customer_id,
        c.customer_first_name,
        c.customer_last_name
    FROM {{ ref('stg_accounts') }} a
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_customers') }} c ON a.customer_id = c.customer_id
)
SELECT
    customer_id,
    customer_first_name,
    customer_last_name
FROM joined_data
GROUP BY customer_id, customer_first_name, customer_last_name
ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
LIMIT 1

Business question: What was the average attendance of the 2011 season that had games smaller than 519 with several teams bigger than 14 that were also part of the sport of association football?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE table_name_46 (average_attendance VARCHAR, sport VARCHAR, _number_of_teams VARCHAR, season VARCHAR, games VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">

  - name: raw
    tables:

</file>

    description: "Staging model for table_name_46 with cleaned columns."
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_table_name_46.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    average_attendance AS average_attendance,
    sport AS sport,
    _number_of_teams AS number_of_teams,
    season AS season,
    games AS games
FROM {{ source('raw', 'table_name_46') }}
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/dim_attendance_2011.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        average_attendance,
        sport,
        number_of_teams,
        season,
        games
    FROM {{ ref('stg_table_name_46') }}
)
SELECT
    average_attendance,
    sport,
    number_of_teams,
    season,
    games
FROM base
WHERE season = '2011'
AND games < 519
AND number_of_teams > 14
AND sport = 'association football'

Business question: report the total number of degrees granted between 1998 and 2002.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE campuses (campus VARCHAR, id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE degrees (degrees INTEGER, campus VARCHAR, year VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_campuses.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT campus, id FROM {{ source('raw', 'campuses') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_degrees.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT degrees, campus, year::INTEGER AS year FROM {{ source('raw', 'degrees') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: campuses
      - name: degrees
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_campuses
    columns:
      - name: campus
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: id
        tests: [unique]
  - name: stg_degrees
    columns:
      - name: degrees
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: campus
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: year
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_degrees_by_campus.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT c.campus, d.degrees
  FROM {{ ref('stg_campuses') }} c
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_degrees') }} d ON c.id = d.campus
  WHERE d.year >= 1998 AND d.year <= 2002
)
SELECT campus, SUM(degrees) AS total_degrees FROM base GROUP BY campus
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_degrees_by_campus
    description: "Total degrees granted by campus between 1998 and 2002."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per campus"
    columns:
      - name: campus
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: total_degrees
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: display those employees who contain a letter z to their first name and also display their last name, city.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE locations (city VARCHAR, location_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE employees (first_name VARCHAR, last_name VARCHAR, department_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE departments (department_id VARCHAR, location_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_locations.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT city, location_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'locations') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_employees.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT first_name, last_name, department_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'employees') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_departments.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT department_id, location_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'departments') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: locations
      - name: employees
      - name: departments
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_locations
    columns:
      - name: city
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: location_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_employees
    columns:
      - name: first_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: last_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: department_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_departments
    columns:
      - name: department_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: location_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_employee_locations.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH employees AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_employees') }}),
departments AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_departments') }}),
locations AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_locations') }})
SELECT
  e.first_name,
  e.last_name,
  l.city
FROM employees e
INNER JOIN departments d ON e.department_id = d.department_id
INNER JOIN locations l ON d.location_id = l.location_id
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/_intermediate_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: int_employee_locations

{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('int_employee_locations') }})
SELECT
  first_name,
  last_name,
  city
FROM base
WHERE first_name LIKE '%z%'
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:

Business question: Show the station name and number of trains in each station.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE station (name VARCHAR, station_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE train_station (station_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_station.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    name AS station_name,
    station_id AS station_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'station') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_train_station.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    station_id AS station_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'train_station') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: station
      - name: train_station
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_station
    columns:
      - name: station_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: station_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_train_station
    columns:
      - name: station_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_trains_by_station.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        s.station_name,
        t.station_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_station') }} s
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_train_station') }} t ON s.station_id = t.station_id
)
SELECT
    station_name,
    COUNT(*) AS train_count
FROM base
GROUP BY station_id, station_name
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_trains_by_station
    description: "Number of trains in each station."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per station"
    columns:
      - name: station_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: train_count
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Find the first names and offices of all instructors who have taught some course and also find the course description.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE professor (prof_office VARCHAR, emp_num VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE course (crs_description VARCHAR, crs_code VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE CLASS (prof_num VARCHAR, crs_code VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE employee (emp_fname VARCHAR, emp_num VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_professor.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    prof_office,
    emp_num AS professor_emp_num
FROM {{ source('raw', 'professor') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_course.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    crs_description,
    crs_code AS course_crs_code
FROM {{ source('raw', 'course') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_class.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    prof_num AS class_prof_num,
    crs_code AS class_crs_code
FROM {{ source('raw', 'CLASS') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_employee.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    emp_fname,
    emp_num AS employee_emp_num
FROM {{ source('raw', 'employee') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: professor
      - name: course
      - name: CLASS
      - name: employee
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_professor
    columns:
      - name: professor_emp_num
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_course
    columns:
      - name: course_crs_code
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_class
    columns:
      - name: class_prof_num
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: class_crs_code
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_employee
    columns:
      - name: employee_emp_num
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_instructor_courses.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH class_data AS (
    SELECT
        c.class_prof_num,
        c.class_crs_code
    FROM {{ ref('stg_class') }} c
),
employee_data AS (
    SELECT
        e.employee_emp_num,
        e.emp_fname
    FROM {{ ref('stg_employee') }} e
),
professor_data AS (
    SELECT
        p.professor_emp_num,
        p.prof_office
    FROM {{ ref('stg_professor') }} p
),
course_data AS (
    SELECT
        cr.course_crs_code,
        cr.crs_description
    FROM {{ ref('stg_course') }} cr
)
SELECT
    emp.emp_fname,
    prof.prof_office,
    c.crs_description
FROM class_data cl
JOIN employee_data emp ON cl.class_prof_num = emp.employee_emp_num
JOIN professor_data prof ON emp.employee_emp_num = prof.professor_emp_num
JOIN course_data c ON cl.class_crs_code = c.course_crs_code
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/_intermediate_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: int_instructor_courses
    description: "Joined instructor, course, and office data."
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_instructor_courses.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH instructor_course_data AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('int_instructor_courses') }}
)
SELECT
    emp_fname,
    prof_office,
    crs_description
FROM instructor_course_data
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_instructor_courses
    description: "Instructor names, offices, and course descriptions."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per instructor-course combination"
    columns:
      - name: emp_fname
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: prof_office
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: crs_description
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: What are the drivers' first, last names and id who had more than 8 pit stops or participated in more than 5 race results?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE drivers (forename VARCHAR, surname VARCHAR, driverid VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE results (driverid VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE pitstops (driverid VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_drivers.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    forename AS driver_forename,
    surname AS driver_surname,
    driverid AS driver_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'drivers') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_results.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    driverid AS result_driver_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'results') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_pitstops.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    driverid AS pitstop_driver_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'pitstops') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: drivers
      - name: results
      - name: pitstops
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_drivers
    columns:
      - name: driver_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_results
    columns:
      - name: result_driver_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_pitstops
    columns:
      - name: pitstop_driver_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_driver_pitstops.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH driver_data AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_drivers') }}
),
pitstop_data AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_pitstops') }}
)
SELECT
    d.driver_id,
    d.driver_forename,
    d.driver_surname,
    p.pitstop_driver_id
FROM driver_data d
INNER JOIN pitstop_data p ON d.driver_id = p.pitstop_driver_id
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_driver_results.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH driver_data AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_drivers') }}
),
result_data AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_results') }}
)
SELECT
    d.driver_id,
    d.driver_forename,
    d.driver_surname,
    r.result_driver_id
FROM driver_data d
INNER JOIN result_data r ON d.driver_id = r.result_driver_id
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/_intermediate_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: int_driver_pitstops
    description: "Joined driver and pitstop data."
  - name: int_driver_results
    description: "Joined driver and result data."
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_drivers_with_extreme_activity.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH pitstop_counts AS (
    SELECT
        driver_id,
        COUNT(*) AS pitstop_count
    FROM {{ ref('int_driver_pitstops') }}
    GROUP BY driver_id
    HAVING COUNT(*) > 8
),
result_counts AS (
    SELECT
        driver_id,
        COUNT(*) AS result_count
    FROM {{ ref('int_driver_results') }}
    GROUP BY driver_id
    HAVING COUNT(*) > 5
),
combined_data AS (
    SELECT driver_id FROM pitstop_counts
    UNION
    SELECT driver_id FROM result_counts
),
final_data AS (
    SELECT
        d.driver_forename,
        d.driver_surname,
        d.driver_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_drivers') }} d
    INNER JOIN combined_data c ON d.driver_id = c.driver_id
)
SELECT * FROM final_data
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_drivers_with_extreme_activity
    description: "Drivers with more than 8 pit stops or more than 5 race results."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per driver with extreme activity"
    columns:
      - name: driver_forename
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: driver_surname
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: driver_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: What are the drivers' last names and id who had 11 pit stops and participated in more than 5 race results?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE drivers (surname VARCHAR, driverid VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE results (driverid VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE pitstops (driverid VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_drivers.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    surname,
    driverid AS driver_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'drivers') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_results.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    driverid AS driver_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'results') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_pitstops.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    driverid AS driver_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'pitstops') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: drivers
      - name: results
      - name: pitstops
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_drivers
    columns:
      - name: surname
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: driver_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_results
    columns:
      - name: driver_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_pitstops
    columns:
      - name: driver_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_driver_pitstops.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH driver_pitstops AS (
    SELECT
        d.driver_id,
        d.surname,
        COUNT(p.driver_id) AS pit_stop_count
    FROM {{ ref('stg_drivers') }} d
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_pitstops') }} p ON d.driver_id = p.driver_id
    GROUP BY d.driver_id, d.surname
    HAVING COUNT(p.driver_id) = 11
),
driver_results AS (
    SELECT
        d.driver_id,
        d.surname,
        COUNT(r.driver_id) AS result_count
    FROM {{ ref('stg_drivers') }} d
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_results') }} r ON d.driver_id = r.driver_id
    GROUP BY d.driver_id, d.surname
    HAVING COUNT(r.driver_id) > 5
)
SELECT
    dp.surname,
    dp.driver_id
FROM driver_pitstops dp
JOIN driver_results dr ON dp.driver_id = dr.driver_id
</file>

  - name: int_driver_pitstops

{{ config(materialized='table') }}

    driver_id
FROM base

SELECT name AS airline_name, alid AS airline_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'airlines') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_routes.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT alid AS airline_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'routes') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: airlines
      - name: routes
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_airlines
    columns:
      - name: airline_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: airline_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_routes
    columns:
      - name: airline_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_top_airlines.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    a.airline_name,
    r.airline_id
  FROM {{ ref('stg_airlines') }} a
  INNER JOIN {{ ref('stg_routes') }} r ON a.airline_id = r.airline_id
)
SELECT
  airline_name,
  airline_id,
  COUNT(*) AS route_count
FROM base
GROUP BY airline_id, airline_name
ORDER BY route_count DESC
LIMIT 10
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_top_airlines
    description: "Top 10 airlines by number of routes operated."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per airline"
    columns:
      - name: airline_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: airline_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: route_count
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: How many donors have endowment for school named "Glenn"?

<file path="models/staging/stg_school.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT school_id, school_name FROM {{ source('raw', 'school') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_endowment.sql">

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">

models:

      - name: school_id
        tests: [not_null]

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_glenn_donors.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH glenn_schools AS (
  SELECT school_id FROM {{ ref('stg_school') }} WHERE school_name = 'Glenn'
),

        tests: [not_null]

Business question: Find the name of the customer that has been involved in the most policies.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE customers (customer_details VARCHAR, customer_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE policies (customer_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_customers.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    customer_details AS customer_details,
    customer_id AS customer_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'customers') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_policies.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    customer_id AS customer_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'policies') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: customers
      - name: policies
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_customers
    description: "Staging model for customer data."
    columns:
      - name: customer_details
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: customer_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_policies
    description: "Staging model for policy data."
    columns:
      - name: customer_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_top_customer.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH customer_policy_counts AS (
    SELECT
        c.customer_details,
        COUNT(p.customer_id) AS policy_count
    FROM {{ ref('stg_customers') }} c
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_policies') }} p ON c.customer_id = p.customer_id
    GROUP BY c.customer_details
)
SELECT
    customer_details AS top_customer_name
FROM customer_policy_counts
ORDER BY policy_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_top_customer
    description: "The customer involved in the most policies."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per top customer"
    columns:
      - name: top_customer_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: How long is the total lesson time took by customer with first name as Rylan and last name as Goodwin?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Lessons (lesson_time INTEGER, customer_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Customers (customer_id VARCHAR, first_name VARCHAR, last_name VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_lessons.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    lesson_time,
    customer_id AS customer_id_lessons
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Lessons') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_customers.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    customer_id AS customer_id_customers,
    first_name,
    last_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Customers') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Lessons
      - name: Customers
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_lessons
    columns:
      - name: lesson_time
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: customer_id_lessons
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_customers
    columns:
      - name: customer_id_customers
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: first_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: last_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_rylan_goodwin_lesson_time.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        T1.lesson_time,
        T2.customer_id_customers,
        T2.first_name,
        T2.last_name
    FROM {{ ref('stg_lessons') }} AS T1
    INNER JOIN {{ ref('stg_customers') }} AS T2 ON T1.customer_id_lessons = T2.customer_id_customers
)
SELECT SUM(lesson_time) AS total_lesson_time
FROM base
WHERE first_name = 'Rylan' AND last_name = 'Goodwin'
</file>

      model_type: report

        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_class.sql">

    crs_code AS class_crs_code

</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_course.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    dept_code AS course_dept_code,
    crs_credit AS course_crs_credit,
    crs_code AS course_crs_code
FROM {{ source('raw', 'course') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2

  - name: raw

      - name: CLASS

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">

models:

  - name: stg_course

      - name: course_dept_code

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_dept_credits.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH class_data AS (

SELECT
    course_dept_code AS dept_code,
    SUM(course_crs_credit) AS total_credits
FROM class_data
GROUP BY course_dept_code

Business question: Find the name of tracks which are in Movies playlist but not in music playlist.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE playlists (id VARCHAR, name VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE playlist_tracks (track_id VARCHAR, playlist_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE tracks (name VARCHAR, id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_playlists.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT id AS playlist_id, name AS playlist_name FROM {{ source('raw', 'playlists') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_playlist_tracks.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT track_id, playlist_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'playlist_tracks') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_tracks.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT id AS track_id, name AS track_name FROM {{ source('raw', 'tracks') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: playlists
      - name: playlist_tracks
      - name: tracks
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_playlists
    columns:
      - name: playlist_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_playlist_tracks
    columns:
      - name: track_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: playlist_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_tracks
    columns:
      - name: track_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_movies_tracks.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH movies_playlist AS (
  SELECT playlist_id FROM {{ ref('stg_playlists') }} WHERE playlist_name = 'Movies'
),
music_playlist AS (
  SELECT playlist_id FROM {{ ref('stg_playlists') }} WHERE playlist_name = 'Music'
),
movies_tracks AS (
  SELECT t.track_id, t.track_name
  FROM {{ ref('stg_tracks') }} t
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_playlist_tracks') }} pt ON t.track_id = pt.track_id
  JOIN movies_playlist mp ON pt.playlist_id = mp.playlist_id
),
music_tracks AS (
  SELECT track_name FROM {{ ref('stg_tracks') }} t
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_playlist_tracks') }} pt ON t.track_id = pt.track_id
  JOIN music_playlist mp ON pt.playlist_id = mp.playlist_id
)
SELECT mt.track_name AS track_name FROM movies_tracks mt
WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM music_tracks mu WHERE mu.track_name = mt.track_name)
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/_intermediate_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: int_movies_tracks
    description: "Tracks in Movies playlist but not in Music playlist."
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_movies_exclusive_tracks.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('int_movies_tracks') }})
SELECT track_name AS exclusive_track_name
FROM base
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_movies_exclusive_tracks
    description: "Tracks that appear in Movies playlist but not in Music playlist."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per track"
    columns:
      - name: exclusive_track_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: How many coaches does each club has? List the club id, name and the number of coaches.

CREATE TABLE club (club_id VARCHAR, club_name VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE coach (club_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_club.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
  club_id,
  club_name

</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_coach.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

  club_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'coach') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">

  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: club

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">

models:
  - name: stg_club

  - name: stg_coach

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_club_coaches.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH club AS (
  SELECT
    club_id,
    club_name
  FROM {{ ref('stg_club') }}
),
coach AS (
  SELECT

)
SELECT

      - name: coach_count

Business question: What are the movie titles and average rating of the movies with the lowest average rating?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Rating (stars INTEGER, mID VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Movie (title VARCHAR, mID VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_rating.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    stars,
    mid AS movie_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Rating') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_movie.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    title,
    mid AS movie_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Movie') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Rating
      - name: Movie
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_rating
    columns:
      - name: stars
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: movie_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_movie
    columns:
      - name: title
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: movie_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_movies_lowest_rating.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH rating_data AS (
    SELECT
        stars,
        movie_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_rating') }}
),
movie_data AS (
    SELECT
        title,
        movie_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_movie') }}
)
SELECT
    m.title,
    AVG(r.stars) AS average_rating
FROM rating_data r
JOIN movie_data m ON r.movie_id = m.movie_id
GROUP BY r.movie_id, m.title
ORDER BY AVG(r.stars)
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_movies_lowest_rating
    description: "Movie titles and their average ratings, filtered to show only the movie with the lowest rating."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per movie with the lowest average rating"
    columns:
      - name: title
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: average_rating
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

    product_id,

<file path="models/staging/stg_complaints.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    staff_id,
    product_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'complaints') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_staff.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    last_name,
    staff_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'staff') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2

  - name: raw

      - name: products
      - name: complaints

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_products
    columns:
      - name: product_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]

    columns:
      - name: staff_id

        tests: [not_null]

    columns:

        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_cheapest_complaint.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH products AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_products') }}
),
complaints AS (

),
staff AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_staff') }}
)
SELECT
    s.staff_id,
    s.last_name,
    p.product_price
FROM staff s
JOIN complaints c ON s.staff_id = c.staff_id
JOIN products p ON c.product_id = p.product_id
ORDER BY p.product_price ASC
LIMIT 1
</file>

Business question: Which owner has paid for the most treatments on his or her dogs? List the owner id and last name.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Owners (owner_id VARCHAR, last_name VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Dogs (owner_id VARCHAR, dog_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Treatments (dog_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Owners
      - name: Dogs
      - name: Treatments
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_owners
    columns:
      - name: owner_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: last_name
  - name: stg_dogs
    columns:
      - name: owner_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: dog_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_treatments
    columns:
      - name: dog_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_owners.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    owner_id AS owner_id,
    last_name AS last_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Owners') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_dogs.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    owner_id AS owner_id,
    dog_id AS dog_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Dogs') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_treatments.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    dog_id AS dog_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Treatments') }}
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_owner_treatments.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH owner_dogs AS (
    SELECT
        o.owner_id,
        o.last_name,
        d.dog_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_owners') }} o
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_dogs') }} d ON o.owner_id = d.owner_id
),
treatment_counts AS (
    SELECT
        od.owner_id,
        od.last_name,
        COUNT(t.dog_id) AS treatment_count
    FROM owner_dogs od
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_treatments') }} t ON od.dog_id = t.dog_id
    GROUP BY od.owner_id, od.last_name
)
SELECT
    owner_id,
    last_name,
    treatment_count
FROM treatment_counts
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/_intermediate_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: int_owner_treatments
    description: "Joined owner, dog, and treatment data with treatment counts per owner."
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_top_owner_by_treatments.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('int_owner_treatments') }}
)
SELECT
    owner_id,
    last_name
FROM base
ORDER BY treatment_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2

    description: "The owner with the most treatments on their dogs."

      grain: "1 row per top owner"

      - name: owner_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: last_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_visits.sql">

</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_tourist_attractions.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

Business question: Find the name and address of the department that has the highest number of students.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE student (dept_code VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE department (dept_name VARCHAR, dept_address VARCHAR, dept_code VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_student.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT dept_code FROM {{ source('raw', 'student') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_department.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT dept_name, dept_address, dept_code FROM {{ source('raw', 'department') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: student
      - name: department
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_student
    columns:
      - name: dept_code
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_department
    columns:
      - name: dept_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: dept_address
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: dept_code
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_dept_with_most_students.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH dept_counts AS (
  SELECT
    s.dept_code,
    COUNT(*) AS student_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_student') }} s
  GROUP BY s.dept_code
),
ranked_depts AS (
  SELECT
    d.dept_name,
    d.dept_address,
    dc.student_count,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY dc.student_count DESC) AS rank
  FROM {{ ref('stg_department') }} d
  JOIN dept_counts dc ON d.dept_code = dc.dept_code
)
SELECT
  dept_name,
  dept_address
FROM ranked_depts
WHERE rank = 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_dept_with_most_students
    description: "Department with the highest number of students"
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per department with most students"
    columns:
      - name: dept_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: dept_address
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: What is the name and the average gpa of department whose students have the highest average gpa?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE department (dept_name VARCHAR, dept_code VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE student (stu_gpa INTEGER, dept_code VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_department.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT dept_name, dept_code FROM {{ source('raw', 'department') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_student.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT stu_gpa, dept_code FROM {{ source('raw', 'student') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: department
      - name: student
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_department
    columns:
      - name: dept_name

      - name: dept_code

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_dept_avg_gpa.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH student_dept AS (
  SELECT
    s.dept_code,
    d.dept_name,
    s.stu_gpa
  FROM {{ ref('stg_student') }} s
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_department') }} d ON s.dept_code = d.dept_code
),
dept_avg_gpa AS (
  SELECT
    dept_name,
    AVG(stu_gpa) AS avg_gpa
  FROM student_dept
  GROUP BY dept_name, dept_code
)
SELECT
  dept_name,
  avg_gpa
FROM dept_avg_gpa
ORDER BY avg_gpa DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">

        tests: [not_null, unique]

        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: What are the codes of the countries that do not speak English and whose government forms are not Republic?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE countrylanguage (Code VARCHAR, CountryCode VARCHAR, GovernmentForm VARCHAR, LANGUAGE VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE country (Code VARCHAR, CountryCode VARCHAR, GovernmentForm VARCHAR, LANGUAGE VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_country.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    Code AS country_code,
    GovernmentForm AS government_form,
    LANGUAGE AS language
FROM {{ source('raw', 'country') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_countrylanguage.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    CountryCode AS country_code,
    LANGUAGE AS language
FROM {{ source('raw', 'countrylanguage') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: country
      - name: countrylanguage
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_country
    columns:
      - name: country_code
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_countrylanguage
    columns:
      - name: country_code
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_non_english_non_republic.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH non_republic_countries AS (
    SELECT
        country_code
    FROM {{ ref('stg_country') }}
    WHERE government_form <> 'Republic'
),
english_speaking_countries AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT
        country_code
    FROM {{ ref('stg_countrylanguage') }}
    WHERE language = 'English'
)
SELECT
    nrc.country_code AS code
FROM non_republic_countries nrc
LEFT JOIN english_speaking_countries esc ON nrc.country_code = esc.country_code
WHERE esc.country_code IS NULL
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">

models:

    description: "List of country codes that do not speak English and are not Republics."

      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per country"
</file>

Business question: Find the number of pets for each student who has any pet and student id.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE has_pet (stuid VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE student (stuid VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_has_pet.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

</file>

{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (

        tests: [not_null]

Business question: Find the name of customer who has the highest amount of loans.

SQL schemas:

<file path="models/staging/stg_loan.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

    cust_id AS loan_cust_id,
    amount AS loan_amount

</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_customer.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

    cust_id AS customer_cust_id,
    cust_name AS customer_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'customer') }}
</file>

  - name: raw

Business question: Find the maximum price of wins from the appelations in Central Coast area and produced before the year of 2005.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE APPELLATIONS (Appelation VARCHAR, Area VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE WINE (Price INTEGER, Appelation VARCHAR, year VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_appellations.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    Appelation AS appelation,
    Area AS area
FROM {{ source('raw', 'APPELLATIONS') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_wine.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    Price AS price,
    Appelation AS appelation,
    year AS wine_year
FROM {{ source('raw', 'WINE') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: APPELLATIONS
      - name: WINE
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_appellations
    columns:
      - name: appelation
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: area
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_wine
    columns:
      - name: price
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: appelation
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: wine_year
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_central_coast_wins_max_price.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH central_coast_appellations AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT appelation
    FROM {{ ref('stg_appellations') }}
    WHERE area = 'Central Coast'
),
central_coast_wins AS (
    SELECT w.price
    FROM {{ ref('stg_wine') }} w
    JOIN central_coast_appellations c ON w.appelation = c.appelation
    WHERE w.wine_year < 2005
)
SELECT MAX(price) AS max_price
FROM central_coast_wins
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: rpt_central_coast_wins_max_price
    description: "Maximum price of wines from Central Coast appellations produced before 2005."
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per result"
    columns:
      - name: max_price
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Find the student ID and middle name for all the students with at most two enrollments.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Student_Course_Enrolment (student_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Students (middle_name VARCHAR, student_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_student_course_enrolment.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT student_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Student_Course_Enrolment') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_students.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT middle_name, student_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Students') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Student_Course_Enrolment
      - name: Students
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_student_course_enrolment
    columns:
      - name: student_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_students
    columns:
      - name: middle_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: student_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_students_with_few_enrollments.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH enrolment_counts AS (
  SELECT
    student_id,
    COUNT(*) AS enrollment_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_student_course_enrolment') }}
  GROUP BY student_id
),
qualifying_students AS (
  SELECT * FROM enrolment_counts WHERE enrollment_count <= 2
)
SELECT
  qs.student_id,
  s.middle_name
FROM qualifying_students qs
JOIN {{ ref('stg_students') }} s ON qs.student_id = s.student_id
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_students_with_few_enrollments
    description: "List of students with at most two enrollments along with their middle names."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per student with <= 2 enrollments"
    columns:
      - name: student_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: middle_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

    customer_id AS complaint_customer_id

</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_customers.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    email_address AS customer_email,
    phone_number AS customer_phone,
    customer_id AS customer_customer_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'customers') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">

  - name: raw

      - name: complaints

Business question: Find the average prices of all products from each manufacture, and list each company's name.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE products (price INTEGER, Manufacturer VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE manufacturers (name VARCHAR, code VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_products.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT price, manufacturer AS manufacturer_code FROM {{ source('raw', 'products') }}
</file>

{{ config(materialized='view') }}

version: 2

  - name: stg_products

  - name: stg_manufacturers

        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_average_price_by_manufacturer.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH products AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_products') }}),
manufacturers AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_manufacturers') }})
SELECT
    m.name AS manufacturer_name,
    AVG(p.price) AS average_price
FROM products p
JOIN manufacturers m ON p.manufacturer_code = m.manufacturer_code
GROUP BY m.name
</file>

      grain: "1 row per manufacturer"
    columns:
      - name: manufacturer_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: average_price
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: What is the customer last name, id and phone number with most number of orders?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Orders (customer_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Customers (customer_last_name VARCHAR, phone_number VARCHAR, customer_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_orders.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT customer_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Orders') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_customers.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    customer_id,
    customer_last_name,
    phone_number
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Customers') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Orders
      - name: Customers
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_orders
    columns:
      - name: customer_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_customers
    columns:
      - name: customer_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: customer_last_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: phone_number
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_top_customer.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH ordered_customers AS (
    SELECT
        c.customer_last_name,
        o.customer_id,
        c.phone_number,
        COUNT(*) AS order_count
    FROM {{ ref('stg_orders') }} o
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_customers') }} c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id
    GROUP BY o.customer_id, c.customer_last_name, c.phone_number
    ORDER BY order_count DESC
    LIMIT 1
)
SELECT
    customer_last_name,
    customer_id,
    phone_number
FROM ordered_customers
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_top_customer
    description: "Customer with the most number of orders"
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per customer with most orders"
    columns:
      - name: customer_last_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: customer_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: phone_number
</file>

Business question: Find the name and city of the airport which is the destination of the most number of routes.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE airports (name VARCHAR, city VARCHAR, apid VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE routes (dst_apid VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_airports.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    name AS airport_name,
    city AS airport_city,
    apid AS airport_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'airports') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_routes.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    dst_apid AS destination_airport_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'routes') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: airports
      - name: routes
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_airports
    columns:
      - name: airport_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_routes
    columns:
      - name: destination_airport_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_top_destination_airport.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH destination_counts AS (
    SELECT
        a.airport_id,
        a.airport_name,
        a.airport_city,
        COUNT(r.destination_airport_id) AS route_count
    FROM {{ ref('stg_airports') }} a
    INNER JOIN {{ ref('stg_routes') }} r ON a.airport_id = r.destination_airport_id
    GROUP BY a.airport_id, a.airport_name, a.airport_city
)
SELECT
    airport_name AS name,
    airport_city AS city,
    airport_id AS dst_apid
FROM destination_counts
ORDER BY route_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_top_destination_airport
    description: "Airport that is the destination of the most number of routes."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per top destination airport"
    columns:
      - name: name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: city
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: dst_apid
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

    SELECT
        film_title,
        director_name,
        film_identifier
    FROM {{ ref('stg_film') }}
),
scheduled_films AS (
    SELECT
        scheduled_film_identifier
    FROM {{ ref('stg_schedule') }}
)
SELECT
    f.film_title,
    f.director_name
FROM film_data f
WHERE NOT f.film_identifier IN (SELECT scheduled_film_identifier FROM scheduled_films)
</file>

      - name: director_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: How many documents were shipped by USPS?

<file path="models/staging/stg_documents.sql">

SELECT
    Id AS document_id,
    shipping_agent_code

</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_ref_shipping_agents.sql">

    Id AS shipping_agent_id,
    shipping_agent_name

models:

      - name: document_id

        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_usps_documents.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH usps_agents AS (
    SELECT
        shipping_agent_id,
        shipping_agent_name
    FROM {{ ref('stg_ref_shipping_agents') }}
    WHERE shipping_agent_name = 'USPS'
),
document_counts AS (

    FROM {{ ref('stg_documents') }}
    JOIN usps_agents USING (shipping_agent_code)
)
SELECT
    usps_document_count
FROM document_counts
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_student.sql">

    Lname AS last_name,
    StuID AS student_id

</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_minor_in.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    StuID AS student_id,
    DNO AS department_number
FROM {{ source('raw', 'MINOR_IN') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">

  - name: raw

Business question: Show the name of the customer who has the most orders.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE customers (customer_name VARCHAR, customer_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE customer_orders (customer_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_customers.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    customer_id,
    customer_name AS name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'customers') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_customer_orders.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    customer_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'customer_orders') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: customers
      - name: customer_orders
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_customers
    columns:
      - name: customer_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_customer_orders
    columns:
      - name: customer_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_top_customer.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH joined_data AS (
    SELECT
        c.customer_id,
        c.name,
        COUNT(o.customer_id) AS order_count
    FROM {{ ref('stg_customers') }} c
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_customer_orders') }} o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
    GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.name
)
SELECT
    name AS top_customer_name
FROM joined_data
ORDER BY order_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_top_customer
    description: "Identifies the customer with the most orders."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per top customer"
    columns:
      - name: top_customer_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Which city has most number of arriving flights?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE FLIGHTS (DestAirport VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE AIRPORTS (City VARCHAR, AirportCode VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_flights.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT DestAirport FROM {{ source('raw', 'FLIGHTS') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_airports.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT City, AirportCode FROM {{ source('raw', 'AIRPORTS') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: FLIGHTS
      - name: AIRPORTS
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_flights
    columns:
      - name: DestAirport
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_airports
    columns:
      - name: City
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: AirportCode
        tests: [unique, not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_top_destination_city.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH flights_by_airport AS (
  SELECT
    DestAirport,
    COUNT(*) AS flight_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_flights') }}
  GROUP BY DestAirport
),
city_stats AS (
  SELECT
    a.City,
    f.flight_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_airports') }} a
  JOIN flights_by_airport f ON a.AirportCode = f.DestAirport
)
SELECT
  City AS top_destination_city,
  flight_count AS arriving_flights
FROM city_stats
ORDER BY flight_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_top_destination_city
    description: "Identifies the city with the highest number of arriving flights."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per top destination city"
    columns:
      - name: top_destination_city
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: arriving_flights
        tests: [not_null, greater_than_zero]
</file>

Business question: For the cars with 4 cylinders, which model has the largest horsepower?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE CAR_NAMES (Model VARCHAR, MakeId VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE CARS_DATA (Id VARCHAR, Cylinders VARCHAR, horsepower VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_car_names.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT Model AS model, MakeId AS make_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'CAR_NAMES') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_cars_data.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT Id AS id, Cylinders AS cylinders, horsepower FROM {{ source('raw', 'CARS_DATA') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: CAR_NAMES
      - name: CARS_DATA
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_car_names
    columns:
      - name: model
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: make_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_cars_data
    columns:
      - name: id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: cylinders
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_top_horsepower_4cylinder.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    cn.model,
    cd.horsepower
  FROM {{ ref('stg_car_names') }} AS cn
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_cars_data') }} AS cd ON cn.make_id = cd.id
  WHERE cd.cylinders = '4'
)
SELECT model FROM base ORDER BY horsepower DESC LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: rpt_top_horsepower_4cylinder
    description: "Top horsepower model among 4-cylinder cars."
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per top horsepower 4-cylinder model"
    columns:
      - name: model
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: Find the total ranking points for each player and their first name.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE players (first_name VARCHAR, player_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE rankings (player_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_players.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT first_name, player_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'players') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_rankings.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT player_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'rankings') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: players
      - name: rankings
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_players
    columns:
      - name: first_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: player_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_rankings
    columns:
      - name: player_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_player_ranking_points.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH player_rankings AS (
  SELECT
    p.first_name,
    COUNT(r.player_id) AS ranking_points
  FROM {{ ref('stg_players') }} p
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_rankings') }} r ON p.player_id = r.player_id
  GROUP BY p.first_name
)
SELECT
  first_name,
  SUM(ranking_points) AS total_ranking_points
FROM player_rankings
GROUP BY first_name
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_player_ranking_points
    description: "Total ranking points for each player."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per player"
    columns:
      - name: first_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: total_ranking_points
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: What is the project id and detail for the project with at least two documents?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Projects (project_id VARCHAR, project_details VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Documents (project_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Projects
      - name: Documents
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_projects
    columns:
      - name: project_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: project_details
  - name: stg_documents
    columns:
      - name: project_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_projects.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    project_id,
    project_details
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Projects') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_documents.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    project_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Documents') }}
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_projects_with_documents.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH project_data AS (
    SELECT
        p.project_id,
        p.project_details
    FROM {{ ref('stg_projects') }} AS p
    INNER JOIN {{ ref('stg_documents') }} AS d ON p.project_id = d.project_id
    GROUP BY p.project_id, p.project_details
    HAVING COUNT(*) > 2
)
SELECT * FROM project_data
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">

models:
  - name: fct_projects_with_documents
    description: "Projects with more than two associated documents"

      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per project with >2 documents"
    columns:
      - name: project_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: project_details
</file>

Business question: What is the semester which most student registered in? Show both the name and the id.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Student_Enrolment (semester_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Semesters (semester_name VARCHAR, semester_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_student_enrolment.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT semester_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Student_Enrolment') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_semesters.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT semester_name, semester_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Semesters') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Student_Enrolment
      - name: Semesters
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_student_enrolment
    columns:
      - name: semester_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_semesters
    columns:
      - name: semester_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: semester_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_top_semester.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    s.semester_name,
    s.semester_id,
    COUNT(*) AS enrolment_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_semesters') }} s
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_student_enrolment') }} e ON s.semester_id = e.semester_id
  GROUP BY s.semester_name, s.semester_id
)
SELECT semester_name, semester_id FROM base ORDER BY enrolment_count DESC LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_top_semester
    description: "Semester with the highest student enrolment count."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per semester"
    columns:
      - name: semester_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: semester_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: What are the name and id of the team with the most victories in 2008 postseason?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE postseason (team_id_winner VARCHAR, year VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE team (name VARCHAR, team_id_br VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_postseason.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    team_id_winner,
    year
FROM {{ source('raw', 'postseason') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_team.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    name,
    team_id_br AS team_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'team') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: postseason
      - name: team
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_postseason
    columns:
      - name: team_id_winner
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: year
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_team
    columns:
      - name: name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: team_id
        tests: [unique, not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_team_postseason_victories.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH postseason_2008 AS (
    SELECT
        team_id_winner,
        COUNT(*) AS victory_count
    FROM {{ ref('stg_postseason') }}
    WHERE year = '2008'
    GROUP BY team_id_winner
),
ranked_teams AS (
    SELECT
        p.team_id_winner,
        t.name,
        p.victory_count,
        RANK() OVER (ORDER BY p.victory_count DESC) AS rank
    FROM postseason_2008 p
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_team') }} t ON p.team_id_winner = t.team_id
)
SELECT
    name AS team_name,
    team_id_winner AS team_id
FROM ranked_teams
WHERE rank = 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_team_postseason_victories
    description: "Team with the most victories in 2008 postseason."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per team with most victories in 2008 postseason"
    columns:
      - name: team_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: team_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_status.sql">

      - name: staff_id

        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_staff
    columns:
      - name: staff_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]

<file path="models/intermediate/int_staff_rentals.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH customer_data AS (
    SELECT
        customer_id,
        customer_first_name,
        customer_last_name
    FROM {{ ref('stg_customer') }}
),
rental_data AS (
    SELECT
        staff_id,
        customer_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_rental') }}
),
staff_data AS (
    SELECT
        staff_id,
        staff_first_name,
        staff_last_name
    FROM {{ ref('stg_staff') }}
)
SELECT
    s.staff_id,
    s.staff_first_name,
    s.staff_last_name,
    c.customer_first_name,
    c.customer_last_name
FROM staff_data s
INNER JOIN rental_data r ON s.staff_id = r.staff_id
INNER JOIN customer_data c ON r.customer_id = c.customer_id
</file>

    SELECT
        staff_id,
        staff_first_name,
        staff_last_name,
        customer_first_name,
        customer_last_name
    FROM {{ ref('int_staff_rentals') }}
),
filtered AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT
        staff_first_name,
        staff_last_name
    FROM base
    WHERE customer_first_name = 'APRIL'
      AND customer_last_name = 'BURNS'
)
SELECT
    staff_first_name AS staff_first_name,
    staff_last_name AS staff_last_name
FROM filtered
</file>

    columns:
      - name: staff_first_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: staff_last_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Find the title and score of the movie with the lowest rating among all movies directed by each director.

<file path="models/staging/stg_rating.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_movie.sql">

SELECT title, director, m_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Movie') }}
</file>

  - name: raw

      - name: Rating

  - name: stg_movie
    columns:

        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: director
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: m_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_lowest_rated_movies.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH rating AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_rating') }}),
movie AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_movie') }})
SELECT
    m.title,
    r.stars AS lowest_rating,
    m.director
FROM rating r
JOIN movie m ON r.m_id = m.m_id
GROUP BY m.director, m.title, r.stars
QUALIFY stars = MIN(stars) OVER (PARTITION BY director)
</file>

    columns:
      - name: title
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: lowest_rating
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: director
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: What is the description, code and the corresponding count of each service type?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Services (Service_Type_Code VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Ref_Service_Types (Service_Type_Description VARCHAR, Service_Type_Code VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_services.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT Service_Type_Code AS service_type_code FROM {{ source('raw', 'Services') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_ref_service_types.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT Service_Type_Description AS service_type_description, Service_Type_Code AS service_type_code FROM {{ source('raw', 'Ref_Service_Types') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Services
      - name: Ref_Service_Types
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_services
    columns:
      - name: service_type_code
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_ref_service_types
    columns:
      - name: service_type_description
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: service_type_code
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_service_type_counts.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    s.service_type_description,
    s.service_type_code,
    COUNT(*) AS count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_ref_service_types') }} s
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_services') }} svc ON s.service_type_code = svc.service_type_code
  GROUP BY s.service_type_code, s.service_type_description
)
SELECT
  service_type_description,
  service_type_code,
  count
FROM base
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_service_type_counts
    description: "Count of services by type with descriptions."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per service type code"
    columns:
      - name: service_type_description
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: service_type_code
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: count
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Find the id and last name of the student that has the most behavior incidents?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Students (last_name VARCHAR, student_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Behavior_Incident (student_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_students.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    student_id,
    last_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Students') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_behavior_incident.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    student_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Behavior_Incident') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Students
      - name: Behavior_Incident
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_students
    columns:
      - name: student_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: last_name
  - name: stg_behavior_incident
    columns:
      - name: student_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_top_student_incidents.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH student_incidents AS (
    SELECT
        s.student_id,
        s.last_name,
        COUNT(*) AS incident_count
    FROM {{ ref('stg_behavior_incident') }} bi
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_students') }} s ON bi.student_id = s.student_id
    GROUP BY s.student_id, s.last_name
)
SELECT
    student_id,
    last_name
FROM student_incidents
ORDER BY incident_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_top_student_incidents
    description: "Identifies the student with the most behavior incidents."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per student with most incidents"
    columns:
      - name: student_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: last_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Show the names of students who have a grade higher than 5 and have at least 2 friends.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Friend (student_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Highschooler (name VARCHAR, id VARCHAR, grade INTEGER)

<file path="models/staging/stg_friend.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT student_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Friend') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_highschooler.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT name, id AS student_id, grade FROM {{ source('raw', 'Highschooler') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Friend
      - name: Highschooler
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_friend
    columns:
      - name: student_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_highschooler
    columns:
      - name: student_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: grade
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_students_with_grade_and_friends.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    h.student_id,
    h.name,
    h.grade
  FROM {{ ref('stg_highschooler') }} h
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_friend') }} f ON h.student_id = f.student_id
  WHERE h.grade > 5
),
aggregated AS (
  SELECT
    student_id,
    name,
    COUNT(*) AS friend_count
  FROM base
  GROUP BY student_id, name
  HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2
)
SELECT
  name AS student_name,
  friend_count
FROM aggregated
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_students_with_grade_and_friends
    description: "Students with grade higher than 5 and at least 2 friends."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per student"
    columns:
      - name: student_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: friend_count
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Which language is the most popular on the Asian continent?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE country (Code VARCHAR, Continent VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE countrylanguage (Language VARCHAR, CountryCode VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_country.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT Code AS country_code, Continent FROM {{ source('raw', 'country') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_countrylanguage.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT Language, CountryCode AS country_code FROM {{ source('raw', 'countrylanguage') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: country
      - name: countrylanguage
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_country
    columns:
      - name: country_code
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: continent
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_countrylanguage
    columns:
      - name: language
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: country_code
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_most_popular_asian_language.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH asian_countries AS (
  SELECT country_code FROM {{ ref('stg_country') }} WHERE continent = 'Asia'
),
asian_languages AS (
  SELECT language FROM {{ ref('stg_countrylanguage') }}
  WHERE country_code IN (SELECT country_code FROM asian_countries)
),
language_counts AS (
  SELECT language, COUNT(*) AS language_count
  FROM asian_languages
  GROUP BY language
)
SELECT language, language_count
FROM language_counts
ORDER BY language_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_most_popular_asian_language
    description: "The most popular language on the Asian continent."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per most popular language"
    columns:
      - name: language
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: language_count
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: What is the average ROM size of phones produced by the company named "Nokia Corporation"?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE phone (chip_model VARCHAR, Company_name VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE chip_model (ROM_MiB INTEGER, Model_name VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_phone.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
  chip_model AS phone_chip_model,
  Company_name AS phone_company_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'phone') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_chip_model.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
  ROM_MiB AS chip_rom_mib,
  Model_name AS chip_model_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'chip_model') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: phone
      - name: chip_model
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_phone
    description: "Cleaned phone data."
    columns:
      - name: phone_chip_model
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: phone_company_name
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_chip_model
    description: "Cleaned chip model data."
    columns:
      - name: chip_rom_mib
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: chip_model_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_nokia_phone_rom.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH nokia_phones AS (
  SELECT
    p.phone_chip_model,
    c.chip_rom_mib
  FROM {{ ref('stg_phone') }} p
  INNER JOIN {{ ref('stg_chip_model') }} c
    ON p.phone_chip_model = c.chip_model_name
  WHERE p.phone_company_name = 'Nokia Corporation'
)
SELECT AVG(chip_rom_mib) AS avg_nokia_phone_rom FROM nokia_phones
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2

  - name: rpt_nokia_phone_rom

      - name: avg_nokia_phone_rom
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">

  - name: stg_person
    description: "Staging table for Person"
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_person_friend.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

    friend AS friend_name,
    name AS person_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'PersonFriend') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_person.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

    name AS person_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Person') }}

Business question: Which building does the instructor who teaches the most number of courses live in?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE COURSE (Instructor VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE FACULTY (Building VARCHAR, FacID VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: COURSE
      - name: FACULTY
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_course
    description: "Staging model for course data."
    columns:
      - name: instructor
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_faculty
    description: "Staging model for faculty data."
    columns:
      - name: building
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: fac_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_course.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
  Instructor AS instructor
FROM {{ source('raw', 'COURSE') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_faculty.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
  Building AS building,
  FacID AS fac_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'FACULTY') }}
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_instructor_building.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH instructor_courses AS (
  SELECT
    instructor,
    COUNT(*) AS course_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_course') }}
  GROUP BY instructor
),
ranked_instructors AS (
  SELECT
    instructor,
    course_count,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY course_count DESC) AS rank
  FROM instructor_courses
)
SELECT
  f.building,
  i.course_count AS course_count
FROM ranked_instructors r
JOIN {{ ref('stg_faculty') }} f ON r.instructor = f.fac_id
WHERE r.rank = 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_instructor_building
    description: "Building where the instructor teaching the most courses lives."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per instructor with highest course count"
    columns:
      - name: building
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: course_count
        tests: [not_null, greater_than_zero]
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_genre.sql">

Business question: How many countries do not have an roller coaster longer than 3000?

<file path="models/staging/stg_country.sql">
