    country_id AS country_id,
    LENGTH AS country_length

</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">

Business question: Show the location name and code with the least documents.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Document_locations (location_code VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Ref_locations (location_name VARCHAR, location_code VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_document_locations.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT location_code FROM {{ source('raw', 'Document_locations') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_ref_locations.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT location_name, location_code FROM {{ source('raw', 'Ref_locations') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2

  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Document_locations
      - name: Ref_locations
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_document_locations
    columns:
      - name: location_code
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_ref_locations

        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: location_name
        tests: [not_null]

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_least_documents_location.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH joined_data AS (
  SELECT
    T1.location_code,
    T2.location_name
  FROM {{ ref('stg_document_locations') }} AS T1
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_ref_locations') }} AS T2 ON T1.location_code = T2.location_code
)
SELECT
  location_name,
  location_code
FROM joined_data
GROUP BY location_code, location_name
ORDER BY COUNT(*) ASC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_enzyme.sql">

    product AS enzyme_product,

{{ config(materialized='view') }}

    enzyme_id AS medicine_enzyme_interaction_enzyme_id,
    interaction_type AS medicine_enzyme_interaction_type
FROM {{ source('raw', 'medicine_enzyme_interaction') }}

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_enzyme_inhibitors.sql">

WITH enzyme_data AS (

Business question: How many distinct locations have the things with service detail 'Unsatisfied' been located in?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Timed_Locations_of_Things (Location_Code VARCHAR, thing_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Things (thing_id VARCHAR, service_details VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_timed_locations_of_things.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

    Location_Code AS location_code,
    thing_id AS thing_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Timed_Locations_of_Things') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_things.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    thing_id AS thing_id,
    service_details AS service_details
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Things') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Timed_Locations_of_Things
      - name: Things
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_timed_locations_of_things
    columns:
      - name: location_code

      - name: thing_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_things
    columns:
      - name: thing_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: service_details
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_unsatisfied_locations.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH unsatisfied_things AS (
    SELECT
        thing_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_things') }}
    WHERE service_details = 'Unsatisfied'
),
unsatisfied_locations AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT
        location_code
    FROM {{ ref('stg_timed_locations_of_things') }}
    WHERE thing_id IN (SELECT thing_id FROM unsatisfied_things)
)
SELECT
    COUNT(DISTINCT location_code) AS distinct_unsatisfied_locations_count
FROM unsatisfied_locations
</file>

      - name: distinct_unsatisfied_locations_count
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: What are the manager's first name, last name and id who won the most manager award?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE player (name_first VARCHAR, name_last VARCHAR, player_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE manager_award (player_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_player.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    name_first AS first_name,
    name_last AS last_name,
    player_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'player') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_manager_award.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    player_id AS manager_player_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'manager_award') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: player
      - name: manager_award
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_player
    columns:
      - name: first_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: last_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: player_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_manager_award
    columns:
      - name: manager_player_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_top_manager_award.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        p.first_name,
        p.last_name,
        ma.manager_player_id AS player_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_player') }} p
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_manager_award') }} ma ON p.player_id = ma.manager_player_id
),
award_counts AS (
    SELECT
        player_id,
        COUNT(*) AS award_count
    FROM base
    GROUP BY player_id
)
SELECT
    b.first_name,
    b.last_name,
    a.player_id
FROM base b
JOIN award_counts a ON b.player_id = a.player_id
ORDER BY a.award_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_top_manager_award
    description: "Top manager award winner with first name, last name and id"
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per top manager award winner"
    columns:
      - name: first_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: last_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: player_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

    GROUP BY T1.customer_id, T2.customer_name
)
SELECT
    customer_name AS customer_name
FROM base
WHERE mailshot_count >= 2
</file>

      grain: "1 row per customer with multiple orders"
    columns:
      - name: customer_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: Find the id and forenames of drivers who participated both the races with name Australian Grand Prix and the races with name Chinese Grand Prix?

<file path="models/staging/stg_races.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

</file>

{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT driverid, raceid FROM {{ source('raw', 'results') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_drivers.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT forename, driverid FROM {{ source('raw', 'drivers') }}
</file>

  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: races
      - name: results
      - name: drivers

version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_races
    columns:
      - name: raceid
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_results
    columns:
      - name: driverid
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: raceid
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_drivers
    columns:
      - name: driverid
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_driver_races.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH races AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_races') }}),
results AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_results') }}),
drivers AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_drivers') }})
SELECT
    r.raceid,
    r.name AS race_name,
    res.driverid,
    d.forename
FROM races r
INNER JOIN results res ON r.raceid = res.raceid
INNER JOIN drivers d ON res.driverid = d.driverid
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/_intermediate_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: int_driver_races
    description: "Joined races, results, and drivers data."
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_drivers_in_both_races.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('int_driver_races') }}),
australian_drivers AS (
    SELECT driverid, forename
    FROM base
    WHERE race_name = 'Australian Grand Prix'
),
chinese_drivers AS (
    SELECT driverid, forename
    FROM base
    WHERE race_name = 'Chinese Grand Prix'
)
SELECT ad.driverid, ad.forename
FROM australian_drivers ad
INNER JOIN chinese_drivers cd ON ad.driverid = cd.driverid
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_drivers_in_both_races
    description: "Drivers who participated in both Australian and Chinese Grand Prix races."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per driver"
    columns:
      - name: driverid
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: forename
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_credit_card_customers.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        c.customer_name,
        COUNT(o.order_count) AS total_orders
    FROM {{ ref('stg_customers') }} c
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_customer_orders') }} o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
    WHERE c.payment_method = 'Credit Card'
    GROUP BY c.customer_name, c.customer_id
)
SELECT
    customer_name,
    total_orders
FROM base
WHERE total_orders > 2
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">

      grain: "1 row per customer"
    columns:
      - name: customer_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: total_orders
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_captain.sql">

</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_ships_with_multiple_captains.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH captain_list AS (
  SELECT t2.ship_id

)

</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_room.sql">

      - name: block_floor

Business question: Find the name of students who took any class in the years of 2009 and 2010.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE student (name VARCHAR, id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE takes (id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_student.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    name AS student_name,
    id AS student_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'student') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_takes.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    id AS student_id,
    year
FROM {{ source('raw', 'takes') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: student
      - name: takes
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_student
    columns:
      - name: student_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: student_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_takes
    columns:
      - name: student_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: year
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_students_2009_2010.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT T1.student_name
    FROM {{ ref('stg_student') }} AS T1
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_takes') }} AS T2 ON T1.student_id = T2.student_id
    WHERE T2.year IN (2009, 2010)
)
SELECT student_name FROM base
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: rpt_students_2009_2010
    description: "List of students who took classes in 2009 or 2010."
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per student"
    columns:
      - name: student_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: What is the party of the representative that has the smallest number of votes.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE representative (Party VARCHAR, Representative_ID VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE election (Representative_ID VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_representative.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    Party,
    Representative_ID AS representative_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'representative') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_election.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    Representative_ID AS representative_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'election') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: representative
      - name: election
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_representative
    columns:
      - name: representative_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_election
    columns:
      - name: representative_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_representative_party.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH election_reps AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT representative_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_election') }}
),
representative_parties AS (
    SELECT
        r.representative_id,
        r.Party AS party
    FROM {{ ref('stg_representative') }} r
    INNER JOIN election_reps e ON r.representative_id = e.representative_id
)
SELECT party FROM representative_parties ORDER BY party LIMIT 1;
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: rpt_representative_party
    description: "Party of the representative with the smallest number of votes."
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per representative party"
    columns:
      - name: party
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: What is total number of show times per dat for each cinema?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE schedule (show_times_per_day INTEGER, cinema_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE cinema (name VARCHAR, cinema_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_schedule.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT cinema_id, show_times_per_day FROM {{ source('raw', 'schedule') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_cinema.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT cinema_id, name FROM {{ source('raw', 'cinema') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: schedule
      - name: cinema
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_schedule
    description: "Staging model for schedule table"
    columns:
      - name: cinema_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_cinema
    description: "Staging model for cinema table"
    columns:
      - name: cinema_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_show_times_per_day.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    s.cinema_id,
    c.name,
    s.show_times_per_day
  FROM {{ ref('stg_schedule') }} s
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_cinema') }} c ON s.cinema_id = c.cinema_id
)
SELECT
  name,
  SUM(show_times_per_day) AS total_show_times_per_day
FROM base
GROUP BY name
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_show_times_per_day
    description: "Total number of show times per day for each cinema"
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per cinema"
    columns:
      - name: name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: total_show_times_per_day
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Find the names of the candidates whose support percentage is lower than their oppose rate.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE candidate (people_id VARCHAR, support_rate INTEGER, oppose_rate VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE people (name VARCHAR, people_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_candidate.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    people_id AS candidate_people_id,
    support_rate,
    CAST(oppose_rate AS INTEGER) AS oppose_rate
FROM {{ source('raw', 'candidate') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_people.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    name,
    people_id AS people_people_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'people') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: candidate
      - name: people
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_candidate
    columns:
      - name: candidate_people_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: support_rate
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: oppose_rate
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_people
    columns:
      - name: name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: people_people_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_candidates_with_low_support.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        p.name,
        c.support_rate,
        c.oppose_rate
    FROM {{ ref('stg_people') }} p
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_candidate') }} c ON p.people_people_id = c.candidate_people_id
)
SELECT name FROM base WHERE support_rate < oppose_rate

Business question: For each country and airline name, how many routes are there?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE airlines (country VARCHAR, name VARCHAR, alid VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE routes (alid VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_airlines.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    country AS airline_country,
    name AS airline_name,
    alid AS airline_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'airlines') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_routes.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    alid AS route_airline_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'routes') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: airlines
      - name: routes
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_airlines
    columns:
      - name: airline_country
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: airline_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: airline_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_routes
    columns:
      - name: route_airline_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_routes_by_country_and_airline.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        a.airline_country,
        a.airline_name,
        r.route_airline_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_airlines') }} a
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_routes') }} r ON a.airline_id = r.route_airline_id
)
SELECT
    airline_country,
    airline_name,
    COUNT(*) AS route_count
FROM base
GROUP BY airline_country, airline_name
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_routes_by_country_and_airline
    description: "Count of routes grouped by country and airline name."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per country and airline combination"
    columns:
      - name: airline_country
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: airline_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: route_count
        tests: [not_null, greater_than_zero]
</file>

Business question: Find the title, credit, and department name of courses that have more than one prerequisites?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE prereq (course_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE course (title VARCHAR, credits VARCHAR, dept_name VARCHAR, course_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_prereq.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT course_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'prereq') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_course.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    title,
    credits,
    dept_name,
    course_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'course') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: prereq
      - name: course
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_prereq
    description: "Staging model for prerequisites."
    columns:
      - name: course_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_course
    description: "Staging model for courses."
    columns:
      - name: title
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: credits
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: dept_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: course_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_courses_with_multiple_prereqs.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        c.title,
        c.credits,
        c.dept_name
    FROM {{ ref('stg_course') }} AS c
    INNER JOIN {{ ref('stg_prereq') }} AS p ON c.course_id = p.course_id
)
SELECT
    title,
    credits,
    dept_name
FROM base
GROUP BY course_id, title, credits, dept_name
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1

  SELECT
    a.aircraft_id,

Business question: What are the ids and names of the architects who built at least 3 bridges ?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE architect (id VARCHAR, name VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE bridge (architect_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_architect.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT id AS architect_id, name FROM {{ source('raw', 'architect') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_bridge.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT architect_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'bridge') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: architect
      - name: bridge
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_architect
    columns:
      - name: architect_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_bridge
    columns:
      - name: architect_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_architects_with_three_plus_bridges.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    a.architect_id,
    a.name,
    COUNT(b.architect_id) AS bridge_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_architect') }} a
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_bridge') }} b ON a.architect_id = b.architect_id
  GROUP BY a.architect_id, a.name
)
SELECT
  architect_id,
  name AS architect_name
FROM base
WHERE bridge_count >= 3
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_architects_with_three_plus_bridges
    description: "Architects who built at least 3 bridges."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per architect"
</file>

Business question: Find the name of dorms which have TV Lounge but no Study Room as amenity.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE dorm (dorm_name VARCHAR, dormid VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE has_amenity (dormid VARCHAR, amenid VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE dorm_amenity (amenid VARCHAR, amenity_name VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_dorm.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    dorm_name,
    dormid AS dorm_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'dorm') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_has_amenity.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    dormid AS dorm_id,
    amenid AS amenity_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'has_amenity') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_dorm_amenity.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    amenid AS amenity_id,
    amenity_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'dorm_amenity') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: dorm
      - name: has_amenity
      - name: dorm_amenity
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_dorm
    description: "Staging model for dorm table"
    columns:
      - name: dorm_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: dorm_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_has_amenity
    description: "Staging model for has_amenity table"
    columns:
      - name: dorm_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: amenity_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_dorm_amenity
    description: "Staging model for dorm_amenity table"
    columns:
      - name: amenity_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: amenity_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_dorm_amenities.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH dorms AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_dorm') }}
),
amenity_links AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_has_amenity') }}
),
amenities AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_dorm_amenity') }}
)
SELECT
    d.dorm_id,
    d.dorm_name,
    a.amenity_name
FROM dorms d
INNER JOIN amenity_links al ON d.dorm_id = al.dorm_id
INNER JOIN amenities a ON al.amenity_id = a.amenity_id
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/_intermediate_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: int_dorm_amenities
    description: "Joined dorms with their amenities"
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_dorms_with_tv_no_study.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('int_dorm_amenities') }}
),
dorms_with_tv AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT dorm_name
    FROM base
    WHERE amenity_name = 'TV Lounge'
),
dorms_with_study AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT dorm_name
    FROM base
    WHERE amenity_name = 'Study Room'
)
SELECT
    dorm_name AS dorm_with_tv_no_study
FROM dorms_with_tv
WHERE dorm_name NOT IN (SELECT dorm_name FROM dorms_with_study)
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_dorms_with_tv_no_study
    description: "Dorms that have TV Lounge but no Study Room"
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per dorm with specific amenity criteria"
    columns:
      - name: dorm_with_tv_no_study
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_body_builder.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT People_ID FROM {{ source('raw', 'body_builder') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_people.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    Name,
    People_ID,
    CAST(Weight AS DECIMAL) AS weight
FROM {{ source('raw', 'people') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: body_builder
      - name: people
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_body_builder
    columns:
      - name: People_ID
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_people
    columns:
      - name: Name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: People_ID
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: weight
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_heaviest_body_builder.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        p.Name,
        p.Weight
    FROM {{ ref('stg_body_builder') }} AS bb
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_people') }} AS p ON bb.People_ID = p.People_ID
)
SELECT Name FROM base
ORDER BY Weight DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: rpt_heaviest_body_builder
    description: "Report showing the name of the body builder with the greatest body weight."
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per heaviest body builder"
    columns:
      - name: Name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: Find the names of schools that have more than one donator with donation amount above 8.5.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE school (School_name VARCHAR, school_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE endowment (school_id VARCHAR, amount INTEGER)

<file path="models/staging/stg_school.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    School_name AS school_name,
    school_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'school') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_endowment.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    school_id,
    amount
FROM {{ source('raw', 'endowment') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: school
      - name: endowment
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_school
    columns:
      - name: school_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_endowment
    columns:
      - name: school_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_schools_with_multiple_large_donations.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH large_donations AS (
    SELECT
        school_id,
        COUNT(*) AS donor_count
    FROM {{ ref('stg_endowment') }}
    WHERE amount > 8.5
    GROUP BY school_id
    HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
SELECT
    s.school_name
FROM {{ ref('stg_school') }} s
JOIN large_donations ld ON s.school_id = ld.school_id
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_schools_with_multiple_large_donations
    description: "Schools with more than one donator contributing above 8.5."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per school with multiple large donations"
    columns:
      - name: school_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Find all airlines that have flights from airport 'CVO' but not from 'APG'.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE AIRLINES (Airline VARCHAR, uid VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE FLIGHTS (Airline VARCHAR, SourceAirport VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_airlines.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    Airline AS airline,
    uid AS uid
FROM {{ source('raw', 'AIRLINES') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_flights.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    Airline AS airline,
    SourceAirport AS source_airport
FROM {{ source('raw', 'FLIGHTS') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: AIRLINES
      - name: FLIGHTS
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_airlines
    columns:
      - name: airline
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: uid
        tests: [unique, not_null]
  - name: stg_flights
    columns:
      - name: airline
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: source_airport
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_airlines_with_cvo_flights.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH airlines AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_airlines') }}),
flights_cvo AS (
    SELECT
        f.airline,
        f.source_airport
    FROM {{ ref('stg_flights') }} f
    WHERE f.source_airport = 'CVO'
)
SELECT DISTINCT a.airline
FROM airlines a
INNER JOIN flights_cvo fc ON a.uid = fc.airline
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_airlines_with_apg_flights.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH airlines AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_airlines') }}),
flights_apg AS (
    SELECT
        f.airline,
        f.source_airport
    FROM {{ ref('stg_flights') }} f
    WHERE f.source_airport = 'APG'
)
SELECT DISTINCT a.airline
FROM airlines a
INNER JOIN flights_apg fa ON a.uid = fa.airline
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/_intermediate_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: int_airlines_with_cvo_flights
    description: "Airlines with flights from airport CVO"
  - name: int_airlines_with_apg_flights
    description: "Airlines with flights from airport APG"
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_airlines_with_cvo_not_apg.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH cvo_airlines AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('int_airlines_with_cvo_flights') }}
),
apg_airlines AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('int_airlines_with_apg_flights') }}
)
SELECT c.airline
FROM cvo_airlines c
LEFT JOIN apg_airlines a ON c.airline = a.airline
WHERE a.airline IS NULL
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_airlines_with_cvo_not_apg
    description: "Airlines that have flights from CVO but not APG"
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per airline"
    columns:
      - name: airline
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: How many likes does Kyle have?

</file>

Business question: Show the ids of all the faculty members who participate in an activity and advise a student.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Student (FacID VARCHAR, advisor VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Faculty_participates_in (FacID VARCHAR, advisor VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_student.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    FacID AS faculty_id,
    advisor AS student_advisor
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Student') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_faculty_participates_in.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    FacID AS faculty_id,
    advisor AS activity_advisor
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Faculty_participates_in') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Student
      - name: Faculty_participates_in
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_student
    columns:
      - name: faculty_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: student_advisor
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_faculty_participates_in
    columns:
      - name: faculty_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: activity_advisor
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_faculty_advising_students.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH student_advisors AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT advisor AS faculty_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_student') }}
),
faculty_participants AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT FacID AS faculty_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_faculty_participates_in') }}
)
SELECT s.faculty_id
FROM student_advisors s
INNER JOIN faculty_participants f ON s.faculty_id = f.faculty_id
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: rpt_faculty_advising_students
    description: "List of faculty members who both advise students and participate in activities."
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per faculty member"
    columns:
      - name: faculty_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE CHARACTERISTICS (characteristic_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE products (product_id VARCHAR, product_name VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE product_characteristics (product_id VARCHAR, characteristic_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_characteristics.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT characteristic_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'characteristics') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_products.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT product_id, product_name FROM {{ source('raw', 'products') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_product_characteristics.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT product_id, characteristic_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'product_characteristics') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: characteristics
      - name: products
      - name: product_characteristics
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_characteristics
    columns:
      - name: characteristic_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_products
    columns:
      - name: product_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: product_name
  - name: stg_product_characteristics
    columns:
      - name: product_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: characteristic_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_product_characteristics.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH products AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_products') }}),
characteristics AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_characteristics') }}),
product_chars AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_product_characteristics') }})
SELECT p.product_id, p.product_name, c.characteristic_id
FROM products p
INNER JOIN product_chars pc ON p.product_id = pc.product_id
INNER JOIN characteristics c ON pc.characteristic_id = c.characteristic_id
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/_intermediate_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: int_product_characteristics

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_laurel_characteristics.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('int_product_characteristics') }})
SELECT COUNT(*) AS laurel_characteristic_count
FROM base
WHERE product_name = 'laurel'
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_laurel_characteristics
    description: "Count of characteristics for the product named 'laurel'."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per query result"
    columns:
      - name: laurel_characteristic_count
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

    columns:
      - name: stg_customer_order_customer_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: stg_customer_order_order_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_customer_products.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH joined_data AS (
    SELECT
        c.stg_customer_id,
        c.stg_customer_name,
        oi.order_item_product_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_customers') }} c
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_customer_orders') }} co ON c.stg_customer_id = co.stg_customer_order_customer_id
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_order_items') }} oi ON co.stg_customer_order_order_id = oi.order_item_order_id
)
SELECT
    stg_customer_id,
    stg_customer_name,
    order_item_product_id
FROM joined_data
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/_intermediate_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: int_customer_products
    description: "Joined customer data with their purchased products."
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_customers_with_three_products.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base_data AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('int_customer_products') }}
),
product_counts AS (
    SELECT
        stg_customer_id,
        COUNT(DISTINCT order_item_product_id) AS distinct_product_count
    FROM base_data
    GROUP BY stg_customer_id
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT order_item_product_id) >= 3
)
SELECT DISTINCT
    b.stg_customer_name AS customer_name
FROM base_data b
JOIN product_counts pc ON b.stg_customer_id = pc.stg_customer_id
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_customers_with_three_products
    description: "List of customers who have purchased at least three distinct products."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per customer with 3+ distinct products"
    columns:
      - name: customer_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Show all branch names with the number of members in each branch registered after 2015.

<file path="models/staging/stg_branch.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

    name AS branch_name,
    branch_id AS branch_id

</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_membership_register_branch.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

    branch_id AS membership_branch_id,
    register_year AS membership_register_year
FROM {{ source('raw', 'membership_register_branch') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2

  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: branch

version: 2
models:

  - name: stg_membership_register_branch
    columns:

        tests: [not_null]

        tests: [not_null]

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_branch_members_post_2015.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (

        b.branch_name,

{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT customer_name, customer_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'customers') }}

      - name: customer_id

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_customers_with_multiple_orders.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH joined_data AS (
  SELECT
    c.customer_name,
    c.customer_id
  FROM {{ ref('stg_customers') }} c
  INNER JOIN {{ ref('stg_orders') }} o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
),
aggregated_data AS (

)

  order_count
FROM aggregated_data
WHERE order_count >= 2
</file>

models:

<file path="models/intermediate/int_order_products.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH orders AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_orders') }}),
order_items AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_order_items') }}),
products AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_products') }})
SELECT o.order_id, oi.product_id
FROM orders o
INNER JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id
INNER JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_small_airlines.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        a.airline_name,

)
SELECT
    airline_name,
    flight_count
FROM base
WHERE flight_count < 200
</file>

Business question: What is the invoice number and invoice date for the invoice with most number of transactions?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Invoices (invoice_number VARCHAR, invoice_date VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Financial_transactions (invoice_number VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Invoices
      - name: Financial_transactions
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_invoices
    columns:
      - name: invoice_number
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: invoice_date
  - name: stg_financial_transactions
    columns:
      - name: invoice_number
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_invoices.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    invoice_number,
    invoice_date
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Invoices') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_financial_transactions.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    invoice_number
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Financial_transactions') }}
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_invoice_with_most_transactions.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH invoice_transaction_counts AS (
    SELECT
        t.invoice_number,
        i.invoice_date,
        COUNT(*) AS transaction_count
    FROM {{ ref('stg_financial_transactions') }} t
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_invoices') }} i ON t.invoice_number = i.invoice_number
    GROUP BY t.invoice_number, i.invoice_date
)
SELECT
    invoice_number,
    invoice_date
FROM invoice_transaction_counts
ORDER BY transaction_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">

models:

    description: "Invoice with the most number of transactions"

      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per invoice with most transactions"
    columns:
      - name: invoice_number
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: invoice_date
</file>

Business question: How many services has each resident requested? List the resident id, details, and the count in descending order of the count.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Residents_Services (resident_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Residents (resident_id VARCHAR, other_details VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_residents.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    resident_id,
    other_details
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Residents') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_residents_services.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    resident_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Residents_Services') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Residents
      - name: Residents_Services
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_residents
    columns:
      - name: resident_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_residents_services
    columns:
      - name: resident_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_service_requests.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        r.resident_id,
        r.other_details,
        rs.resident_id AS service_resident_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_residents') }} r
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_residents_services') }} rs
        ON r.resident_id = rs.service_resident_id
),
aggregated AS (
    SELECT
        resident_id,
        other_details,
        COUNT(*) AS service_count
    FROM base
    GROUP BY resident_id, other_details
)
SELECT
    resident_id,
    other_details,
    service_count
FROM aggregated
ORDER BY service_count DESC
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_service_requests
    description: "Number of services requested by each resident."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per resident"
    columns:
      - name: resident_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: other_details
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: service_count
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: What is the name and capacity of the stadium with the most concerts after 2013 ?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE concert (stadium_id VARCHAR, year INTEGER); CREATE TABLE stadium (name VARCHAR, capacity VARCHAR, stadium_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_concert.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    stadium_id,
    year
FROM {{ source('raw', 'concert') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_stadium.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    name,
    capacity,
    stadium_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'stadium') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: concert
      - name: stadium
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_concert
    description: "Staging table for concert data."
    columns:
      - name: stadium_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: year
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_stadium
    description: "Staging table for stadium data."
    columns:
      - name: name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: capacity
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: stadium_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_stadium_concerts.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH concert_data AS (
    SELECT
        stadium_id,
        year
    FROM {{ ref('stg_concert') }}
),
stadium_data AS (
    SELECT
        name,
        capacity,
        stadium_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_stadium') }}
)
SELECT
    s.name,
    s.capacity
FROM concert_data c
JOIN stadium_data s ON c.stadium_id = s.stadium_id
WHERE c.year > 2013
GROUP BY s.stadium_id, s.name, s.capacity
ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_stadium_concerts
    description: "Stadium with the most concerts after 2013."

      grain: "1 row per stadium with most concerts after 2013"
    columns:
      - name: name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: capacity
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: List first name and last name of customers that have more than 2 payments.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Customers (first_name VARCHAR, last_name VARCHAR, customer_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Customer_Payments (customer_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_customers.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    first_name,
    last_name,
    customer_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Customers') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_customer_payments.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    customer_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Customer_Payments') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Customers
      - name: Customer_Payments
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_customers
    description: "Staging model for customer data."
    columns:
      - name: first_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: last_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: customer_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_customer_payments
    description: "Staging model for customer payments."
    columns:
      - name: customer_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_customers_with_multiple_payments.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH customer_payment_counts AS (
    SELECT
        c.customer_id,
        c.first_name,
        c.last_name,
        COUNT(p.customer_id) AS payment_count
    FROM {{ ref('stg_customers') }} c
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_customer_payments') }} p ON c.customer_id = p.customer_id
    GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
)
SELECT
    first_name,
    last_name
FROM customer_payment_counts
WHERE payment_count > 2
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_customers_with_multiple_payments
    description: "Customers who have made more than 2 payments."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per customer with more than 2 payments"
    columns:
      - name: first_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: last_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: What is the document status description of the document with id 1?

SQL schemas:

<file path="models/staging/stg_documents.sql">

SELECT
    Id AS document_id,
    document_status_code

</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_ref_document_status.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    Id AS document_status_id,
    document_status_code,

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">

    columns:
      - name: document_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: document_status_code
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_ref_document_status
    columns:
      - name: document_status_id

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_document_status.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (

        d.document_status_code,

    JOIN {{ ref('stg_ref_document_status') }} r ON d.document_status_code = r.document_status_code
    WHERE d.document_id = 1
)
SELECT document_status_description FROM base

Business question: List the name of the stadium where both the player 'Walter Samuel' and the player 'Thiago Motta' got injured.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE stadium (name VARCHAR, id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE game (stadium_id VARCHAR, id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE injury_accident (game_id VARCHAR, player VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_stadium.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT id AS stadium_id, name FROM {{ source('raw', 'stadium') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_game.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT id AS game_id, stadium_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'game') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_injury_accident.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT game_id, player FROM {{ source('raw', 'injury_accident') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: stadium
      - name: game
      - name: injury_accident
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_stadium
    columns:
      - name: stadium_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_game
    columns:
      - name: game_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_injury_accident
    columns:
      - name: game_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: player
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_injury_data.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH stadiums AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_stadium') }}),
games AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_game') }}),
injuries AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_injury_accident') }})
SELECT s.name AS stadium_name, i.player
FROM games g
INNER JOIN stadiums s ON g.stadium_id = s.stadium_id
INNER JOIN injuries i ON g.game_id = i.game_id
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/_intermediate_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: int_injury_data
    description: "Combined injury and stadium data."
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_shared_injury_stadiums.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('int_injury_data') }}),
walter_samuel AS (
  SELECT stadium_name
  FROM base
  WHERE player = 'Walter Samuel'
),
thiago_motta AS (
  SELECT stadium_name
  FROM base
  WHERE player = 'Thiago Motta'
)
SELECT ws.stadium_name
FROM walter_samuel ws
INNER JOIN thiago_motta tm ON ws.stadium_name = tm.stadium_name
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_shared_injury_stadiums
    description: "Stadiums where both Walter Samuel and Thiago Motta got injured."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per shared injury stadium"
    columns:
      - name: stadium_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: List the organisation id with the maximum outcome count, and the count.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Project_outcomes (project_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Projects (organisation_id VARCHAR, project_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_projects.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    organisation_id,
    project_id AS project_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Projects') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_project_outcomes.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    project_id AS outcome_project_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Project_outcomes') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Projects
      - name: Project_outcomes
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_projects
    columns:
      - name: organisation_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: project_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_project_outcomes
    columns:
      - name: outcome_project_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_organisation_max_outcomes.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        p.organisation_id,
        COUNT(*) AS outcome_count
    FROM {{ ref('stg_projects') }} p
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_project_outcomes') }} po ON p.project_id = po.outcome_project_id
    GROUP BY p.organisation_id
)
SELECT
    organisation_id,
    outcome_count
FROM base
ORDER BY outcome_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_organisation_max_outcomes
    description: "Organisation with the maximum number of project outcomes."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per organisation with max outcomes"
    columns:
      - name: organisation_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: outcome_count
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

</file>

SELECT clubid, stuid FROM {{ source('raw', 'member_of_club') }}

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">

<file path="models/staging/stg_customers_cards.sql">

    customer_id AS customer_id,
    COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id) AS card_count

{{ config(materialized='view') }}

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_customers_with_multiple_cards.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH customers_with_cards AS (
    SELECT
        c.customer_id,
        c.customer_first_name,
        c.customer_last_name

    HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2
)

    customer_first_name,
    customer_last_name

Business question: Show the names of customers who have the most mailshots.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE customers (customer_name VARCHAR, customer_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE mailshot_customers (customer_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_customers.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    customer_id AS customer_id,
    customer_name AS customer_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'customers') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_mailshot_customers.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    customer_id AS customer_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'mailshot_customers') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: customers
      - name: mailshot_customers
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_customers
    columns:
      - name: customer_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: customer_name
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_mailshot_customers
    columns:
      - name: customer_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_top_mailshot_customer.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH mailshot_counts AS (
    SELECT
        T1.customer_id,
        COUNT(*) AS mailshot_count
    FROM {{ ref('stg_mailshot_customers') }} T1
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_customers') }} T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id
    GROUP BY T1.customer_id
)
SELECT
    T2.customer_name AS top_mailshot_customer
FROM mailshot_counts T1
JOIN {{ ref('stg_customers') }} T2 ON T1.customer_id = T2.customer_id
ORDER BY mailshot_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_top_mailshot_customer
    description: "Customer with the most mailshots."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per top customer"
    columns:
      - name: top_mailshot_customer
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: Find the number of students whose city code is NYC and who have class senator votes in the spring election cycle.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE STUDENT (StuID VARCHAR, city_code VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE VOTING_RECORD (Election_Cycle VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_student.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT StuID AS student_id, city_code FROM {{ source('raw', 'STUDENT') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_voting_record.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT Election_Cycle AS election_cycle, Class_Senator_Vote AS class_senator_vote FROM {{ source('raw', 'VOTING_RECORD') }}

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">

models:

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_nyc_senator_votes.sql">

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Maintenance_Contracts (maintenance_contract_company_id VARCHAR, contract_end_date VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Third_Party_Companies (company_name VARCHAR, company_id VARCHAR, company_type_code VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Ref_Company_Types (company_type_code VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_maintenance_contracts.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    maintenance_contract_company_id AS company_id,
    contract_end_date
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Maintenance_Contracts') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_third_party_companies.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    company_name,
    company_id,
    company_type_code AS company_type
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Third_Party_Companies') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_ref_company_types.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    company_type_code AS company_type,
    description
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Ref_Company_Types') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Maintenance_Contracts
      - name: Third_Party_Companies
      - name: Ref_Company_Types
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_maintenance_contracts
    columns:
      - name: company_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: contract_end_date
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_third_party_companies
    columns:
      - name: company_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: company_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: company_type
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_ref_company_types
    columns:
      - name: company_type
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_contract_company_types.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH contracts AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_maintenance_contracts') }}
),
companies AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_third_party_companies') }}
),
types AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_ref_company_types') }}
)
SELECT
    c.company_name,
    c.company_id,
    c.company_type,
    t.description AS company_type_description,
    ct.contract_end_date
FROM companies c
JOIN contracts ct ON c.company_id = ct.company_id
LEFT JOIN types t ON c.company_type = t.company_type
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/_intermediate_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: int_contract_company_types
    description: "Joined contracts with company details and types."
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_recent_contract_company.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('int_contract_company_types') }}
)
SELECT
    company_name,
    company_type_description AS description_of_type
FROM base
ORDER BY contract_end_date DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_recent_contract_company
    description: "Most recently ended contract company and its type."

      grain: "1 row per most recent contract"
    columns:
      - name: company_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: description_of_type
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Find the average age and experience working length of journalists working on different role type.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE news_report (work_type VARCHAR, journalist_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE journalist (age INTEGER, journalist_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_news_report.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    work_type,
    journalist_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'news_report') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_journalist.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    age,
    journalist_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'journalist') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: news_report
      - name: journalist
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_news_report
    columns:
      - name: work_type
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: journalist_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_journalist
    columns:
      - name: age
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: journalist_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_role_stats.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH journalist_data AS (
    SELECT
        age,
        journalist_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_journalist') }}
),
news_report_data AS (
    SELECT
        work_type,
        journalist_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_news_report') }}
)
SELECT
    nr.work_type,
    AVG(j.age) AS avg_age,
    AVG(j.age - EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE) + EXTRACT(YEAR FROM TO_DATE('1900-01-01', 'YYYY-MM-DD'))) AS avg_years_working
FROM journalist_data j
JOIN news_report_data nr ON j.journalist_id = nr.journalist_id
GROUP BY nr.work_type
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_role_stats
    description: "Average age and years of experience by journalist role type."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per work_type"
    columns:
      - name: work_type
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: avg_age
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: avg_years_working
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

</file>

WITH base AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_genres') }}),

SELECT b.name, COUNT(*) AS total_tracks

Business question: List singer names and number of concerts for each singer.

CREATE TABLE singer_in_concert (singer_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE singer (name VARCHAR, singer_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_singer_in_concert.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

</file>

  - name: raw

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_concerts_by_singer.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    s.singer_id,
    s.name
  FROM {{ ref('stg_singer') }} AS s
),
concerts AS (

SELECT

  COUNT(c.singer_id) AS concert_count
FROM base b

GROUP BY b.name, b.singer_id

</file>

{{ config(materialized='view') }}

</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">

  - name: raw

      - name: EMPLOYEE
      - name: CUSTOMER

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_employee_customer_count.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH customer_data AS (
  SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_customer') }}
),
employee_data AS (
  SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_employee') }}
)
SELECT
  c.last_name,
  COUNT(*) AS customer_count
FROM customer_data c
JOIN employee_data e ON c.support_rep_id = e.employee_id
GROUP BY c.last_name, c.support_rep_id

<file path="models/staging/stg_apartment_bookings.sql">

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">

models:

Business question: Show the product name and total order quantity for each product.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE products (product_name VARCHAR, product_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE order_items (order_quantity INTEGER, product_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_products.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    product_name,
    product_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'products') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_order_items.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    order_quantity,
    product_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'order_items') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2

  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: products
      - name: order_items

version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_products
    columns:

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_product_quantity.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        p.product_name,
        p.product_id,
        oi.order_quantity
    FROM {{ ref('stg_products') }} AS p
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_order_items') }} AS oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id
)
SELECT
    product_name,
    SUM(order_quantity) AS total_order_quantity
FROM base
GROUP BY product_id, product_name
</file>

      - name: total_order_quantity
        tests: [not_null]

WITH railway_data AS (
  SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_railway') }}
),
train_data AS (
  SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_train') }}
)
SELECT
    r.railway_id,
    r.railway_location AS location
FROM railway_data r
INNER JOIN train_data t ON r.railway_id = t.railway_id
GROUP BY r.railway_id, r.railway_location
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
</file>

        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: location
</file>

Business question: Find the number and averaged salary of all instructors who are in the department with the highest budget.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE department (dept_name VARCHAR, budget VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE instructor (salary INTEGER, dept_name VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_department.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    dept_name AS department_name,
    budget AS department_budget
FROM {{ source('raw', 'department') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_instructor.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    salary AS instructor_salary,
    dept_name AS department_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'instructor') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: department
      - name: instructor
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_department
    columns:
      - name: department_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_instructor
    columns:
      - name: instructor_salary
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: department_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_instructor_salary_stats.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        i.instructor_salary,
        d.department_budget
    FROM {{ ref('stg_instructor') }} i
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_department') }} d ON i.department_name = d.department_name
),
ranked AS (
    SELECT
        *
    FROM base
    ORDER BY department_budget DESC
    LIMIT 1
)
SELECT
    AVG(instructor_salary) AS average_salary,
    COUNT(*) AS instructor_count
FROM ranked
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: rpt_instructor_salary_stats
    description: "Average salary and count of instructors in the department with highest budget."
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per report"
</file>

Business question: List the medicine name and trade name which can both interact as 'inhibitor' and 'activitor' with enzymes.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE medicine (name VARCHAR, trade_name VARCHAR, id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE medicine_enzyme_interaction (medicine_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_medicine.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    id AS medicine_id,
    name AS medicine_name,
    trade_name AS medicine_trade_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'medicine') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_medicine_enzyme_interaction.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    medicine_id AS medicine_enzyme_interaction_id,
    interaction_type AS enzyme_interaction_type
FROM {{ source('raw', 'medicine_enzyme_interaction') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: medicine
      - name: medicine_enzyme_interaction
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_medicine
    columns:
      - name: medicine_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_medicine_enzyme_interaction
    columns:
      - name: medicine_enzyme_interaction_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_medicine_enzyme_interactions.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH medicine_data AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_medicine') }}
),
interaction_data AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_medicine_enzyme_interaction') }}
)
SELECT
    m.medicine_id,
    m.medicine_name,
    m.medicine_trade_name,
    i.enzyme_interaction_type
FROM medicine_data m
INNER JOIN interaction_data i ON m.medicine_id = i.medicine_enzyme_interaction_id
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/_intermediate_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: int_medicine_enzyme_interactions
    description: "Joined medicine and enzyme interaction data."
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_medicine_enzyme_interactions.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('int_medicine_enzyme_interactions') }}
),
inhibitors AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT medicine_name, medicine_trade_name
    FROM base
    WHERE enzyme_interaction_type = 'inhibitor'
),
activators AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT medicine_name, medicine_trade_name
    FROM base
    WHERE enzyme_interaction_type = 'activitor'
)
SELECT i.medicine_name, i.medicine_trade_name
FROM inhibitors i
INNER JOIN activators a ON i.medicine_name = a.medicine_name AND i.medicine_trade_name = a.medicine_trade_name
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_medicine_enzyme_interactions
    description: "Medicines that can both inhibit and activate enzymes."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per medicine with dual interaction types"
    columns:
      - name: medicine_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: medicine_trade_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Find the names and number of works of all artists who have at least one English songs.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE song (artist_name VARCHAR, languages VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE artist (artist_name VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: song
      - name: artist
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_song
    columns:
      - name: artist_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: languages
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_artist
    columns:
      - name: artist_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_song.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
  artist_name,
  languages AS song_languages
FROM {{ source('raw', 'song') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_artist.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
  artist_name AS artist_name_cleaned
FROM {{ source('raw', 'artist') }}
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_english_songs_by_artist
    description: "Count of English songs per artist who has at least one English song."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per artist with English songs"
    columns:
      - name: artist_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: english_song_count
        tests: [not_null, greater_than_zero]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_english_songs_by_artist.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH english_songs AS (
  SELECT
    s.artist_name,
    COUNT(*) AS english_song_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_song') }} s
  WHERE s.song_languages = 'english'
  GROUP BY s.artist_name
)
SELECT
  a.artist_name_cleaned AS artist_name,
  es.english_song_count
FROM {{ ref('stg_artist') }} a
INNER JOIN english_songs es ON a.artist_name_cleaned = es.artist_name
WHERE es.english_song_count >= 1
</file>

Business question: Find the names and number of works of the three artists who have produced the most songs.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE song (artist_name VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE artist (artist_name VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_artist.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT artist_name FROM {{ source('raw', 'artist') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_song.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT artist_name FROM {{ source('raw', 'song') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: artist
      - name: song
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_artist
    columns:
      - name: artist_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_song
    columns:
      - name: artist_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_top_three_artists.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    a.artist_name,
    COUNT(s.artist_name) AS song_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_artist') }} a
  INNER JOIN {{ ref('stg_song') }} s ON a.artist_name = s.artist_name
  GROUP BY a.artist_name
)
SELECT artist_name, song_count FROM base
ORDER BY song_count DESC
LIMIT 3
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">

Business question: Which city does has most number of customers?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Customers (customer_address_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Addresses (city VARCHAR, address_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_customers.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT customer_address_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Customers') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_addresses.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT city, address_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Addresses') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Customers
      - name: Addresses
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_customers
    columns:
      - name: customer_address_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_addresses
    columns:
      - name: city
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: address_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_city_customer_count.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH customers AS (
  SELECT customer_address_id FROM {{ ref('stg_customers') }}
),
addresses AS (
  SELECT city, address_id FROM {{ ref('stg_addresses') }}
),
joined AS (
  SELECT a.city
  FROM customers c
  JOIN addresses a ON c.customer_address_id = a.address_id
)
SELECT city, COUNT(*) AS customer_count
FROM joined
GROUP BY city
ORDER BY customer_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_city_customer_count
    description: "City with the most customers."
    meta:

      grain: "1 row per city"
    columns:
      - name: city
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: customer_count
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Show the official names of the cities that have hosted more than one competition.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE farm_competition (Host_city_ID VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE city (Official_Name VARCHAR, City_ID VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_city.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
  City_ID AS city_id,
  Official_Name AS official_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'city') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_farm_competition.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
  Host_city_ID AS host_city_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'farm_competition') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">

  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: city
      - name: farm_competition
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_cities_with_multiple_competitions.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH hosted_cities AS (
  SELECT
    c.city_id,
    c.official_name,
    COUNT(f.host_city_id) AS competition_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_city') }} c
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_farm_competition') }} f ON c.city_id = f.host_city_id
  GROUP BY c.city_id, c.official_name
)
SELECT
  official_name AS city_official_name,
  competition_count
FROM hosted_cities
WHERE competition_count > 1
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_party.sql">

      - name: party_id

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_party_with_most_members.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH member_counts AS (
    SELECT
        p.party_id,
        p.party_name,
        COUNT(m.party_id) AS member_count
    FROM {{ ref('stg_party') }} p
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_member') }} m ON p.party_id = m.party_id
    GROUP BY p.party_id, p.party_name
)
SELECT
    party_name AS party_with_most_members
FROM member_counts
ORDER BY member_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_addresses.sql">

    state_province_county AS address_state_province,
    address_id AS address_address_id,

Business question: Find the name and ID of the product whose total order quantity is the largest.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE order_items (product_id VARCHAR, order_quantity INTEGER); CREATE TABLE products (product_details VARCHAR, product_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_order_items.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    product_id,
    order_quantity
FROM {{ source('raw', 'order_items') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_products.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    product_details,
    product_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'products') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: order_items
      - name: products
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_order_items
    columns:
      - name: product_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: order_quantity
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_products
    columns:
      - name: product_details
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: product_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_top_product.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH order_quantity_by_product AS (
    SELECT
        o.product_id,
        p.product_details,
        SUM(o.order_quantity) AS total_order_quantity
    FROM {{ ref('stg_order_items') }} o
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_products') }} p ON o.product_id = p.product_id
    GROUP BY o.product_id, p.product_details
)
SELECT
    product_details,
    product_id
FROM order_quantity_by_product
ORDER BY total_order_quantity DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_top_product
    description: "The product with the largest total order quantity."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per top product"
    columns:
      - name: product_details
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: product_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

WITH order_items AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_order_items') }}),
