Business question: What are the birth date and birth place of the body builder with the highest total points?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE body_builder (People_ID VARCHAR, Total VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE people (Birth_Date VARCHAR, Birth_Place VARCHAR, People_ID VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_body_builder.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    People_ID AS people_id,
    Total AS total_points
FROM {{ source('raw', 'body_builder') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_people.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    Birth_Date AS birth_date,
    Birth_Place AS birth_place,
    People_ID AS people_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'people') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: body_builder
      - name: people
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_body_builder
    columns:
      - name: people_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_people
    columns:
      - name: people_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_top_body_builder.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH body_builders AS (
    SELECT
        people_id,
        total_points
    FROM {{ ref('stg_body_builder') }}
),
people_data AS (
    SELECT
        people_id,
        birth_date,
        birth_place
    FROM {{ ref('stg_people') }}
),
joined_data AS (
    SELECT
        p.birth_date,
        p.birth_place,
        bb.total_points
    FROM people_data p
    JOIN body_builders bb ON p.people_id = bb.people_id
)
SELECT
    birth_date,
    birth_place
FROM joined_data
ORDER BY total_points DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: rpt_top_body_builder
    description: "Top body builder's birth date and place based on total points."
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per top body builder"
</file>

Business question: List the name of the aircraft that has been named winning aircraft the most number of times.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE MATCH (Winning_Aircraft VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE aircraft (Aircraft VARCHAR, Aircraft_ID VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_match.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT Winning_Aircraft FROM {{ source('raw', 'MATCH') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_aircraft.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT Aircraft, Aircraft_ID FROM {{ source('raw', 'aircraft') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: MATCH
      - name: aircraft
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_match
    columns:
      - name: Winning_Aircraft
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_aircraft
    columns:
      - name: Aircraft_ID
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_winning_aircraft.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH winning_counts AS (
  SELECT
    T2.Winning_Aircraft,
    COUNT(*) AS win_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_aircraft') }} AS T1
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_match') }} AS T2 ON T1.Aircraft_ID = T2.Winning_Aircraft
  GROUP BY T2.Winning_Aircraft
)
SELECT
  Aircraft AS winning_aircraft_name,
  win_count
FROM (
  SELECT
    T1.Aircraft,
    wc.win_count,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY wc.win_count DESC) AS rank
  FROM {{ ref('stg_aircraft') }} AS T1
  JOIN winning_counts wc ON T1.Aircraft_ID = wc.Winning_Aircraft
) ranked
WHERE rank = 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">

models:
  - name: fct_winning_aircraft
    description: "Aircraft with the most wins."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per winning aircraft"
    columns:
      - name: winning_aircraft_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: win_count
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: What is the name of the movie that is rated by most of times?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Rating (mID VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Movie (title VARCHAR, mID VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_rating.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT mID AS movie_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Rating') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_movie.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT title, mID AS movie_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Movie') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2

  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Rating
      - name: Movie

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">

    columns:
      - name: movie_id

  - name: stg_movie

        tests: [not_null]
      - name: movie_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_most_rated_movie.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH movie_ratings AS (
  SELECT
    m.movie_id,
    m.title,
    COUNT(r.movie_id) AS rating_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_movie') }} m
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_rating') }} r ON m.movie_id = r.movie_id
  GROUP BY m.movie_id, m.title
)
SELECT
  movie_id,
  title,
  rating_count
FROM movie_ratings
ORDER BY rating_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

Business question: Show the description and code of the attraction type most tourist attractions belong to.

<file path="models/staging/stg_ref_attraction_types.sql">

    Attraction_Type_Code AS attraction_type_code

</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_tourist_attractions.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

    Attraction_Type_Code AS attraction_type_code
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Tourist_Attractions') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">

  - name: raw

      - name: Ref_Attraction_Types

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_popular_attraction_type.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        r.attraction_type_description,
        r.attraction_type_code,
        COUNT(*) AS attraction_count
    FROM {{ ref('stg_ref_attraction_types') }} r
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_tourist_attractions') }} t ON r.attraction_type_code = t.attraction_type_code

    attraction_type_code
FROM base
ORDER BY attraction_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

Business question: For any rating where the name of reviewer is the same as the director of the movie, return the reviewer name, movie title, and number of stars.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Rating (stars VARCHAR, mID VARCHAR, rID VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Reviewer (name VARCHAR, rID VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Movie (title VARCHAR, mID VARCHAR, director VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_rating.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT stars, mID AS movie_id, rID AS reviewer_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Rating') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_reviewer.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT name, rID AS reviewer_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Reviewer') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_movie.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT title, mID AS movie_id, director FROM {{ source('raw', 'Movie') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Rating
      - name: Reviewer
      - name: Movie
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_rating
    columns:
      - name: stars
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: movie_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: reviewer_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_reviewer
    columns:
      - name: name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: reviewer_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_movie
    columns:
      - name: title
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: movie_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: director
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_movie_reviews.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH ratings AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_rating') }}),
reviewers AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_reviewer') }}),
movies AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_movie') }})
SELECT
    r.reviewer_id,
    rev.name AS reviewer_name,
    m.movie_id,
    mo.title AS movie_title,
    mo.director,
    r.stars
FROM ratings r
INNER JOIN reviewers rev ON r.reviewer_id = rev.reviewer_id
INNER JOIN movies mo ON r.movie_id = mo.movie_id
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/_intermediate_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: int_movie_reviews
    description: "Joined data for movies, reviewers, and their ratings."
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_same_name_reviews.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('int_movie_reviews') }})
SELECT DISTINCT
    reviewer_name,
    movie_title,
    stars AS number_of_stars
FROM base
WHERE director = reviewer_name
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_same_name_reviews
    description: "Reviews where the reviewer's name matches the movie director."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per matching reviewer-director review"
    columns:
      - name: reviewer_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: movie_title
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: number_of_stars
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: How many movie reviews does each director get?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Rating (mID VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Movie (director VARCHAR, mID VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_rating.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT mid AS movie_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Rating') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_movie.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT director, mid AS movie_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Movie') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Rating
      - name: Movie
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_rating
    columns:
      - name: movie_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_movie
    columns:
      - name: director
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: movie_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_reviews_by_director.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH joined_data AS (
  SELECT
    m.director,
    r.movie_id
  FROM {{ ref('stg_movie') }} m
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_rating') }} r ON m.movie_id = r.movie_id
)
SELECT
  COUNT(*) AS review_count,
  director
FROM joined_data
GROUP BY director
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">

models:
  - name: fct_reviews_by_director
    description: "Number of movie reviews per director."

      grain: "1 row per director"
    columns:
      - name: review_count
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: director
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: Which dogs have not cost their owner more than 1000 for treatment ? List the dog names .

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE dogs (name VARCHAR, dog_id VARCHAR, cost_of_treatment INTEGER); CREATE TABLE treatments (name VARCHAR, dog_id VARCHAR, cost_of_treatment INTEGER)

<file path="models/staging/stg_dogs.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    name AS dog_name,
    dog_id AS dog_id,
    cost_of_treatment AS treatment_cost
FROM {{ source('raw', 'dogs') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_treatments.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    name AS treatment_name,
    dog_id AS dog_id,
    cost_of_treatment AS treatment_cost
FROM {{ source('raw', 'treatments') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: dogs
      - name: treatments
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_dogs
    description: "Staging model for dogs table."
    columns:
      - name: dog_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: dog_id
        tests: [unique, not_null]
  - name: stg_treatments
    description: "Staging model for treatments table."
    columns:
      - name: treatment_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: dog_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_dogs_under_threshold.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH expensive_dogs AS (
    SELECT
        dog_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_treatments') }}
    GROUP BY dog_id
    HAVING SUM(treatment_cost) > 1000
)
SELECT
    dog_name AS name
FROM {{ ref('stg_dogs') }}
WHERE dog_id NOT IN (SELECT dog_id FROM expensive_dogs)
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_dogs_under_threshold
    description: "Dogs whose treatment costs are under $1000."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per dog"
    columns:
      - name: name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">

Business question: Find the title and star rating of the movie that got the least rating star for each reviewer.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Rating (rID VARCHAR, stars INTEGER, mID VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Movie (title VARCHAR, mID VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_rating.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT rid AS r_id, stars, mid AS m_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Rating') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_movie.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT title, mid AS m_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Movie') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Rating
      - name: Movie
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_rating
    columns:
      - name: r_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: stars
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: m_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_movie
    columns:
      - name: title
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: m_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_min_rating_by_reviewer.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    t1.r_id,
    t2.title,
    t1.stars,
    MIN(t1.stars) OVER (PARTITION BY t1.r_id) AS min_stars
  FROM {{ ref('stg_rating') }} t1
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_movie') }} t2 ON t1.m_id = t2.m_id
)
SELECT
  r_id,
  title,
  stars,
  min_stars
FROM base
WHERE stars = min_stars
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_min_rating_by_reviewer
    description: "The title and star rating of the movie that got the least rating star for each reviewer."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per reviewer's minimum rated movie"
    columns:
      - name: r_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: title
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: stars
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: min_stars
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: List the name of all different customers who have some loan sorted by their total loan amount.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE loan (cust_id VARCHAR, amount INTEGER); CREATE TABLE customer (cust_name VARCHAR, cust_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_loan.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    cust_id AS loan_cust_id,
    amount AS loan_amount
FROM {{ source('raw', 'loan') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_customer.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    cust_name AS customer_cust_name,
    cust_id AS customer_cust_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'customer') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: loan
      - name: customer
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_loan
    columns:
      - name: loan_cust_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: loan_amount
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_customer
    columns:
      - name: customer_cust_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: customer_cust_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_customer_loan_amounts.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH customer_loans AS (
    SELECT
        c.customer_cust_name,
        l.loan_amount
    FROM {{ ref('stg_customer') }} c
    INNER JOIN {{ ref('stg_loan') }} l ON c.customer_cust_id = l.loan_cust_id
)
SELECT
    customer_cust_name,
    SUM(loan_amount) AS total_loan_amount
FROM customer_loans
GROUP BY customer_cust_name
ORDER BY SUM(loan_amount) DESC
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_customer_loan_amounts
    description: "Total loan amounts per customer."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per customer"
    columns:
      - name: customer_cust_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: total_loan_amount
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Which film has the most number of actors or actresses? List the film name, film id and description.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE film_actor (film_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE film (title VARCHAR, film_id VARCHAR, description VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_film_actor.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT film_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'film_actor') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_film.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT title AS film_title, film_id, description FROM {{ source('raw', 'film') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: film_actor
      - name: film
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_film_actor
    columns:
      - name: film_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_film
    columns:
      - name: film_title
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: film_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: description
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_film_with_most_actors.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH film_actor_count AS (
  SELECT
    T2.film_id,
    COUNT(*) AS actor_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_film_actor') }} AS T1
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_film') }} AS T2 ON T1.film_id = T2.film_id
  GROUP BY T2.film_id
),
top_films AS (
  SELECT film_id FROM film_actor_count ORDER BY actor_count DESC LIMIT 1
),
final_result AS (
  SELECT
    T2.film_title,
    T2.film_id,
    T2.description
  FROM top_films AS T1
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_film') }} AS T2 ON T1.film_id = T2.film_id
)
SELECT * FROM final_result
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_film_with_most_actors
    description: "Film with the highest number of actors or actresses."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per film with most actors"
    columns:
      - name: film_title
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: film_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: description
</file>

    medication AS prescribed_medication_code

  - name: raw

      - name: medication

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_prescriptions_by_brand.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH medication AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_medication') }}
),
prescribes AS (

Business question: Find the first name and major of the students who are not allegry to soy.

<file path="models/staging/stg_has_allergy.sql">

SELECT
    fname AS has_allergy_fname,
    major AS has_allergy_major,
    StuID AS has_allergy_stuid,
    Allergy AS has_allergy
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Has_allergy') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_student.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    fname AS student_fname,
    major AS student_major,
    StuID AS student_stuid,
    Allergy AS student_allergy
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Student') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">

models:

Business question: Find the number of flights landing in the city of Aberdeen or Abilene.

<file path="models/staging/stg_airports.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

    city AS city

</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_flights.sql">

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">

Business question: Find the full name of employee who supported the most number of customers.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE employees (first_name VARCHAR, last_name VARCHAR, id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE customers (support_rep_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_employees.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    id AS employee_id,
    first_name,
    last_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'employees') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_customers.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    support_rep_id AS employee_id,
    id AS customer_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'customers') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: employees
      - name: customers
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_employees
    columns:
      - name: employee_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_customers
    columns:
      - name: employee_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_top_support_rep.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH employee_support_counts AS (
    SELECT
        e.employee_id,
        e.first_name,
        e.last_name,
        COUNT(DISTINCT c.customer_id) AS customer_count
    FROM {{ ref('stg_employees') }} e
    LEFT JOIN {{ ref('stg_customers') }} c ON e.employee_id = c.employee_id
    GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
)
SELECT
    first_name,
    last_name,
    customer_count
FROM employee_support_counts
ORDER BY customer_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_top_support_rep
    description: "Employee who supported the most number of customers"
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per top support representative"
    columns:
      - name: first_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: last_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: customer_count
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: What are the country names, area and population which has both roller coasters with speed higher

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE country (name VARCHAR, area VARCHAR, population VARCHAR, Country_ID VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE roller_coaster (Country_ID VARCHAR, speed INTEGER)

<file path="models/staging/stg_country.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    name AS country_name,
    area AS country_area,
    population AS country_population,
    Country_ID AS country_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'country') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_roller_coaster.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    Country_ID AS country_id,
    speed AS roller_coaster_speed
FROM {{ source('raw', 'roller_coaster') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: country
      - name: roller_coaster
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_country
    description: "Staging model for country data"
    columns:
      - name: country_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: country_area
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: country_population
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: country_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_roller_coaster
    description: "Staging model for roller coaster data"
    columns:
      - name: country_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: roller_coaster_speed
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_country_roller_coasters.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH country_data AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_country') }}
),
roller_coaster_data AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_roller_coaster') }}
)
SELECT
    c.country_name,
    c.country_area,
    c.country_population,
    r.roller_coaster_speed
FROM country_data c
INNER JOIN roller_coaster_data r ON c.country_id = r.country_id
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/_intermediate_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: int_country_roller_coasters
    description: "Joined country and roller coaster data"
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_countries_with_speed_extremes.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('int_country_roller_coasters') }}
),
fast_countries AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT country_name, country_area, country_population
    FROM base
    WHERE roller_coaster_speed > 60
),
slow_countries AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT country_name, country_area, country_population
    FROM base
    WHERE roller_coaster_speed < 55
)
SELECT
    f.country_name,
    f.country_area,
    f.country_population
FROM fast_countries f
INNER JOIN slow_countries s ON f.country_name = s.country_name
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_countries_with_speed_extremes
    description: "Countries with both very fast and very slow roller coasters"
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per country with speed extremes"
    columns:
      - name: country_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: country_area
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: country_population
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Show the dates of performances with attending members whose roles are "Violin".

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE performance (Date VARCHAR, Performance_ID VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE member (Member_ID VARCHAR, Role VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE member_attendance (Member_ID VARCHAR, Performance_ID VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_performance.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    Date AS performance_date,
    Performance_ID AS performance_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'performance') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_member.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    Member_ID AS member_id,
    Role AS role
FROM {{ source('raw', 'member') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_member_attendance.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    Member_ID AS member_id,
    Performance_ID AS performance_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'member_attendance') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: performance

models:
  - name: stg_performance

      - name: performance_id

        tests: [not_null, unique]

    columns:
      - name: member_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: performance_id

<file path="models/intermediate/int_member_performance.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH member_attendance AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_member_attendance') }}),
performance AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_performance') }})
SELECT
    m.member_id,
    m.role,
    p.performance_date,
    p.performance_id
FROM member_attendance ma
JOIN {{ ref('stg_member') }} m ON ma.member_id = m.member_id
JOIN performance p ON ma.performance_id = p.performance_id

<file path="models/staging/stg_channel.sql">

</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_broadcast.sql">

SELECT channel_id, time_of_day FROM {{ source('raw', 'broadcast') }}

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2

  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: channel
      - name: broadcast

broadcast_data AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_broadcast') }})

INNER JOIN broadcast_data b ON c.channel_id = b.channel_id

WITH morning_channels AS (

night_channels AS (

  WHERE time_of_day = 'Night'
)
SELECT name
FROM morning_channels
INTERSECT
SELECT name
FROM night_channels

Business question: Find the name of the item with the lowest average rating.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE item (title VARCHAR, i_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE review (i_id VARCHAR, rating INTEGER)

<file path="models/staging/stg_item.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    title,
    i_id AS item_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'item') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_review.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    i_id AS item_id,
    rating
FROM {{ source('raw', 'review') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: item
      - name: review
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_item
    columns:
      - name: title
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: item_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_review
    columns:
      - name: item_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: rating
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_lowest_rated_item.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH item_reviews AS (
    SELECT
        i.title,
        r.item_id,
        AVG(r.rating) AS average_rating
    FROM {{ ref('stg_item') }} i
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_review') }} r ON i.item_id = r.item_id
    GROUP BY i.title, r.item_id
)
SELECT
    title AS item_name,
    average_rating
FROM item_reviews
ORDER BY average_rating ASC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_lowest_rated_item
    description: "The item with the lowest average rating."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per item with lowest average rating"
    columns:
      - name: item_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: average_rating
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: What are the wines that have prices higher than 50 and made of Red color grapes?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE WINE (Name VARCHAR, Grape VARCHAR, price VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Grapes (Grape VARCHAR, Color VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_wine.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    Name AS wine_name,
    Grape AS grape_type,
    price AS wine_price
FROM {{ source('raw', 'WINE') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_grapes.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    Grape AS grape_type,
    Color AS grape_color
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Grapes') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: WINE
      - name: Grapes
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_wine
    columns:
      - name: wine_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: grape_type
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: wine_price
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_grapes
    columns:
      - name: grape_type
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: grape_color
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_red_wines_above_50.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH wine_data AS (
    SELECT
        wine_name,
        grape_type,
        CAST(wine_price AS DECIMAL(10,2)) AS wine_price
    FROM {{ ref('stg_wine') }}
),
grape_data AS (
    SELECT
        grape_type,
        grape_color
    FROM {{ ref('stg_grapes') }}
)
SELECT DISTINCT w.wine_name
FROM wine_data w
INNER JOIN grape_data g ON w.grape_type = g.grape_type
WHERE g.grape_color = 'Red'
AND w.wine_price > 50
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: rpt_red_wines_above_50
    description: "List of red wines priced above 50"
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per unique red wine above 50"
    columns:
      - name: wine_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Which semesters do not have any student enrolled? List the semester name.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Student_Enrolment (semester_name VARCHAR, semester_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Semesters (semester_name VARCHAR, semester_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_semesters.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    semester_name AS semester_name,
    semester_id AS semester_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Semesters') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_student_enrolment.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    semester_name AS enrolment_semester_name,
    semester_id AS enrolment_semester_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Student_Enrolment') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Semesters
      - name: Student_Enrolment
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_semesters
    columns:
      - name: semester_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: semester_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_student_enrolment
    columns:
      - name: enrolment_semester_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: enrolment_semester_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_semesters_without_enrolment.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base_semesters AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_semesters') }}
),
base_enrolment AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT enrolment_semester_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_student_enrolment') }}
)
SELECT
    b.semester_name AS semester_name_without_enrolment
FROM base_semesters b
WHERE NOT b.semester_id IN (SELECT enrolment_semester_id FROM base_enrolment)
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: rpt_semesters_without_enrolment
    description: "List of semesters with no student enrolments"
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per semester without enrolment"
    columns:
      - name: semester_name_without_enrolment
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: Find all the instruments ever used by the musician with last name "Heilo"?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE instruments (bandmateid VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Band (id VARCHAR, lastname VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_instruments.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    bandmateid AS bandmate_id,
    instrument
FROM {{ source('raw', 'instruments') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_band.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    id AS band_id,
    lastname AS last_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Band') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: instruments
      - name: Band
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_instruments
    columns:
      - name: bandmate_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_band
    columns:
      - name: band_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_heilo_instruments.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH heilo_musicians AS (
    SELECT band_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_band') }}
    WHERE last_name = 'Heilo'
),
heilo_instruments AS (
    SELECT T1.instrument
    FROM {{ ref('stg_instruments') }} AS T1
    JOIN heilo_musicians AS T2 ON T1.bandmate_id = T2.band_id
)
SELECT instrument FROM heilo_instruments
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: rpt_heilo_instruments
    description: "List of instruments used by musicians with last name Heilo"
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per instrument used by Heilo musicians"
    columns:
      - name: instrument
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Find the name of the courses that do not have any prerequisite?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE prereq (title VARCHAR, course_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE course (title VARCHAR, course_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_course.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    title AS course_title,
    course_id AS course_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'course') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_prereq.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

    title AS prereq_title,
    course_id AS prerequisite_course_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'prereq') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">

  - name: raw

      - name: course

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">

      - name: course_title

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_courses_without_prerequisites.sql">

WITH course_data AS (

),
prereq_ids AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT course_id FROM {{ ref('stg_prereq') }}
)
SELECT
    c.course_title AS course_name
FROM course_data c
WHERE NOT c.course_id IN (SELECT course_id FROM prereq_ids)

Business question: Find all first-grade students who are NOT taught by OTHA MOYER. Report their first and last names.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE list (firstname VARCHAR, lastname VARCHAR, classroom VARCHAR, grade VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE teachers (classroom VARCHAR, firstname VARCHAR, lastname VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_list.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    firstname AS student_firstname,
    lastname AS student_lastname,
    classroom,
    grade
FROM {{ source('raw', 'list') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_teachers.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    classroom,
    firstname AS teacher_firstname,
    lastname AS teacher_lastname
FROM {{ source('raw', 'teachers') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: list
      - name: teachers
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_list
    columns:
      - name: student_firstname
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: student_lastname
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: classroom
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: grade
  - name: stg_teachers
    columns:
      - name: classroom
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: teacher_firstname
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: teacher_lastname
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_students_by_grade.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH students AS (
    SELECT
        student_firstname,
        student_lastname,
        classroom,
        grade
    FROM {{ ref('stg_list') }}
),
teachers AS (
    SELECT
        classroom,
        teacher_firstname,
        teacher_lastname
    FROM {{ ref('stg_teachers') }}
)
SELECT
    s.student_firstname,
    s.student_lastname,
    s.classroom,
    t.teacher_firstname,
    t.teacher_lastname
FROM students s
INNER JOIN teachers t ON s.classroom = t.classroom
WHERE s.grade = '1'
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_otha_moyer_students.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH students AS (
    SELECT
        student_firstname,
        student_lastname,
        classroom
    FROM {{ ref('stg_list') }}
),
teachers AS (
    SELECT
        classroom,
        teacher_firstname,
        teacher_lastname
    FROM {{ ref('stg_teachers') }}
)
SELECT
    s.student_firstname,
    s.student_lastname
FROM students s
INNER JOIN teachers t ON s.classroom = t.classroom
WHERE t.teacher_firstname = 'OTHA'
AND t.teacher_lastname = 'MOYER'
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/_intermediate_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: int_students_by_grade
    description: "First-grade students with their teachers."
  - name: int_otha_moyer_students
    description: "Students taught by OTHA MOYER."
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_first_grade_students.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH first_grade_students AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT
        student_firstname,
        student_lastname
    FROM {{ ref('int_students_by_grade') }}
),
otha_moyer_students AS (
    SELECT
        student_firstname,
        student_lastname
    FROM {{ ref('int_otha_moyer_students') }}
)
SELECT DISTINCT
    fgs.student_firstname,
    fgs.student_lastname
FROM first_grade_students fgs
LEFT JOIN otha_moyer_students oms ON fgs.student_firstname = oms.student_firstname
    AND fgs.student_lastname = oms.student_lastname
WHERE oms.student_firstname IS NULL
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_first_grade_students
    description: "First-grade students not taught by OTHA MOYER."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per first-grade student"
    columns:
      - name: student_firstname
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: student_lastname
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Find the first name and age of the students who are playing both Football and Lacrosse.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Student (fname VARCHAR, age VARCHAR, StuID VARCHAR, SportName VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Sportsinfo (fname VARCHAR, age VARCHAR, StuID VARCHAR, SportName VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_student.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    fname AS student_fname,
    age AS student_age,
    stu_id AS student_stu_id,
    sport_name AS student_sport_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Student') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_sportsinfo.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    fname AS sports_fname,
    age AS sports_age,
    stu_id AS sports_stu_id,
    sport_name AS sports_sport_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Sportsinfo') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Student
      - name: Sportsinfo
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_student
    columns:
      - name: student_fname
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: student_age
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: student_stu_id
        tests: [unique, not_null]
  - name: stg_sportsinfo
    columns:
      - name: sports_fname
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: sports_age
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: sports_stu_id
        tests: [unique, not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_students_playing_both_sports.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH football_students AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT student_stu_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_sportsinfo') }}
    WHERE sports_sport_name = 'Football'
),
lacrosse_students AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT student_stu_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_sportsinfo') }}
    WHERE sports_sport_name = 'Lacrosse'
),
both_sports_students AS (
    SELECT student_stu_id
    FROM football_students
    INTERSECT
    SELECT student_stu_id
    FROM lacrosse_students
)
SELECT
    s.student_fname,
    s.student_age
FROM {{ ref('stg_student') }} s
JOIN both_sports_students b ON s.student_stu_id = b.student_stu_id
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: rpt_students_playing_both_sports
    description: "Students who play both Football and Lacrosse"
    meta:
      model_type: report

<file path="models/staging/stg_settlements.sql">

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_largest_settlement_date.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

Business question: Find the first name and gender of student who have more than one pet.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE student (fname VARCHAR, sex VARCHAR, stuid VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE has_pet (stuid VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_student.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    fname AS student_fname,
    sex AS student_sex,
    stuid AS student_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'student') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_has_pet.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    stuid AS pet_owner_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'has_pet') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: student
      - name: has_pet
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_student
    columns:
      - name: student_fname

  - name: stg_has_pet

        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_student_pets.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH student_with_pets AS (
    SELECT
        s.student_id,
        s.student_fname,
        s.student_sex,
        COUNT(p.pet_owner_id) AS pet_count
    FROM {{ ref('stg_student') }} s
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_has_pet') }} p ON s.student_id = p.pet_owner_id
    GROUP BY s.student_id, s.student_fname, s.student_sex
)
SELECT
    student_fname AS fname,
    student_sex AS sex
FROM student_with_pets
WHERE pet_count > 1
</file>

Business question: Find the id and local authority of the station whose maximum precipitation is higher than 50.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE weekly_weather (station_id VARCHAR, precipitation INTEGER); CREATE TABLE station (id VARCHAR, local_authority VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_weekly_weather.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    station_id,
    precipitation
FROM {{ source('raw', 'weekly_weather') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_station.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    id AS station_id,
    local_authority
FROM {{ source('raw', 'station') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: weekly_weather
      - name: station
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_weekly_weather
    columns:
      - name: station_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: precipitation
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_station
    columns:
      - name: station_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: local_authority
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_high_precipitation_stations.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        t1.station_id,
        MAX(t1.precipitation) AS max_precipitation
    FROM {{ ref('stg_weekly_weather') }} t1
    GROUP BY t1.station_id
    HAVING MAX(t1.precipitation) > 50
)
SELECT
    t2.station_id,
    t2.local_authority
FROM base
JOIN {{ ref('stg_station') }} t2 ON base.station_id = t2.station_id
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_high_precipitation_stations
    description: "Stations with maximum precipitation higher than 50."

      grain: "1 row per station with high precipitation"
    columns:
      - name: station_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: local_authority
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_customer_master_index.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

    master_customer_id AS master_customer_id,
    cmi_details AS cmi_details
FROM {{ source('raw', 'customer_master_index') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_cmi_cross_references.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

    source_system_code AS source_system_code,
    master_customer_id AS master_customer_id

  - name: stg_cmi_cross_references

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_cross_reference_codes.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        cmi_details,
        master_customer_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_customer_master_index') }}
),
filtered AS (
    SELECT
        master_customer_id
    FROM base
    WHERE cmi_details = 'Gottlieb ,  Becker and Wyman'
)
SELECT DISTINCT
    c.source_system_code AS source_system_code
FROM {{ ref('stg_cmi_cross_references') }} c
JOIN filtered f ON c.master_customer_id = f.master_customer_id
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">

Business question: Find the id and last name of the teacher that has the most detentions with detention type code "AFTER"?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Detention (teacher_id VARCHAR, detention_type_code VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Teachers (last_name VARCHAR, teacher_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_detention.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    teacher_id,
    detention_type_code
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Detention') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_teachers.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    last_name,
    teacher_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Teachers') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Detention
      - name: Teachers
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_detention
    columns:
      - name: teacher_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_teachers
    columns:
      - name: teacher_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_teacher_with_most_after_detentions.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH after_detentions AS (
    SELECT
        d.teacher_id,
        t.last_name
    FROM {{ ref('stg_detention') }} d
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_teachers') }} t ON d.teacher_id = t.teacher_id
    WHERE d.detention_type_code = 'AFTER'
),
detention_counts AS (
    SELECT
        teacher_id,
        COUNT(*) AS detention_count
    FROM after_detentions
    GROUP BY teacher_id
)
SELECT
    ad.teacher_id,
    ad.last_name
FROM after_detentions ad
JOIN (
    SELECT teacher_id, MAX(detention_count) AS max_count
    FROM detention_counts
    GROUP BY teacher_id
) dc ON ad.teacher_id = dc.teacher_id AND dc.max_count = (
    SELECT MAX(detention_count)
    FROM detention_counts
)
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_teacher_with_most_after_detentions
    description: "Teacher with the most 'AFTER' type detentions"
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per teacher with most AFTER detentions"
    columns:
      - name: teacher_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: last_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Give me a list of cities whose temperature in Mar is lower than that in July and which have also served as host cities?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE city (city VARCHAR, city_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE hosting_city (host_city VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE temperature (city_id VARCHAR, Mar INTEGER, Jul VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_city.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    city AS city_name,
    city_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'city') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_hosting_city.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    host_city AS hosting_city_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'hosting_city') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_temperature.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    city_id,
    Mar AS temperature_march,
    Jul AS temperature_july
FROM {{ source('raw', 'temperature') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: city
      - name: hosting_city
      - name: temperature
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_city
    columns:
      - name: city_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: city_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_hosting_city
    columns:
      - name: hosting_city_name
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_temperature
    columns:
      - name: city_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: temperature_march
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: temperature_july
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_city_temperatures.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH city_data AS (
    SELECT
        c.city_id,
        c.city_name,
        t.temperature_march,
        t.temperature_july
    FROM {{ ref('stg_city') }} c
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_temperature') }} t ON c.city_id = t.city_id
)
SELECT
    city_id,
    city_name,
    temperature_march,
    temperature_july,
    CASE WHEN temperature_march < temperature_july THEN TRUE ELSE FALSE END AS is_cooler_in_march
FROM city_data
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_hosting_cities.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    c.city_id,
    c.city_name
FROM {{ ref('stg_city') }} c
JOIN {{ ref('stg_hosting_city') }} h ON c.city_name = h.hosting_city_name
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/_intermediate_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: int_city_temperatures
    description: "City temperature data with comparison flag"
  - name: int_hosting_cities
    description: "List of cities that have hosted events"
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_cooler_host_cities.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH cooler_cities AS (
    SELECT
        city_name,
        temperature_march,
        temperature_july
    FROM {{ ref('int_city_temperatures') }}
    WHERE is_cooler_in_march = TRUE
),
host_cities AS (
    SELECT city_name FROM {{ ref('int_hosting_cities') }}
)
SELECT
    c.city_name
FROM cooler_cities c
JOIN host_cities h ON c.city_name = h.city_name
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_cooler_host_cities
    description: "List of host cities with March temperature lower than July"
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per city"
    columns:
      - name: city_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_actor.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT Name AS actor_name, Musical_ID FROM {{ source('raw', 'actor') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_musical.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT Name AS musical_name, Musical_ID FROM {{ source('raw', 'musical') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: actor
      - name: musical
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_actor
    columns:
      - name: actor_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: musical_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_musical
    columns:
      - name: musical_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: musical_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_actors_and_musicals.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH actors AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_actor') }}),
musicals AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_musical') }})
SELECT a.actor_name, m.musical_name
FROM actors a
INNER JOIN musicals m ON a.musical_id = m.musical_id
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: rpt_actors_and_musicals
    description: "List of actors and the musicals they are in."
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per actor-musical relationship"
    columns:
      - name: actor_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: musical_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Find the number of amenities for each of the dorms that can accommodate more than 100 students.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE dorm (dormid VARCHAR, student_capacity INTEGER); CREATE TABLE has_amenity (dormid VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_dorm.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT dormid, student_capacity FROM {{ source('raw', 'dorm') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_has_amenity.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT dormid FROM {{ source('raw', 'has_amenity') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: dorm
      - name: has_amenity
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_dorm
    columns:
      - name: dormid
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: student_capacity
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_has_amenity
    columns:
      - name: dormid
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_dorm_amenities.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT d.dormid, COUNT(*) AS amenity_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_dorm') }} d
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_has_amenity') }} h ON d.dormid = h.dormid
  WHERE d.student_capacity > 100
  GROUP BY d.dormid
)
SELECT dormid, amenity_count FROM base
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_dorm_amenities
    description: "Number of amenities for dorms with capacity over 100."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per dorm"
    columns:
      - name: dormid
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: amenity_count
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Give me the average prices of wines that are produced by appelations in Sonoma County.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE WINE (Price INTEGER, Appelation VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE APPELLATIONS (Appelation VARCHAR, County VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_wine.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT Price, Appelation FROM {{ source('raw', 'WINE') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_appellations.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT Appelation, County FROM {{ source('raw', 'APPELLATIONS') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: WINE
      - name: APPELLATIONS
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_wine
    columns:
      - name: Price
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: Appelation
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_appellations
    columns:
      - name: Appelation
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: County
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_sonoma_wine_prices.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH sonoma_appellations AS (
  SELECT Appelation FROM {{ ref('stg_appellations') }}
  WHERE County = 'Sonoma'
),
sonoma_wines AS (
  SELECT w.Price FROM {{ ref('stg_wine') }} w
  JOIN sonoma_appellations s ON w.Appelation = s.Appelation
)
SELECT AVG(Price) AS average_sonoma_wine_price FROM sonoma_wines
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: rpt_sonoma_wine_prices
    description: "Average prices of wines produced by appellations in Sonoma County"
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per report"
    columns:
      - name: average_sonoma_wine_price
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Find the names of users who did not leave any review.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE review (name VARCHAR, u_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE useracct (name VARCHAR, u_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_review.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT name AS reviewer_name, u_id AS user_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'review') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_useracct.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT name AS user_name, u_id AS user_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'useracct') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: review
      - name: useracct
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_review
    columns:
      - name: reviewer_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: user_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_useracct
    columns:
      - name: user_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: user_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_users_without_reviews.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH user_accounts AS (
  SELECT
    user_name,
    user_id
  FROM {{ ref('stg_useracct') }}
),
reviewers AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT user_id FROM {{ ref('stg_review') }}
)
SELECT
  u.user_name AS name
FROM user_accounts u
WHERE NOT u.user_id IN (SELECT user_id FROM reviewers)
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: rpt_users_without_reviews
    description: "List of users who have not left any review."
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per user without reviews"
    columns:
      - name: name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Find the addresses and author IDs of the course authors that teach at least two courses.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Courses (author_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Course_Authors_and_Tutors (address_line_1 VARCHAR, author_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_courses.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT author_id AS course_author_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Courses') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_course_authors_and_tutors.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT address_line_1, author_id AS course_author_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Course_Authors_and_Tutors') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Courses
      - name: Course_Authors_and_Tutors
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_courses
    columns:
      - name: course_author_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_course_authors_and_tutors
    columns:
      - name: address_line_1
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: course_author_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_multi_course_authors.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    T1.address_line_1,
    T2.course_author_id
  FROM {{ ref('stg_course_authors_and_tutors') }} AS T1
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_courses') }} AS T2 ON T1.course_author_id = T2.course_author_id
)
SELECT
  address_line_1,
  course_author_id AS author_id
FROM base
GROUP BY course_author_id, address_line_1
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_multi_course_authors
    description: "Addresses and IDs of authors teaching at least two courses."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per author teaching >=2 courses"
    columns:
      - name: address_line_1
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: author_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: Find the name of the swimmer who has the most records.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE record (swimmer_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE swimmer (name VARCHAR, id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_swimmer.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT name, id AS swimmer_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'swimmer') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_record.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT swimmer_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'record') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: swimmer
      - name: record
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_swimmer
    columns:
      - name: swimmer_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_record
    columns:
      - name: swimmer_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_swimmer_most_records.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT s.name, COUNT(r.swimmer_id) AS record_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_swimmer') }} s
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_record') }} r ON s.swimmer_id = r.swimmer_id
  GROUP BY s.name, s.swimmer_id
)
SELECT name AS swimmer_name
FROM base
ORDER BY record_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_swimmer_most_records
    description: "Swimmer with the most records."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per swimmer with most records"
    columns:
      - name: swimmer_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_table_name_65.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

  title AS track_title,
  artist AS track_artist,
  year AS release_year

<file path="models/staging/stg_manufacturer.sql">

    manufacturer_id,
    num_of_shops::INTEGER AS num_of_shops

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">

WITH joined_data AS (

Business question: Find the id and city of the student address with the highest average monthly rental.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Student_Addresses (address_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Addresses (city VARCHAR, address_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_student_addresses.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
  address_id AS student_address_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Student_Addresses') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_addresses.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
  city,
  address_id AS address_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Addresses') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Student_Addresses
      - name: Addresses
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_student_addresses
    columns:
      - name: student_address_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_addresses
    columns:
      - name: city
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: address_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_highest_avg_rental.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH combined_data AS (
  SELECT
    s.student_address_id,
    a.city,
    -- Assuming monthly_rental is available in the Addresses table
    -- If not, this would need to be adjusted based on actual schema
    AVG(monthly_rental) AS avg_monthly_rental
  FROM {{ ref('stg_student_addresses') }} s
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_addresses') }} a ON s.student_address_id = a.address_id
  GROUP BY s.student_address_id, a.city
)
SELECT
  student_address_id AS address_id,
  city
FROM combined_data
ORDER BY avg_monthly_rental DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_highest_avg_rental
    description: "Student address with the highest average monthly rental."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per address with highest average rental"
    columns:
      - name: address_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: city
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_savings.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

</file>

  - name: raw

      - name: checking

      - name: checking_balance

      - name: name

      - name: custid

<file path="models/intermediate/int_account_balances.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH accounts AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_accounts') }}),
checking AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_checking') }}),
savings AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_savings') }})
SELECT
  a.custid,
  a.name,
  c.checking_balance,
  s.savings_balance
FROM accounts a
INNER JOIN checking c ON a.custid = c.custid
INNER JOIN savings s ON a.custid = s.custid
WHERE a.name = 'Brown'

<file path="models/staging/stg_participants.sql">

</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_participants_in_events.sql">

  Participant_ID AS participant_id

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_participant_events.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (

    p.participant_id,

<file path="models/staging/stg_documents.sql">

    document_id,
    document_status_code,
    document_type_code,
    shipping_agent_code

{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    document_id,
    document_status_code,
    document_type_code,
    shipping_agent_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Ref_Shipping_Agents') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">

  - name: raw

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_shipped_documents.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH paper_documents AS (
    SELECT document_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_documents') }}
    WHERE document_status_code = 'done'
      AND document_type_code = 'Paper'
),
usps_documents AS (
    SELECT d.document_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_documents') }} d
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_ref_shipping_agents') }} a ON d.shipping_agent_code = a.document_id
    WHERE a.shipping_agent_name = 'USPS'
)
SELECT document_id FROM paper_documents
INTERSECT
SELECT document_id FROM usps_documents
</file>

      grain: "1 row per qualifying document"
    columns:
      - name: document_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_transcript_contents
    columns:
      - name: transcript_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_transcripts
    columns:
      - name: transcript_date
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: transcript_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_min_results_transcript.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        T1.transcript_id,
        T2.transcript_date
    FROM {{ ref('stg_transcript_contents') }} AS T1
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_transcripts') }} AS T2 ON T1.transcript_id = T2.transcript_id
),
aggregated AS (
    SELECT
        transcript_id,
        transcript_date,
        COUNT(*) AS result_count
    FROM base
    GROUP BY transcript_id, transcript_date
)
SELECT
    transcript_date,
    transcript_id
FROM aggregated
ORDER BY result_count ASC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_min_results_transcript
    description: "Transcript with the least number of results."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per transcript with minimum results"
    columns:
      - name: transcript_date
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: transcript_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_professor.sql">

</file>

  - name: raw

      - name: professor

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_smallest_department.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH professor AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_professor') }}),
department AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_department') }})

FROM professor AS T1

ORDER BY COUNT(*) ASC
LIMIT 1

Business question: How many faculty lines are there in "San Francisco State University" in year 2004?

<file path="models/staging/stg_campuses.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_faculty.sql">

WITH base AS (

Business question: Show the name of each county along with the corresponding number of delegates from that county.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE election (District VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE county (County_name VARCHAR, County_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_county.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    County_name AS county_name,
    County_id AS county_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'county') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_election.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

    District AS district
FROM {{ source('raw', 'election') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2

  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: county
      - name: election

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_county
    description: "Cleaned county data with snake_case columns."
  - name: stg_election
    description: "Cleaned election data with snake_case columns."
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_delegates_by_county.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH county_data AS (
    SELECT
        county_name,
        county_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_county') }}
),
election_data AS (
    SELECT
        district
    FROM {{ ref('stg_election') }}
)
SELECT
    c.county_name,
    COUNT(*) AS delegate_count
FROM county_data c
JOIN election_data e ON c.county_id = e.district
GROUP BY c.county_name, c.county_id
</file>

        tests: [not_null, positive_values]
</file>

    facID AS facID,
    actid AS actid
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Faculty_participates_in') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_faculty.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

    lname AS lname,
    facID AS facID

      - name: activity

version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_activity

      - name: actid

  - name: stg_faculty_participates_in

        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_faculty
    columns:
      - name: facID
        tests: [not_null, unique]

<file path="models/intermediate/int_faculty_activities.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH faculty AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_faculty') }}),
participates_in AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_faculty_participates_in') }}),
activity AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_activity') }})
SELECT
    f.facID,
    f.lname,
    a.activity_name
FROM faculty f
INNER JOIN participates_in p ON f.facID = p.facID
INNER JOIN activity a ON p.actid = a.actid
</file>

version: 2
models:

{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('int_faculty_activities') }}),
canoeing_faculty AS (

    FROM base
    WHERE activity_name = 'Canoeing'
),
kayaking_faculty AS (
    SELECT lname
    FROM base
    WHERE activity_name = 'Kayaking'
)
SELECT lname
FROM canoeing_faculty
INTERSECT
SELECT lname
FROM kayaking_faculty
</file>

Business question: How many drivers did not participate in the races held in 2009?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE races (driverId VARCHAR, raceId VARCHAR, YEAR VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE results (driverId VARCHAR, raceId VARCHAR, YEAR VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_races.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT driverId AS race_driver_id, raceId AS race_race_id, YEAR AS race_year FROM {{ source('raw', 'races') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_results.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT driverId AS result_driver_id, raceId AS result_race_id, YEAR AS result_year FROM {{ source('raw', 'results') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: races
      - name: results
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_races
    columns:
      - name: race_driver_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: race_race_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: race_year
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_results
    columns:
      - name: result_driver_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: result_race_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: result_year
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_non_participating_drivers.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH races_2009 AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT race_race_id
  FROM {{ ref('stg_races') }}
  WHERE race_year = '2009'
),
results_with_2009_races AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT result_driver_id
  FROM {{ ref('stg_results') }}
  WHERE result_race_id IN (SELECT race_race_id FROM races_2009)
),
non_participating_drivers AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT result_driver_id
  FROM {{ ref('stg_results') }}
  WHERE result_driver_id NOT IN (SELECT result_driver_id FROM results_with_2009_races)
)
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT result_driver_id) AS non_participating_count
FROM non_participating_drivers
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: rpt_non_participating_drivers
    description: "Count of drivers who did not participate in 2009 races."
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per report"
    columns:
      - name: non_participating_count
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_product_quantity.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        p.product_name,
        oi.product_quantity
    FROM {{ ref('stg_products') }} AS p
    INNER JOIN {{ ref('stg_order_items') }} AS oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id

Business question: Find the first names of students who took exactly one class.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE enroll (stu_num VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE student (stu_fname VARCHAR, stu_num VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_enroll.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT stu_num AS student_number FROM {{ source('raw', 'enroll') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_student.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT stu_fname AS student_first_name, stu_num AS student_number FROM {{ source('raw', 'student') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: enroll
      - name: student
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_enroll
    columns:
      - name: student_number
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_student
    columns:
      - name: student_first_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: student_number
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_students_with_one_class.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH joined_data AS (
  SELECT
    s.student_first_name,
    e.student_number
  FROM {{ ref('stg_student') }} s
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_enroll') }} e ON s.student_number = e.student_number
),
counts AS (
  SELECT
    student_number,
    COUNT(*) AS enrollment_count
  FROM joined_data
  GROUP BY student_number
)
SELECT
  j.student_first_name AS first_name
FROM joined_data j
JOIN counts c ON j.student_number = c.student_number
WHERE c.enrollment_count = 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_students_with_one_class
    description: "First names of students who took exactly one class."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per student with single enrollment"
    columns:
      - name: first_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: Find the id and first name of the student that has the most number of assessment notes?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Students (first_name VARCHAR, student_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Assessment_Notes (student_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_students.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT first_name, student_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Students') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_assessment_notes.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT student_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Assessment_Notes') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2

  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Students
      - name: Assessment_Notes
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_students
    columns:
      - name: first_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: student_id

  - name: stg_assessment_notes
    columns:
      - name: student_id
        tests: [not_null]

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_top_student.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT a.student_id, s.first_name
  FROM {{ ref('stg_assessment_notes') }} a
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_students') }} s ON a.student_id = s.student_id
),
ranked AS (

  FROM base
  GROUP BY student_id, first_name
  ORDER BY note_count DESC
  LIMIT 1
)
SELECT student_id, first_name FROM ranked

Business question: Show the ids and details of the investors who have at least two transactions with type code "SALE".

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE TRANSACTIONS (investor_id VARCHAR, transaction_type_code VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE INVESTORS (Investor_details VARCHAR, investor_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_transactions.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    investor_id,
    transaction_type_code
FROM {{ source('raw', 'TRANSACTIONS') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_investors.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    investor_details AS Investor_details,
    investor_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'INVESTORS') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: TRANSACTIONS
      - name: INVESTORS
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_transactions
    columns:
      - name: investor_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: transaction_type_code
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_investors
    columns:
      - name: Investor_details
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: investor_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_investors_with_multiple_sales.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH sale_transactions AS (
    SELECT
        investor_id,
        COUNT(*) AS transaction_count
    FROM {{ ref('stg_transactions') }}
    WHERE transaction_type_code = 'SALE'
    GROUP BY investor_id
    HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2
),
investor_data AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT
        investor_id,
        Investor_details
    FROM {{ ref('stg_investors') }}
)
SELECT
    i.investor_id,
    i.Investor_details
FROM investor_data i
INNER JOIN sale_transactions s ON i.investor_id = s.investor_id
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_investors_with_multiple_sales
    description: "Investors who have at least two SALE transactions."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per investor with >=2 SALE transactions"
    columns:
      - name: investor_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: Investor_details
        tests: [not_null]
</file>
