Business question: Show the stadium name and capacity with most number of concerts in year 2014 or after.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE stadium (name VARCHAR, capacity VARCHAR, stadium_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE concert (stadium_id VARCHAR, year VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: stadium
      - name: concert
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_stadium
    description: "Staging table for stadium data."
    columns:
      - name: stadium_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: capacity
  - name: stg_concert
    description: "Staging table for concert data."
    columns:
      - name: stadium_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: year
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_stadium.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
  stadium_id,
  name,
  capacity
FROM {{ source('raw', 'stadium') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_concert.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
  stadium_id,
  year
FROM {{ source('raw', 'concert') }}
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_stadium_concerts
    description: "Stadium with the most concerts in 2014 or later."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per stadium with most concerts"
    columns:
      - name: name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: capacity
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_stadium_concerts.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH concert_data AS (
  SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_concert') }} WHERE year >= '2014'
),
joined_data AS (
  SELECT
    s.name,
    s.capacity,
    COUNT(c.stadium_id) AS concert_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_stadium') }} s
  JOIN concert_data c ON s.stadium_id = c.stadium_id
  GROUP BY s.stadium_id, s.name, s.capacity
)
SELECT
  name,
  capacity
FROM joined_data
ORDER BY concert_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

{{ config(materialized='table') }}

Business question: For each product that has problems, find the number of problems reported after 1986-11-13 and the product id?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE product (product_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE problems (product_id VARCHAR, date_problem_reported INTEGER)

<file path="models/staging/stg_product.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT product_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'product') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_problems.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT product_id, date_problem_reported FROM {{ source('raw', 'problems') }}

{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (

Business question: For each station, return its longitude and the average duration of trips that started from the station.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE station (name VARCHAR, long VARCHAR, id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE trip (duration INTEGER, start_station_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_station.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT id AS station_id, name AS station_name, long AS longitude FROM {{ source('raw', 'station') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_trip.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT duration, start_station_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'trip') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: station
      - name: trip
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_station
    columns:
      - name: station_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_trip
    columns:
      - name: duration
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: start_station_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_station_avg_duration.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH station_data AS (
  SELECT station_id, station_name, longitude FROM {{ ref('stg_station') }}
),
trip_data AS (
  SELECT start_station_id, duration FROM {{ ref('stg_trip') }}
)
SELECT
  s.station_name,
  s.longitude,
  AVG(t.duration) AS avg_trip_duration
FROM station_data s
JOIN trip_data t ON s.station_id = t.start_station_id
GROUP BY station_id, station_name, longitude
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_station_avg_duration
    description: "Average trip duration per station"
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per station"
    columns:
      - name: station_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: longitude
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: avg_trip_duration
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Find the start and end dates of detentions of teachers with last name "Schultz".

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Detention (datetime_detention_start VARCHAR, teacher_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Teachers (teacher_id VARCHAR, last_name VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_detention.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    datetime_detention_start AS detention_start,
    teacher_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Detention') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_teachers.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    teacher_id,
    last_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Teachers') }}

  - name: raw

teachers AS (

<file path="models/staging/stg_authorship.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT authid AS authorship_id, paperid AS paper_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'authorship') }}
</file>

{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT paperid AS paper_id, title FROM {{ source('raw', 'papers') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_authors.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT fname AS first_name, lname AS last_name, authid AS authorship_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'authors') }}
</file>

  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: authorship

version: 2

  - name: stg_authorship
    columns:
      - name: authorship_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: paper_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_papers
    columns:
      - name: paper_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: title
  - name: stg_authors
    columns:
      - name: first_name

<file path="models/intermediate/int_paper_authors.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH authorship AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_authorship') }}),
papers AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_papers') }}),
authors AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_authors') }})
SELECT a.first_name, a.last_name, p.title
FROM authorship au
INNER JOIN papers p ON au.paper_id = p.paper_id

Business question: What is the zip code of staff with first name as Janessa and last name as Sawayn lived?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Addresses (zip_postcode VARCHAR, address_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Staff (staff_address_id VARCHAR, first_name VARCHAR, last_name VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_addresses.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    zip_postcode AS address_zip,
    address_id AS address_identifier
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Addresses') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_staff.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    staff_address_id AS staff_address_identifier,
    first_name AS staff_first_name,
    last_name AS staff_last_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Staff') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Addresses
      - name: Staff

version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_addresses
    columns:

  - name: stg_staff
    columns:
      - name: staff_address_identifier
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_staff_zip_codes.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH staff_data AS (
    SELECT
        s.staff_address_identifier,
        s.staff_first_name,
        s.staff_last_name
    FROM {{ ref('stg_staff') }} s
    WHERE s.staff_first_name = 'Janessa'
      AND s.staff_last_name = 'Sawayn'
),
address_data AS (
    SELECT
        a.address_identifier,
        a.address_zip
    FROM {{ ref('stg_addresses') }} a
)
SELECT
    a.address_zip AS staff_zip_code
FROM address_data a
JOIN staff_data s ON a.address_identifier = s.staff_address_identifier
</file>

Business question: Show the names of people that are on affirmative side of debates with number of audience bigger than 200.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE people (Name VARCHAR, People_ID VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE debate (Debate_ID VARCHAR, Num_of_Audience INTEGER); CREATE TABLE debate_people (Debate_ID VARCHAR, Affirmative VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_people.sql">

    Name AS name,
    People_ID AS people_id

</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_debate.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    Debate_ID AS debate_id,
    Num_of_Audience AS num_of_audience
FROM {{ source('raw', 'debate') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_debate_people.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    Debate_ID AS debate_id,
    Affirmative AS affirmative
FROM {{ source('raw', 'debate_people') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: people
      - name: debate
      - name: debate_people
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_people
    columns:
      - name: people_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_debate
    columns:
      - name: debate_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_debate_people
    columns:
      - name: debate_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: affirmative
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_debate_participants.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH debate_people AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_debate_people') }}
),
debates AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_debate') }}
)
SELECT
    dp.debate_id,
    dp.affirmative,
    d.num_of_audience
FROM debate_people dp
INNER JOIN debates d ON dp.debate_id = d.debate_id
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/_intermediate_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: int_debate_participants
    description: "Joined debate participants with audience numbers."
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_affirmative_debaters.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('int_debate_participants') }}
),
filtered AS (
    SELECT
        affirmative,
        num_of_audience
    FROM base
    WHERE num_of_audience > 200
),
people_names AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_people') }}
)
SELECT
    p.name AS name
FROM filtered f
INNER JOIN people_names p ON f.affirmative = p.people_id
GROUP BY p.name
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_affirmative_debaters
    description: "Names of people on the affirmative side of debates with audience > 200."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per affirmative debater"
    columns:
      - name: name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: Find the name and hours of project that has the most number of scientists.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE assignedto (project VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE projects (name VARCHAR, hours VARCHAR, code VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_assignedto.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT project AS assigned_project FROM {{ source('raw', 'assignedto') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_projects.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT name AS project_name, hours AS project_hours, code AS project_code FROM {{ source('raw', 'projects') }}

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">

Business question: Find the name and id of the item with the highest average rating.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE item (title VARCHAR, i_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE review (i_id VARCHAR, rating INTEGER)

<file path="models/staging/stg_item.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    title,
    i_id AS item_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'item') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_review.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

    i_id AS item_id,
    rating
FROM {{ source('raw', 'review') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">

  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: item
      - name: review
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_item
    columns:
      - name: title
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: item_id

  - name: stg_review
    columns:
      - name: item_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: rating
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_top_rated_item.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH joined_data AS (
    SELECT
        i.title,
        i.item_id,
        r.rating
    FROM {{ ref('stg_item') }} AS i
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_review') }} AS r ON i.item_id = r.item_id
)
SELECT
    title,
    item_id
FROM joined_data
GROUP BY item_id, title
ORDER BY AVG(rating) DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

Business question: List the top 10 customers by total gross sales. List customers' first and last name and total gross sales.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE invoices (total INTEGER, customer_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE customers (first_name VARCHAR, last_name VARCHAR, id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_customers.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    id AS customer_id,
    first_name,
    last_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'customers') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_invoices.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    total,
    customer_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'invoices') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: customers
      - name: invoices
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_customers
    columns:
      - name: customer_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_invoices
    columns:
      - name: total
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: customer_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_top_customers_by_sales.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH customer_data AS (
    SELECT
        first_name,
        last_name,
        customer_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_customers') }}
),
invoice_data AS (
    SELECT
        total,
        customer_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_invoices') }}
)
SELECT
    c.first_name,
    c.last_name,
    SUM(i.total) AS total_gross_sales
FROM customer_data c
INNER JOIN invoice_data i ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
GROUP BY c.first_name, c.last_name, c.customer_id
ORDER BY total_gross_sales DESC
LIMIT 10
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_top_customers_by_sales
    description: "Top 10 customers by total gross sales."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per customer"
    columns:
      - name: first_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: last_name

Business question: What is the name of the breed with the most dogs?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Dogs (breed_code VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Breeds (breed_name VARCHAR, breed_code VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_dogs.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT breed_code FROM {{ source('raw', 'Dogs') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_breeds.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">

{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH dogs_per_breed AS (

Business question: What is the name of the customer who has the most policies listed?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE customers (customer_name VARCHAR, customer_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE customers_policies (customer_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_customers.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    customer_name,
    customer_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'customers') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_customers_policies.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    customer_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'customers_policies') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: customers
      - name: customers_policies
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_customers
    columns:
      - name: customer_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: customer_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_customers_policies
    columns:
      - name: customer_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_customer_policy_count.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH customer_policies AS (
    SELECT
        c.customer_name,
        COUNT(p.customer_id) AS policy_count
    FROM {{ ref('stg_customers') }} c
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_customers_policies') }} p ON c.customer_id = p.customer_id
    GROUP BY c.customer_name
)
SELECT
    customer_name AS top_customer_name
FROM customer_policies
ORDER BY policy_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_customer_policy_count
    description: "Identifies the customer with the most policies."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per customer with highest policy count"
    columns:
      - name: top_customer_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: What is the average weeks on top of volumes associated with the artist aged 25 or younger?

<file path="models/staging/stg_volume.sql">

    Artist_ID AS artist_id

    age::INTEGER AS age

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_average_transaction_amount.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH transactions AS (
  SELECT transaction_id FROM {{ ref('stg_transactions') }}
),
transactions_lots AS (
  SELECT lot_id, transaction_id FROM {{ ref('stg_transactions_lots') }}
)
SELECT
  tl.lot_id,
  AVG(t.amount_of_transaction) AS average_amount
FROM transactions_lots tl
JOIN transactions t ON tl.transaction_id = t.transaction_id
GROUP BY tl.lot_id
ORDER BY average_amount DESC
</file>

    description: "Average transaction amount for each lot, ordered by average amount."

      grain: "1 row per lot"
    columns:
      - name: lot_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: average_amount
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Find the names of the regions which were affected by the storm that killed the greatest number of people.

<file path="models/staging/stg_region.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_affected_region.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    region_id,
    storm_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'affected_region') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_storm.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    storm_id,
    Number_Deaths::INTEGER AS number_deaths
FROM {{ source('raw', 'storm') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: region
      - name: affected_region
      - name: storm
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_region
    columns:
      - name: region_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: region_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_affected_region
    columns:
      - name: region_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: storm_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_storm
    columns:
      - name: storm_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: number_deaths
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_storm_impact.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH region_data AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_region') }}
),
affected_data AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_affected_region') }}
),
storm_data AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_storm') }}
)
SELECT
    r.region_name,
    r.region_id,
    a.storm_id,
    s.number_deaths
FROM affected_data a
INNER JOIN region_data r ON a.region_id = r.region_id
INNER JOIN storm_data s ON a.storm_id = s.storm_id
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/_intermediate_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: int_storm_impact
    description: "Joined regions, affected regions, and storm data with death counts."
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_region_storm_impact.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH storm_impacts AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('int_storm_impact') }}
),
ranked_regions AS (
    SELECT
        region_name,
        number_deaths,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY number_deaths DESC) AS death_rank
    FROM storm_impacts
)
SELECT
    region_name,
    number_deaths
FROM ranked_regions
WHERE death_rank = 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_region_storm_impact
    description: "Regions affected by storms with the highest death counts."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per storm-affected region"
    columns:
      - name: region_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: number_deaths
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_compatible_browsers.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH cachebox_browsers AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT T3.name
  FROM {{ ref('stg_web_client_accelerator') }} AS T1
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_accelerator_compatible_browser') }} AS T2 ON T2.accelerator_id = T1.accelerator_id
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_browser') }} AS T3 ON T2.browser_id = T3.browser_id
  WHERE T1.name = 'CACHEbox'
),
fasterfox_browsers AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT T3.name
  FROM {{ ref('stg_web_client_accelerator') }} AS T1
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_accelerator_compatible_browser') }} AS T2 ON T2.accelerator_id = T1.accelerator_id
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_browser') }} AS T3 ON T2.browser_id = T3.browser_id
  WHERE T1.name = 'Fasterfox'
)
SELECT name FROM cachebox_browsers
INTERSECT
SELECT name FROM fasterfox_browsers
</file>

Business question: Find the name and gender of the staff who has been assigned the job of Sales Person but never Clerical Staff.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE staff (staff_name VARCHAR, staff_gender VARCHAR, staff_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Staff_Department_Assignments (staff_id VARCHAR, job_title_code VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_staff.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    staff_name,
    staff_gender,
    staff_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'staff') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_staff_department_assignments.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    staff_id,
    job_title_code
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Staff_Department_Assignments') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: staff
      - name: Staff_Department_Assignments
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_staff
    description: "Cleaned staff data."
    columns:
      - name: staff_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: staff_gender
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: staff_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_staff_department_assignments
    description: "Cleaned staff department assignments."
    columns:
      - name: staff_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: job_title_code
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_staff_jobs.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH staff_data AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_staff') }}
),
assignment_data AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_staff_department_assignments') }}
)
SELECT
    s.staff_name,
    s.staff_gender,
    a.job_title_code
FROM staff_data s
INNER JOIN assignment_data a ON s.staff_id = a.staff_id
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/_intermediate_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: int_staff_jobs
    description: "Joined staff data with their job assignments."
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_sales_person_not_clerical.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH sales_people AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT staff_name, staff_gender
    FROM {{ ref('int_staff_jobs') }}
    WHERE job_title_code = 'Sales Person'
),
clerical_people AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT staff_name, staff_gender
    FROM {{ ref('int_staff_jobs') }}
    WHERE job_title_code = 'Clerical Staff'
)
SELECT
    s.staff_name,
    s.staff_gender
FROM sales_people s
LEFT JOIN clerical_people c ON s.staff_name = c.staff_name AND s.staff_gender = c.staff_gender
WHERE c.staff_name IS NULL
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_sales_person_not_clerical
    description: "Staff members who are Sales Persons but never Clerical Staff."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per staff member"
    columns:
      - name: staff_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: staff_gender
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: For each user, return the name and the average rating of reviews given by them.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE review (rating INTEGER, u_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE useracct (name VARCHAR, u_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_review.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

</file>

FROM base
GROUP BY user_id, name

Business question: Show the crime rate of counties with a city having white percentage more than 90.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE county_public_safety (Crime_rate VARCHAR, County_ID VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE city (County_ID VARCHAR, White INTEGER)

<file path="models/staging/stg_county_public_safety.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

    Crime_rate AS crime_rate,
    County_ID AS county_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'county_public_safety') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_city.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    County_ID AS county_id,
    White AS white_percentage
FROM {{ source('raw', 'city') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: county_public_safety
      - name: city

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_county_public_safety
    columns:
      - name: crime_rate
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: county_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_city
    columns:
      - name: county_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: white_percentage

</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_high_white_crime_rate.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        c.crime_rate,

FROM base
WHERE white_percentage > 90

    FROM {{ ref('stg_dogs') }} AS d
)

Business question: Find the name and id of the good with the highest average rank.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE review (i_id VARCHAR, rank INTEGER); CREATE TABLE item (title VARCHAR, i_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_review.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    i_id AS review_item_id,
    rank AS review_rank
FROM {{ source('raw', 'review') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_item.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    i_id AS item_id,
    title AS item_title
FROM {{ source('raw', 'item') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: review
      - name: item
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_review
    columns:
      - name: review_item_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_item
    columns:
      - name: item_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_highest_avg_rank.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH ranked_items AS (
    SELECT
        i.item_title,
        i.item_id,
        AVG(r.review_rank) AS avg_rank
    FROM {{ ref('stg_item') }} i
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_review') }} r ON i.item_id = r.review_item_id
    GROUP BY i.item_id, i.item_title
)
SELECT
    item_title,
    item_id
FROM ranked_items

Business question: Show aircraft names and number of flights for each aircraft.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Aircraft (name VARCHAR, aid VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Flight (aid VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_aircraft.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT name, aid AS aircraft_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Aircraft') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_flight.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT aid AS aircraft_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Flight') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Aircraft
      - name: Flight

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_aircraft
    columns:
      - name: aircraft_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_flight
    columns:
      - name: aircraft_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_flights_by_aircraft.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH aircraft AS (
  SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_aircraft') }}
),
flights AS (
  SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_flight') }}
)
SELECT
  a.name AS aircraft_name,
  COUNT(f.aircraft_id) AS number_of_flights
FROM aircraft a
JOIN flights f ON a.aircraft_id = f.aircraft_id
GROUP BY a.aircraft_id, a.name
</file>

        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: number_of_flights
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Find the name of the program that is broadcast most frequently.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE program (name VARCHAR, program_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE broadcast (program_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_program.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT name, program_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'program') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_broadcast.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT program_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'broadcast') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: program
      - name: broadcast
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_program
    columns:
      - name: program_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_broadcast
    columns:
      - name: program_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_most_frequent_program.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT p.name, COUNT(b.*) AS broadcast_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_program') }} p
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_broadcast') }} b ON p.program_id = b.program_id
  GROUP BY p.name, p.program_id
)

ORDER BY broadcast_count DESC
LIMIT 1

    columns:
      - name: name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: Show the denomination of the school that has the most players.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE player (School_ID VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE school (Denomination VARCHAR, School_ID VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_player.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT School_ID AS school_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'player') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_school.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT Denomination AS denomination, School_ID AS school_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'school') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: player
      - name: school
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_player
    columns:
      - name: school_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_school
    columns:
      - name: denomination
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: school_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_most_players_school.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT s.denomination, COUNT(*) AS player_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_player') }} p
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_school') }} s ON p.school_id = s.school_id
  GROUP BY s.denomination, s.school_id
),
ranked AS (
  SELECT denomination, player_count,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY player_count DESC) AS rank
  FROM base
)
SELECT denomination AS most_players_school_denomination
FROM ranked
WHERE rank = 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_most_players_school
    description: "Denomination of the school with the most players."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per school with most players"
    columns:
      - name: most_players_school_denomination
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: Find the name of the source user with the highest average trust score.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE useracct (name VARCHAR, u_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE trust (source_u_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: useracct
      - name: trust
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_useracct
    description: "Staging model for user accounts."
  - name: stg_trust
    description: "Staging model for trust scores."
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_useracct.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    name,
    u_id AS user_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'useracct') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_trust.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    source_u_id AS user_id,
    trust AS trust_score
FROM {{ source('raw', 'trust') }}
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_highest_avg_trust_user.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH user_trust_scores AS (
    SELECT
        u.user_id,
        u.name,
        t.trust_score
    FROM {{ ref('stg_useracct') }} u
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_trust') }} t ON u.user_id = t.user_id
),
average_trust AS (
    SELECT
        user_id,
        name,
        AVG(trust_score) AS avg_trust
    FROM user_trust_scores
    GROUP BY user_id, name
)
SELECT
    name AS source_user_name
FROM average_trust
ORDER BY avg_trust DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_highest_avg_trust_user
    description: "Highest average trust score per user."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per source user with highest average trust score"
    columns:
      - name: source_user_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Which city has the most addresses? List the city name, number of addresses, and city id.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE address (city_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE city (city VARCHAR, city_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_address.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT city_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'address') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_city.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT city_id, city FROM {{ source('raw', 'city') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: address
      - name: city
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_address
    columns:
      - name: city_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_city
    columns:
      - name: city_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: city
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_city_address_count.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    T1.city_id,
    T2.city
  FROM {{ ref('stg_address') }} AS T1
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_city') }} AS T2 ON T1.city_id = T2.city_id
),
aggregated AS (
  SELECT
    city,
    COUNT(*) AS address_count,
    T1.city_id
  FROM base
  GROUP BY city, T1.city_id
)
SELECT
  city,
  address_count,
  city_id
FROM aggregated
ORDER BY address_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:

Business question: Find the description of the most popular role among the users that have logged in.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE users (role_description VARCHAR, role_code VARCHAR, user_login VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE ROLES (role_description VARCHAR, role_code VARCHAR, user_login VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_users.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    role_description AS user_role_description,
    role_code AS user_role_code,
    user_login
FROM {{ source('raw', 'users') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_roles.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    role_description AS role_role_description,
    role_code AS role_role_code,
    user_login
FROM {{ source('raw', 'ROLES') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: users
      - name: ROLES
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_users
    columns:
      - name: user_role_description
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: user_role_code
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: user_login
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_roles
    columns:
      - name: role_role_description
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: role_role_code
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: user_login
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_popular_role.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH most_popular_role AS (
    SELECT
        user_role_code,
        COUNT(*) AS login_count
    FROM {{ ref('stg_users') }}
    WHERE user_login = 1
    GROUP BY user_role_code
    ORDER BY login_count DESC
    LIMIT 1
)
SELECT
    r.role_role_description AS popular_role_description
FROM {{ ref('stg_roles') }} r
JOIN most_popular_role mpr ON r.role_role_code = mpr.user_role_code
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_popular_role
    description: "Description of the most popular role among users that have logged in."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per most popular role"
    columns:
      - name: popular_role_description
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_aircraft_wins.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        a.aircraft_name,

    FROM {{ ref('stg_aircraft') }} AS a
    INNER JOIN {{ ref('stg_match') }} AS m ON a.aircraft_id = m.winning_aircraft_id
)

FROM base

Business question: What is the total number of unique official languages spoken in the countries that are founded before 1930?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE country (Code VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE countrylanguage (Language VARCHAR, CountryCode VARCHAR, IsOfficial VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_country.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    Code AS country_code,
    IndepYear AS independence_year
FROM {{ source('raw', 'country') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_countrylanguage.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    Language AS language,
    CountryCode AS country_code,
    IsOfficial AS is_official
FROM {{ source('raw', 'countrylanguage') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw

      - name: country

version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_country
    columns:
      - name: country_code

  - name: stg_countrylanguage

</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_unique_official_languages.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (

WITH office_building_ids AS (

Business question: What is the average age of the members of the club "Bootup Baltimore"?

<file path="models/staging/stg_club.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT clubid, clubname FROM {{ source('raw', 'club') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_student.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT age, stuid FROM {{ source('raw', 'student') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_member_of_club.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT clubid, stuid FROM {{ source('raw', 'member_of_club') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: club
      - name: student
      - name: member_of_club
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_club
    description: "Club information"
    columns:
      - name: clubid
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: clubname
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_student
    description: "Student information"
    columns:
      - name: age
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: stuid
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_member_of_club
    description: "Membership information"
    columns:
      - name: clubid
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: stuid
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_club_members.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH club_data AS (
  SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_club') }}
),
membership_data AS (
  SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_member_of_club') }}
),
student_data AS (
  SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_student') }}
)
SELECT
  c.clubid,
  c.clubname,
  s.age,
  m.stuid
FROM club_data c
INNER JOIN membership_data m ON c.clubid = m.clubid
INNER JOIN student_data s ON m.stuid = s.stuid
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/_intermediate_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: int_club_members
    description: "Combined club, membership, and student data"
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_bootup_baltimore_avg_age.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base_data AS (
  SELECT * FROM {{ ref('int_club_members') }}
)
SELECT AVG(age) AS average_age
FROM base_data
WHERE clubname = 'Bootup Baltimore'
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_bootup_baltimore_avg_age
    description: "Average age of members in Bootup Baltimore club"
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per calculation"
    columns:
      - name: average_age
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: How many problems does the product with the most problems have? List the number of the problems and product name.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE problems (product_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE product (product_name VARCHAR, product_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_problems.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT product_id AS problem_product_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'problems') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_product.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT product_name, product_id AS product_product_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'product') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: problems
      - name: product
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_problems
    columns:
      - name: problem_product_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_product
    columns:
      - name: product_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: product_product_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_problems_by_product.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    p.product_name,
    COUNT(*) AS problem_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_product') }} p
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_problems') }} pr ON p.product_product_id = pr.problem_product_id
  GROUP BY p.product_name
)
SELECT
  product_name,
  problem_count
FROM base
ORDER BY problem_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_problems_by_product
    description: "Count of problems per product with the product having the most problems listed first."

      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per product"
    columns:
      - name: product_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: problem_count
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: What are the dates for the documents with both 'GV' type and 'SF' type expenses?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Documents_with_Expenses (document_id VARCHAR, budget_type_code VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Documents (document_date VARCHAR, document_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_documents_with_expenses.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT document_id, budget_type_code FROM {{ source('raw', 'Documents_with_Expenses') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_documents.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT document_date, document_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Documents') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Documents_with_Expenses
      - name: Documents
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_documents_with_expenses
    columns:
      - name: document_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: budget_type_code
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_documents
    columns:
      - name: document_date
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: document_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_documents_with_expenses.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH documents AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_documents') }}),
expenses AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_documents_with_expenses') }})
SELECT d.document_date, e.budget_type_code
FROM documents d
INNER JOIN expenses e ON d.document_id = e.document_id
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/_intermediate_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: int_documents_with_expenses
    description: "Joined documents and expenses with budget types."
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_documents_with_gv_sf_dates.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('int_documents_with_expenses') }}),
gv_dates AS (
  SELECT document_date
  FROM base
  WHERE budget_type_code = 'GV'
),
sf_dates AS (
  SELECT document_date
  FROM base
  WHERE budget_type_code = 'SF'
)
SELECT gv.document_date AS gv_document_date
FROM gv_dates gv
INNER JOIN sf_dates sf ON gv.document_date = sf.document_date
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_documents_with_gv_sf_dates
    description: "Dates of documents with both GV and SF type expenses."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per document date with both GV and SF expenses"
    columns:
      - name: gv_document_date
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Which airline has most number of flights?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE FLIGHTS (Airline VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE AIRLINES (Airline VARCHAR, uid VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_flights.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT Airline FROM {{ source('raw', 'FLIGHTS') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_airlines.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT Airline AS airline_name, uid FROM {{ source('raw', 'AIRLINES') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: FLIGHTS
      - name: AIRLINES
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_flights
    description: "Staging model for flights data"
  - name: stg_airlines
    description: "Staging model for airlines data"
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_airline_with_most_flights.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH airline_flights AS (
  SELECT
    a.airline_name,
    COUNT(f.Airline) AS flight_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_airlines') }} a
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_flights') }} f ON a.uid = f.Airline
  GROUP BY a.airline_name
)
SELECT airline_name FROM airline_flights ORDER BY flight_count DESC LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_airline_with_most_flights
    description: "Identifies the airline with the highest number of flights"
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per airline with most flights"
    columns:
      - name: airline_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_furniture.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

    Market_Rate AS market_rate,
    name AS furniture_name,
    Furniture_ID AS furniture_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'furniture') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_furniture_manufacte.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    Market_Rate AS market_rate,
    name AS furniture_name,
    Furniture_ID AS furniture_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'furniture_manufacte') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2

  - name: raw

      - name: furniture

    columns:

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_unproduced_furniture.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH furniture AS (
    SELECT
        market_rate,
        furniture_name
    FROM {{ ref('stg_furniture') }}
),
manufactured_ids AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT furniture_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_furniture_manufacte') }}
)
SELECT
    f.market_rate,
    f.furniture_name
FROM furniture f
WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM manufactured_ids m WHERE m.furniture_id = f.furniture_id
)
</file>

Business question: What is the maximum number that a certain service is provided? List the service id, details and number.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Services (service_id VARCHAR, service_details VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Residents_Services (service_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_services.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT service_id, service_details FROM {{ source('raw', 'Services') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_residents_services.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

</file>

  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Services
      - name: Residents_Services
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_services
    columns:
      - name: service_id

  - name: stg_residents_services

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_service_usage.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    s.service_id,
    s.service_details,
    COUNT(*) AS service_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_services') }} s
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_residents_services') }} rs ON s.service_id = rs.service_id
  GROUP BY s.service_id, s.service_details
)
SELECT
  service_id,
  service_details,
  service_count
FROM base
ORDER BY service_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: service_details
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: service_count
        tests: [not_null, greater_than_zero]

Business question: Which department has more than 1 head at a time? List the id, name and the number of heads.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE management (department_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE department (department_id VARCHAR, name VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_management.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT department_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'management') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_department.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT department_id, name FROM {{ source('raw', 'department') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: management
      - name: department
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_management
    columns:
      - name: department_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_department
    columns:
      - name: department_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_departments_with_multiple_heads.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    d.department_id,
    d.name
  FROM {{ ref('stg_department') }} AS d
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_management') }} AS m ON d.department_id = m.department_id
),
aggregated AS (
  SELECT
    department_id,
    name,
    COUNT(*) AS head_count
  FROM base
  GROUP BY department_id, name
)
SELECT
  department_id,
  name,
  head_count
FROM aggregated
WHERE head_count > 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_departments_with_multiple_heads
    description: "Departments with more than one head at a time."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per department with multiple heads"
    columns:
      - name: department_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: head_count
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_customer_food_purchases.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH customers AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_customers') }}),
orders AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_orders') }}),
order_items AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_order_items') }}),
products AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_products') }})
SELECT DISTINCT c.customer_id, c.customer_name
FROM customers c
INNER JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
INNER JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id
INNER JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id AND p.product_name = 'food'
</file>

SELECT customer_name
FROM base
GROUP BY customer_id, customer_name
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 1
</file>

        tests: [not_null]

Business question: Which department offers the most number of degrees? List department name and id.

<file path="models/staging/stg_degree_programs.sql">

</file>

Business question: Tell me constructor for Laps less than 17 and Grid more than 11 for alan jones

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE table_name_52 (constructor VARCHAR, driver VARCHAR, laps VARCHAR, grid VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_table_name_52.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    constructor AS constructor,
    driver AS driver,
    laps AS laps,
    grid AS grid
FROM {{ source('raw', 'table_name_52') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: table_name_52
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_table_name_52
    description: "Staging model for table_name_52 containing constructor, driver, laps, and grid information."
    columns:
      - name: constructor
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: driver
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: laps
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: grid
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/dim_constructors.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        constructor,
        driver,
        laps,
        grid
    FROM {{ ref('stg_table_name_52') }}
)
SELECT

FROM base
WHERE laps < 17

    columns:
      - name: constructor
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_lessons.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT vehicle_id AS lesson_vehicle_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Lessons') }}
</file>

{{ config(materialized='view') }}

Business question: What are the first and last names of the instructors who teach the top 3 number of courses?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE COURSE (Instructor VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE FACULTY (Fname VARCHAR, Lname VARCHAR, FacID VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_course.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    Instructor AS instructor_id,
    Instructor AS raw_instructor_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'COURSE') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_faculty.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    Fname AS first_name,
    Lname AS last_name,
    FacID AS faculty_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'FACULTY') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: COURSE
      - name: FACULTY
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_course
    columns:
      - name: instructor_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_faculty
    columns:
      - name: faculty_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_top_instructors.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH instructor_courses AS (
    SELECT
        c.instructor_id,
        COUNT(*) AS course_count
    FROM {{ ref('stg_course') }} c
    GROUP BY c.instructor_id
),
ranked_instructors AS (
    SELECT
        f.faculty_id,
        f.first_name,
        f.last_name,
        ic.course_count,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY ic.course_count DESC) AS instructor_rank
    FROM {{ ref('stg_faculty') }} f
    JOIN instructor_courses ic ON f.faculty_id = ic.instructor_id
)
SELECT
    first_name,
    last_name
FROM ranked_instructors
WHERE instructor_rank <= 3
ORDER BY course_count DESC
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_top_instructors
    description: "Top 3 instructors by number of courses taught"
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per instructor"
    columns:
      - name: first_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: last_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Find the number of rooms for different block code?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE room (blockfloor VARCHAR, blockcode VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE BLOCK (blockcode VARCHAR, blockfloor VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_block.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT blockcode, blockfloor FROM {{ source('raw', 'BLOCK') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_room.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT blockfloor, blockcode FROM {{ source('raw', 'room') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: BLOCK
      - name: room
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_block
    columns:
      - name: blockcode
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: blockfloor
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_room
    columns:
      - name: blockfloor
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: blockcode
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_room_count_by_block.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    b.blockcode,
    COUNT(*) AS room_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_block') }} b
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_room') }} r ON b.blockfloor = r.blockfloor AND b.blockcode = r.blockcode
  GROUP BY b.blockcode
)
SELECT blockcode, room_count FROM base
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_room_count_by_block
    description: "Number of rooms grouped by block code."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per block code"
    columns:
      - name: blockcode
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: room_count
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Find the name of route that has the highest number of deliveries.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Delivery_Routes (route_name VARCHAR, route_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Delivery_Route_Locations (route_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_delivery_routes.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

    route_name,
    route_id

<file path="models/staging/stg_delivery_route_locations.sql">

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">

  - name: raw

      - name: Delivery_Routes
      - name: Delivery_Route_Locations
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_delivery_routes
    columns:
      - name: route_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: route_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_delivery_route_locations
    columns:
      - name: route_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_route_deliveries.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        t1.route_name,
        COUNT(*) AS delivery_count
    FROM {{ ref('stg_delivery_routes') }} t1
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_delivery_route_locations') }} t2 ON t1.route_id = t2.route_id
    GROUP BY t1.route_name, t1.route_id
)
SELECT
    route_name,
    delivery_count
FROM base
ORDER BY delivery_count DESC
LIMIT 1

Business question: Find the names of all distinct wines that have appellations in North Coast area.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE APPELLATIONs (Appelation VARCHAR, Area VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE WINE (Name VARCHAR, Appelation VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_appellations.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT Appelation AS appellation, Area AS area FROM {{ source('raw', 'APPELLATIONs') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_wine.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT Name AS name, Appelation AS appellation FROM {{ source('raw', 'WINE') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: APPELLATIONs
      - name: WINE
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_appellations
    columns:
      - name: appellation
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: area
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_wine
    columns:
      - name: name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: appellation
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_north_coast_wines.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH north_coast_appellations AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT appellation
  FROM {{ ref('stg_appellations') }}
  WHERE area = 'North Coast'
),
wines_with_north_coast_appellations AS (
  SELECT name
  FROM {{ ref('stg_wine') }}
  WHERE appellation IN (SELECT appellation FROM north_coast_appellations)
)
SELECT DISTINCT name AS wine_name
FROM wines_with_north_coast_appellations
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: rpt_north_coast_wines
    description: "List of distinct wines from North Coast appellation area."
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per distinct wine"
    columns:
      - name: wine_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: Among those engineers who have visited, which engineer makes the least number of visits? List the engineer id, first name and last name.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Engineer_Visits (engineer_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Maintenance_Engineers (engineer_id VARCHAR, first_name VARCHAR, last_name VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_engineer_visits.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    engineer_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Engineer_Visits') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_maintenance_engineers.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    engineer_id,
    first_name,
    last_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Maintenance_Engineers') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Engineer_Visits
      - name: Maintenance_Engineers
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_engineer_visits
    columns:
      - name: engineer_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_maintenance_engineers
    columns:
      - name: engineer_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: first_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: last_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_least_visits_engineer.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH engineer_visits AS (
    SELECT
        e.engineer_id,
        e.first_name,
        e.last_name,
        COUNT(v.engineer_id) AS visit_count
    FROM {{ ref('stg_maintenance_engineers') }} e
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_engineer_visits') }} v ON e.engineer_id = v.engineer_id
    GROUP BY e.engineer_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
)
SELECT
    engineer_id,
    first_name,
    last_name
FROM engineer_visits
ORDER BY visit_count ASC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_least_visits_engineer
    description: "Engineer with the least number of visits"

      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per engineer with least visits"
    columns:
      - name: engineer_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: first_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: last_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: What is the storm name and max speed which affected the greatest number of regions?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE storm (name VARCHAR, max_speed VARCHAR, storm_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE affected_region (storm_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_storm.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT storm_id, name, max_speed FROM {{ source('raw', 'storm') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_affected_region.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT storm_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'affected_region') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: storm
      - name: affected_region
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_storm
    columns:
      - name: storm_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_affected_region
    columns:
      - name: storm_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_storm_by_affected_regions.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    s.name,
    s.max_speed,
    COUNT(a.storm_id) AS affected_region_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_storm') }} s
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_affected_region') }} a ON s.storm_id = a.storm_id
  GROUP BY s.name, s.max_speed
)
SELECT
  name,
  max_speed,
  affected_region_count
FROM base
ORDER BY affected_region_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">

models:
  - name: fct_storm_by_affected_regions
    description: "Storm with the greatest number of affected regions and its max speed."

      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per storm with max affected regions"
    columns:
      - name: name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: max_speed
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: affected_region_count
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_top_source_airport.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH airport_routes AS (
  SELECT
    a.airport_name,
    a.airport_city,
    r.source_airport_id
  FROM {{ ref('stg_airports') }} a
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_routes') }} r ON a.airport_id = r.source_airport_id
)
SELECT
  airport_name,
  airport_city
FROM airport_routes
GROUP BY source_airport_id, airport_name, airport_city
ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

Business question: How many engineer visits are required at most for a single fault log? List the number and the log entry id.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Fault_Log (fault_log_entry_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Engineer_Visits (fault_log_entry_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_fault_log.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT fault_log_entry_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Fault_Log') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_engineer_visits.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT fault_log_entry_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Engineer_Visits') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Fault_Log
      - name: Engineer_Visits
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_fault_log
    columns:
      - name: fault_log_entry_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_engineer_visits
    columns:
      - name: fault_log_entry_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_max_visits_per_log.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    T1.fault_log_entry_id,
    COUNT(*) AS visit_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_fault_log') }} AS T1
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_engineer_visits') }} AS T2
    ON T1.fault_log_entry_id = T2.fault_log_entry_id
  GROUP BY T1.fault_log_entry_id
)
SELECT visit_count, fault_log_entry_id FROM base ORDER BY visit_count DESC LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_max_visits_per_log
    description: "Identifies the fault log requiring the most engineer visits."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per fault log entry"
    columns:
      - name: visit_count
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: fault_log_entry_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: What is the average longitude of stations that never had bike availability more than 10?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE station (long INTEGER, id VARCHAR, station_id VARCHAR, bikes_available INTEGER); CREATE TABLE status (long INTEGER, id VARCHAR, station_id VARCHAR, bikes_available INTEGER)

<file path="models/staging/stg_station.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    long AS longitude,
    id AS station_id,
    bikes_available AS bikes_available
FROM {{ source('raw', 'station') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_status.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    long AS longitude,
    id AS status_id,
    station_id AS station_id,
    bikes_available AS bikes_available
FROM {{ source('raw', 'status') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: station
      - name: status
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_station
    description: "Cleaned station data."
    columns:
      - name: longitude
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: station_id
        tests: [unique, not_null]
  - name: stg_status
    description: "Cleaned station status data."
    columns:
      - name: status_id
        tests: [unique, not_null]
      - name: station_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_avg_longitude.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        station_id,
        MAX(bikes_available) AS max_bikes
    FROM {{ ref('stg_status') }}
    GROUP BY station_id
)
SELECT AVG(longitude) AS avg_longitude
FROM {{ ref('stg_station') }}
WHERE station_id NOT IN (SELECT station_id FROM base WHERE max_bikes > 10)
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_avg_longitude
    description: "Average longitude of stations that never had bike availability more than 10."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per calculation"
</file>

    columns:
      - name: physician

<file path="models/staging/stg_department.sql">

    name,

{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH physician_data AS (

affiliation_data AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_affiliated_with') }}
),
department_data AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_department') }}
)
SELECT
    p.employee_id,
    p.name AS physician_name,
    a.department AS department_id,
    d.name AS department_name
FROM physician_data p
INNER JOIN affiliation_data a ON p.employee_id = a.physician
INNER JOIN department_data d ON a.department = d.department_id
WHERE a.primary_affiliation = 1

{{ config(materialized='table') }}

    department_name
FROM base

Business question: Give me the maximum low temperature and average precipitation at the Amersham station.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE weekly_weather (low_temperature INTEGER, precipitation INTEGER, station_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE station (id VARCHAR, network_name VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_weekly_weather.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    low_temperature,
    precipitation,
    station_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'weekly_weather') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_station.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    id AS station_id,
    network_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'station') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: weekly_weather
      - name: station
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_weekly_weather
    columns:
      - name: low_temperature
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: precipitation
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: station_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_station
    columns:
      - name: station_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: network_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_amersham_weather_stats.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        w.low_temperature,
        w.precipitation
    FROM {{ ref('stg_weekly_weather') }} AS w
    INNER JOIN {{ ref('stg_station') }} AS s
        ON w.station_id = s.station_id
    WHERE s.network_name = 'Amersham'
)
SELECT
    MAX(low_temperature) AS max_low_temperature,
    AVG(precipitation) AS avg_precipitation
FROM base
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: rpt_amersham_weather_stats
    description: "Maximum low temperature and average precipitation at the Amersham station."
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per weather metric"
    columns:
      - name: max_low_temperature
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: avg_precipitation
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: What is the primary conference of the school that has the lowest acc percent score in the competition?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE basketball_match (school_id VARCHAR, acc_percent VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE university (Primary_conference VARCHAR, school_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_basketball_match.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    school_id,
    acc_percent
FROM {{ source('raw', 'basketball_match') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_university.sql">

    Primary_conference,
    school_id

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_basketball_match
    columns:
      - name: school_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: acc_percent
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_university
    columns:
      - name: Primary_conference
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: school_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_lowest_acc_conference.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        u.Primary_conference,

    FROM {{ ref('stg_university') }} u

        ON u.school_id = bm.school_id

FROM base
ORDER BY acc_percent ASC
LIMIT 1
</file>

      - name: primary_conference_of_lowest_acc_school
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: What is the name of department where has the largest number of professors with a Ph.D. degree?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE professor (dept_code VARCHAR, prof_high_degree VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE department (dept_name VARCHAR, dept_code VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_professor.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    dept_code,
    prof_high_degree AS prof_highest_degree
FROM {{ source('raw', 'professor') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_department.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    dept_name,
    dept_code
FROM {{ source('raw', 'department') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: professor
      - name: department
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_professor
    columns:
      - name: dept_code
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: prof_highest_degree
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_department
    columns:
      - name: dept_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: dept_code
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_department_phd_count.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        d.dept_name,
        d.dept_code,
        p.prof_highest_degree
    FROM {{ ref('stg_professor') }} p
    INNER JOIN {{ ref('stg_department') }} d ON p.dept_code = d.dept_code
    WHERE p.prof_highest_degree = 'Ph.D.'
),
aggregated AS (
    SELECT
        dept_name,
        dept_code,
        COUNT(*) AS phd_count
    FROM base
    GROUP BY dept_name, dept_code
)
SELECT
    dept_name,
    dept_code,
    phd_count
FROM aggregated
ORDER BY phd_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_department_phd_count
    description: "Department with the largest number of professors holding a Ph.D. degree."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per department"
    columns:
      - name: dept_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: dept_code
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: phd_count
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Show the description of the transaction type that occurs most frequently.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE TRANSACTIONS (transaction_type_code VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Ref_Transaction_Types (transaction_type_description VARCHAR, transaction_type_code VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_transactions.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    transaction_type_code AS transaction_type_code
FROM {{ source('raw', 'TRANSACTIONS') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_ref_transaction_types.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    transaction_type_description AS transaction_type_description,
    transaction_type_code AS transaction_type_code
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Ref_Transaction_Types') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: TRANSACTIONS
      - name: Ref_Transaction_Types
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_transactions
    columns:
      - name: transaction_type_code
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_ref_transaction_types
    columns:
      - name: transaction_type_description
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: transaction_type_code
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_most_frequent_transaction.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH transactions AS (
    SELECT
        transaction_type_code
    FROM {{ ref('stg_transactions') }}

),
transaction_types AS (
    SELECT
        transaction_type_description,
        transaction_type_code
    FROM {{ ref('stg_ref_transaction_types') }}

),
joined_data AS (
    SELECT
        t.transaction_type_description,
        COUNT(t.transaction_type_code) AS transaction_count
    FROM transactions s
    JOIN transaction_types t
        ON s.transaction_type_code = t.transaction_type_code
    GROUP BY t.transaction_type_description, t.transaction_type_code

)
SELECT
    transaction_type_description,
    transaction_count
FROM joined_data
ORDER BY transaction_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_most_frequent_transaction
    description: "The most frequently occurring transaction type and its count."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per transaction type"
    columns:
      - name: transaction_type_description
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: transaction_count
        tests: [not_null]
</file>
