Business question: What are the ids, names and FDA approval status of medicines in descending order of the number of enzymes that it can interact with.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE medicine_enzyme_interaction (medicine_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE medicine (id VARCHAR, Name VARCHAR, FDA_approved VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_medicine.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT id, name AS medicine_name, fda_approved FROM {{ source('raw', 'medicine') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_medicine_enzyme_interaction.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT medicine_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'medicine_enzyme_interaction') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: medicine
      - name: medicine_enzyme_interaction
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_medicine
    columns:
      - name: id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: medicine_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: fda_approved
  - name: stg_medicine_enzyme_interaction
    columns:
      - name: medicine_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_medicine_enzyme_interactions.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH medicine_data AS (
  SELECT id, medicine_name, fda_approved
  FROM {{ ref('stg_medicine') }}
),
interaction_counts AS (
  SELECT
    medicine_id,
    COUNT(*) AS interaction_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_medicine_enzyme_interaction') }}
  GROUP BY medicine_id
)
SELECT
  m.id,
  m.medicine_name,
  m.fda_approved,
  i.interaction_count
FROM medicine_data m
JOIN interaction_counts i ON m.id = i.medicine_id
ORDER BY i.interaction_count DESC
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_medicine_enzyme_interactions
    description: "Medicines with their FDA approval status and number of enzyme interactions, ordered by interaction count."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per medicine"
    columns:
      - name: id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: medicine_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: fda_approved
      - name: interaction_count
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: What kind of decor has the least number of reservations?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Reservations (Room VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Rooms (decor VARCHAR, RoomId VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_reservations.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT Room AS reservation_room FROM {{ source('raw', 'Reservations') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_rooms.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT decor, RoomId AS room_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Rooms') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2

  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Reservations
      - name: Rooms
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_reservations
    columns:
      - name: reservation_room
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_rooms
    columns:
      - name: decor
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: room_id
        tests: [unique, not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_decor_reservations.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH reservations AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_reservations') }}),
rooms AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_rooms') }})
SELECT r.decor, COUNT(r.room_id) AS reservation_count
FROM rooms r
JOIN reservations res ON r.room_id = res.reservation_room
GROUP BY r.decor
ORDER BY reservation_count ASC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">

models:

      - name: decor
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: reservation_count
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE dorm_amenity (amenid VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE lives_in (stuid VARCHAR, dormid VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE dorm (dormid VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE student (fname VARCHAR, stuid VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE has_amenity (dormid VARCHAR, amenid VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_dorm_amenity.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT amenid AS amenid FROM {{ source('raw', 'dorm_amenity') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_lives_in.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT stuid AS student_id, dormid AS dorm_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'lives_in') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_dorm.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT dormid AS dorm_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'dorm') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_student.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT fname AS first_name, stuid AS student_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'student') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_has_amenity.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT dormid AS dorm_id, amenid AS amen_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'has_amenity') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: dorm_amenity
      - name: lives_in
      - name: dorm
      - name: student
      - name: has_amenity
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_dorm_amenity
    columns:
      - name: amenid
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_lives_in
    columns:
      - name: student_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: dorm_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_dorm
    columns:
      - name: dorm_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_student
    columns:
      - name: first_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: student_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_has_amenity
    columns:
      - name: dorm_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: amen_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_dorm_amenities.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH dorms AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_dorm') }}),
amenities AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_has_amenity') }})
SELECT d.dorm_id, a.amen_id
FROM dorms d
INNER JOIN amenities a ON d.dorm_id = a.dorm_id
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/_intermediate_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: int_dorm_amenities
    description: "Joined dorms and their amenities."
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_dorm_amenities.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('int_dorm_amenities') }}),
dorm_with_amenity_count AS (
  SELECT dorm_id, COUNT(amen_id) AS amenity_count
  FROM base
  GROUP BY dorm_id
),
top_dorm AS (
  SELECT dorm_id
  FROM dorm_with_amenity_count
  ORDER BY amenity_count DESC
  LIMIT 1
),
students_in_top_dorm AS (
  SELECT s.first_name
  FROM {{ ref('stg_student') }} s
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_lives_in') }} l ON s.student_id = l.student_id
  WHERE l.dorm_id IN (SELECT dorm_id FROM top_dorm)
)
SELECT first_name
FROM students_in_top_dorm
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_dorm_amenities
    description: "Students living in the dorm with the most amenities."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per student"
    columns:
      - name: first_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

all_star_data AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_all_star') }}
)
SELECT
    p.first_name,
    p.last_name,
    p.player_id,

FROM player_data p
INNER JOIN all_star_data a ON p.player_id = a.player_id
GROUP BY p.player_id, p.first_name, p.last_name
ORDER BY all_star_count DESC
LIMIT 1

Business question: Which products has been complained by the customer who has filed least amount of complaints?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE customers (customer_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE products (product_name VARCHAR, product_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE complaints (product_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_customers.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT customer_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'customers') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_products.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT product_id, product_name FROM {{ source('raw', 'products') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_complaints.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT product_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'complaints') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: customers
      - name: products
      - name: complaints
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_customers
    columns:
      - name: customer_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_products
    columns:
      - name: product_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: product_name
  - name: stg_complaints
    columns:
      - name: product_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_customer_complaints.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH customer_complaints AS (
  SELECT
    c.customer_id,
    p.product_name,
    COUNT(DISTINCT co.product_id) AS complaint_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_customers') }} c
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_complaints') }} co ON true
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_products') }} p ON co.product_id = p.product_id
  GROUP BY c.customer_id, p.product_name
)
SELECT
  customer_id,
  product_name,
  complaint_count
FROM customer_complaints
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/_intermediate_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: int_customer_complaints
    description: "Customer complaints joined with product information"
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_least_complained_products.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH complaint_counts AS (
  SELECT
    customer_id,
    product_name,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY COUNT(*) ASC) AS complaint_rank
  FROM {{ ref('int_customer_complaints') }}
  GROUP BY customer_id, product_name
)
SELECT DISTINCT product_name
FROM complaint_counts
WHERE complaint_rank = 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_least_complained_products
    description: "Products that have been complained by the customer who has filed least amount of complaints"
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per product with least complaints"
    columns:
      - name: product_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: What are the id and the amount of refund of the booking that incurred the most times of payments?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Payments (booking_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Bookings (booking_id VARCHAR, amount_of_refund VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_payments.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT booking_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Payments') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_bookings.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT booking_id, amount_of_refund FROM {{ source('raw', 'Bookings') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Payments
      - name: Bookings
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_payments
    columns:
      - name: booking_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_bookings
    columns:
      - name: booking_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: amount_of_refund
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_top_refund_booking.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH joined_data AS (
  SELECT
    b.booking_id,
    b.amount_of_refund
  FROM {{ ref('stg_bookings') }} b
  INNER JOIN {{ ref('stg_payments') }} p ON b.booking_id = p.booking_id
),
refund_counts AS (
  SELECT
    booking_id,
    amount_of_refund,
    COUNT(*) AS payment_count
  FROM joined_data
  GROUP BY booking_id, amount_of_refund
)
SELECT
  booking_id,
  amount_of_refund
FROM refund_counts
ORDER BY payment_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_top_refund_booking
    description: "Booking with the highest number of payments and its refund amount."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per booking with highest payment count"
    columns:
      - name: booking_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: amount_of_refund
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Find the program which most number of students are enrolled in. List both the id and the summary.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Degree_Programs (degree_program_id VARCHAR, degree_summary_name VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Student_Enrolment (degree_program_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_degree_programs.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

  degree_program_id,
  degree_summary_name AS degree_summary_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Degree_Programs') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_student_enrolment.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
  degree_program_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Student_Enrolment') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2

  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Degree_Programs
      - name: Student_Enrolment
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_degree_programs
    columns:
      - name: degree_program_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: degree_summary_name

  - name: stg_student_enrolment
    columns:
      - name: degree_program_id

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_popular_program.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    dp.degree_program_id,
    dp.degree_summary_name,
    COUNT(se.degree_program_id) AS enrolment_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_degree_programs') }} dp
  INNER JOIN {{ ref('stg_student_enrolment') }} se
    ON dp.degree_program_id = se.degree_program_id
  GROUP BY dp.degree_program_id, dp.degree_summary_name
)
SELECT
  degree_program_id,
  degree_summary_name
FROM base
ORDER BY enrolment_count DESC
LIMIT 1

CREATE TABLE accelerator_compatible_browser (accelerator_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE web_client_accelerator (id VARCHAR, Name VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_accelerator_compatible_browser.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT accelerator_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'accelerator_compatible_browser') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_web_client_accelerator.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT id AS accelerator_id, Name FROM {{ source('raw', 'web_client_accelerator') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: accelerator_compatible_browser
      - name: web_client_accelerator
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_accelerator_compatible_browser
    columns:
      - name: accelerator_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_web_client_accelerator
    columns:
      - name: accelerator_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: Name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_accelerators_compatible_with_multiple_browsers.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    wca.accelerator_id,
    wca.Name
  FROM {{ ref('stg_web_client_accelerator') }} AS wca
  INNER JOIN {{ ref('stg_accelerator_compatible_browser') }} AS acb
    ON wca.accelerator_id = acb.accelerator_id
)
SELECT
  accelerator_id,
  Name
FROM base
GROUP BY accelerator_id, Name
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_accelerators_compatible_with_multiple_browsers
    description: "Web accelerators compatible with two or more browsers."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per accelerator"
    columns:
      - name: accelerator_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: Name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: What is the name of the document with the most number of sections?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE document_sections (document_code VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE documents (document_name VARCHAR, document_code VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_document_sections.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT document_code FROM {{ source('raw', 'document_sections') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_documents.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT document_name, document_code FROM {{ source('raw', 'documents') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: document_sections
      - name: documents
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_document_sections
    description: "Staging table for document sections."
  - name: stg_documents
    description: "Staging table for documents."
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_document_with_most_sections.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    d.document_name,
    COUNT(*) AS section_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_documents') }} d
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_document_sections') }} s ON d.document_code = s.document_code
  GROUP BY d.document_name, d.document_code
)
SELECT document_name FROM base ORDER BY section_count DESC LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_document_with_most_sections
    description: "Document with the most sections."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per document"
</file>

Business question: Which vocal type has the band mate with first name "Marianne" played the most?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE vocals (bandmate VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE band (id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_vocals.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT bandmate AS bandmate_name FROM {{ source('raw', 'vocals') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_band.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT id AS band_id, firstname FROM {{ source('raw', 'band') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: vocals
      - name: band
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_vocals
    description: "Staging model for vocals data."
  - name: stg_band
    description: "Staging model for band data."
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_marianne_vocal_type.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT v.bandmate_name, b.firstname
  FROM {{ ref('stg_vocals') }} v
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_band') }} b ON v.bandmate_name = b.band_id
  WHERE b.firstname = 'Marianne'
)
SELECT bandmate_name AS vocal_type, COUNT(*) AS count
FROM base
GROUP BY bandmate_name
ORDER BY count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_marianne_vocal_type
    description: "Most common vocal type played by band mate with first name Marianne."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per vocal type"
</file>

Business question: List the names of all distinct wines that are made of red color grape.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE GRAPES (Grape VARCHAR, Color VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE WINE (Name VARCHAR, Grape VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_grapes.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT grape AS grape, color AS color FROM {{ source('raw', 'GRAPES') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_wine.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT name AS wine_name, grape AS wine_grape FROM {{ source('raw', 'WINE') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: GRAPES
      - name: WINE
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_grapes
    columns:
      - name: grape
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: color
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_wine
    columns:
      - name: wine_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: wine_grape
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_red_wines.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_grapes') }}),
wines AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_wine') }})
SELECT DISTINCT w.wine_name
FROM base g
INNER JOIN wines w ON g.grape = w.wine_grape
WHERE g.color = 'Red'
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: rpt_red_wines
    description: "List of wines made from red grapes."
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per distinct wine name"
    columns:
      - name: wine_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: What instruments does the the song "Le Pop" use?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE instruments (songid VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE songs (songid VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">

  - name: raw
    schema: public
    tables:
      - name: instruments
        identifier: instruments
      - name: songs
        identifier: songs
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_instruments
    description: "Staging model for instruments table"
  - name: stg_songs
    description: "Staging model for songs table"
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_instruments.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    songid AS song_id,
    instrument
FROM {{ source('raw', 'instruments') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_songs.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    songid AS song_id,
    title
FROM {{ source('raw', 'songs') }}
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: rpt_le_pop_instruments
    description: "Report showing instruments used in the song 'Le Pop'"

        i.instrument,

    JOIN {{ ref('stg_songs') }} AS s ON i.song_id = s.song_id

FROM base

Business question: Show names for all aircraft with at least two flights.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Aircraft (name VARCHAR, aid VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Flight (aid VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_aircraft.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    name AS aircraft_name,
    aid AS aircraft_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Aircraft') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_flight.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    aid AS aircraft_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Flight') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Aircraft
      - name: Flight
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_aircraft
    columns:
      - name: aircraft_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_flight
    columns:
      - name: aircraft_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_aircraft_with_multiple_flights.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH flight_counts AS (
    SELECT
        f.aircraft_id,
        COUNT(*) AS flight_count
    FROM {{ ref('stg_flight') }} f
    GROUP BY f.aircraft_id
)
SELECT
    a.aircraft_name AS name,
    fc.flight_count AS flight_count
FROM {{ ref('stg_aircraft') }} a
INNER JOIN flight_counts fc ON a.aircraft_id = fc.aircraft_id
WHERE fc.flight_count >= 2
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_aircraft_with_multiple_flights
    description: "List of aircraft with at least two flights."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per aircraft with >=2 flights"
    columns:
      - name: name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: flight_count
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: How many settlements were made on the claim with the most recent claim settlement date? List the number and the claim id.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Settlements (claim_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Claims (claim_id VARCHAR, Date_Claim_Settled VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_claims.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT claim_id, Date_Claim_Settled AS date_claim_settled FROM {{ source('raw', 'Claims') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_settlements.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT claim_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Settlements') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Claims
      - name: Settlements
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_claims
    description: "Staging table for claims data."
    columns:
      - name: claim_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: date_claim_settled
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_settlements
    description: "Staging table for settlements data."
    columns:
      - name: claim_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_settlements_by_claim.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    c.claim_id,
    c.date_claim_settled,
    COUNT(s.claim_id) AS settlement_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_claims') }} c
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_settlements') }} s ON c.claim_id = s.claim_id
  GROUP BY c.claim_id, c.date_claim_settled
)
SELECT
  claim_id,
  settlement_count AS number_of_settlements
FROM base
ORDER BY date_claim_settled DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_settlements_by_claim
    description: "Number of settlements per claim, with the most recent claim settlement date."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per claim"
    columns:
      - name: claim_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: number_of_settlements
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_phone.sql">

  Hardware_Model_name AS hardware_model_name

<file path="models/staging/stg_screen_mode.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
  Char_cells AS char_cells,
  Pixels AS pixels,
  Hardware_colours AS hardware_colours,
  Graphics_mode AS graphics_mode
FROM {{ source('raw', 'screen_mode') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2

  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: phone

        tests: [not_null, unique]

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_lg_p760_screen_details.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH phone_filtered AS (
  SELECT
    screen_mode,
    hardware_model_name
  FROM {{ ref('stg_phone') }}

),
screen_details AS (
  SELECT
    char_cells,
    pixels,
    hardware_colours,

  s.char_cells,
  s.pixels,
  s.hardware_colours
FROM phone_filtered p
JOIN screen_details s ON s.graphics_mode = p.screen_mode

</file>

Business question: What are the first and last name of the faculty who has the most students?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Student (advisor VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Faculty (fname VARCHAR, lname VARCHAR, FacID VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_student.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT advisor AS faculty_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Student') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_faculty.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT fname, lname, FacID AS faculty_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Faculty') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Student
      - name: Faculty
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_student
    columns:
      - name: faculty_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_faculty
    columns:
      - name: faculty_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_top_faculty.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH faculty_student_counts AS (
  SELECT
    f.faculty_id,
    COUNT(s.faculty_id) AS student_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_faculty') }} f
  LEFT JOIN {{ ref('stg_student') }} s ON f.faculty_id = s.faculty_id
  GROUP BY f.faculty_id
),
top_faculty AS (
  SELECT faculty_id
  FROM faculty_student_counts
  ORDER BY student_count DESC
  LIMIT 1
),
result AS (
  SELECT f.fname, f.lname
  FROM {{ ref('stg_faculty') }} f
  JOIN top_faculty tf ON f.faculty_id = tf.faculty_id
)
SELECT fname, lname FROM result
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_top_faculty
    description: "Faculty member with the most students"
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per faculty with most students"
    columns:
      - name: fname
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: lname
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

models:

<file path="models/intermediate/int_phone_screen_modes.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH phone_data AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_phone') }}
),
screen_mode_data AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_screen_mode') }}
)
SELECT
    s.pixels,
    p.accreditation_type
FROM screen_mode_data s
INNER JOIN phone_data p ON s.graphics_mode = p.graphics_mode

<file path="models/intermediate/_intermediate_models.yml">

models:
  - name: int_phone_screen_modes

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_shared_screen_pixels.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('int_phone_screen_modes') }}
),
full_accreditation AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT pixels
    FROM base
    WHERE accreditation_type = 'Full'
),
provisional_accreditation AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT pixels
    FROM base
    WHERE accreditation_type = 'Provisional'
)
SELECT DISTINCT f.pixels AS shared_screen_pixels
FROM full_accreditation f
INNER JOIN provisional_accreditation p ON f.pixels = p.pixels
</file>

models:

        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: Show the name of the party that has the most delegates.

SQL schemas:

<file path="models/staging/stg_election.sql">

SELECT Party AS party_name FROM {{ source('raw', 'election') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_party.sql">

</file>

  - name: raw

      - name: election
      - name: party

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_election
    columns:
      - name: party_name
        tests: [not_null]

      - name: party_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_party_with_most_delegates.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH election_parties AS (
  SELECT party_name FROM {{ ref('stg_election') }}
),
party_delegates AS (
  SELECT p.party_name, COUNT(*) AS delegate_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_party') }} p
  JOIN election_parties e ON p.party_id = e.party_name
  GROUP BY p.party_name
)
SELECT party_name AS winning_party, delegate_count
FROM party_delegates
ORDER BY delegate_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2

      grain: "1 row per winning party"
    columns:
      - name: winning_party
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: delegate_count
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: What is the id and type code for the template used by the most documents?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Documents (template_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Templates (Template_Type_Code VARCHAR, template_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Documents
      - name: Templates
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_documents
    columns:
      - name: template_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_templates
    columns:
      - name: template_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: template_type_code
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_documents.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT template_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Documents') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_templates.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT template_id, Template_Type_Code AS template_type_code FROM {{ source('raw', 'Templates') }}
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_most_used_template.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH joined_data AS (
  SELECT
    d.template_id,
    t.template_type_code
  FROM {{ ref('stg_documents') }} AS d
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_templates') }} AS t ON d.template_id = t.template_id
),
ranked_templates AS (
  SELECT
    template_id,
    template_type_code,
    COUNT(*) AS document_count,
    RANK() OVER (ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC) AS rank
  FROM joined_data
  GROUP BY template_id, template_type_code
)
SELECT
  template_id,
  template_type_code AS type_code
FROM ranked_templates
WHERE rank = 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_most_used_template
    description: "The template used by the most documents, with its type code."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per most used template"
    columns:
      - name: template_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: type_code
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: For each director, return the director's name together with the title of the movie they directed that received the highest rating among all of their movies, and the value of that rating. Ignore movies whose director is NULL.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Rating (stars INTEGER, mID VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Movie (title VARCHAR, director VARCHAR, mID VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_rating.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    stars,
    mid AS movie_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Rating') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_movie.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    title,
    director,
    mid AS movie_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Movie') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Rating
      - name: Movie
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_rating
    columns:
      - name: stars
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: movie_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_movie
    columns:
      - name: title
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: director
        tests: []
      - name: movie_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_top_rated_movies.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        m.title,
        r.stars,
        m.director
    FROM {{ ref('stg_rating') }} r
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_movie') }} m ON r.movie_id = m.movie_id
    WHERE m.director IS NOT NULL
),
ranked AS (
    SELECT
        director,
        title,
        stars,
        RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY director ORDER BY stars DESC) AS rank
    FROM base
)
SELECT
    title,
    stars,
    director
FROM ranked
WHERE rank = 1

Business question: Which product has been ordered most number of times?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE products (product_details VARCHAR, product_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE order_items (product_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_products.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    product_id,
    product_details AS product_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'products') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_order_items.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    product_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'order_items') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: products
      - name: order_items
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_products
    columns:
      - name: product_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_order_items
    columns:
      - name: product_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_most_ordered_product.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        o.product_id,
        p.product_name
    FROM {{ ref('stg_order_items') }} o
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_products') }} p ON o.product_id = p.product_id
),
aggregated AS (
    SELECT
        product_name,
        COUNT(*) AS order_count
    FROM base
    GROUP BY product_id, product_name
)
SELECT
    product_name,
    order_count
FROM aggregated
ORDER BY order_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_most_ordered_product
    description: "The product that has been ordered the most number of times."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per most ordered product"
    columns:
      - name: product_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: order_count
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_customer_addresses.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH customer_addresses AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT a.city_town
  FROM {{ ref('stg_addresses') }} a
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_customers') }} c ON a.address_id = c.address_id
),
performer_addresses AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT a.city_town
  FROM {{ ref('stg_addresses') }} a
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_performers') }} p ON a.address_id = p.address_id
)
SELECT ca.city_town FROM customer_addresses ca
EXCEPT
SELECT pa.city_town FROM performer_addresses pa
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/_intermediate_models.yml">

Business question: Find the physician who was trained in the most expensive procedure?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE trained_in (physician VARCHAR, treatment VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE physician (name VARCHAR, employeeid VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE procedures (code VARCHAR, cost VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_physician.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_trained_in.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    physician AS physician_employee_id,
    treatment AS procedure_code
FROM {{ source('raw', 'trained_in') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_procedures.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    code AS procedure_code,
    cost::decimal AS procedure_cost
FROM {{ source('raw', 'procedures') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: physician
      - name: trained_in
      - name: procedures
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_physician
    columns:
      - name: physician_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: employee_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_trained_in
    columns:
      - name: physician_employee_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: procedure_code
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_procedures
    columns:
      - name: procedure_code
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: procedure_cost
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_physician_procedures.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH physician_data AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_physician') }}
),
trained_in_data AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_trained_in') }}
),
procedure_costs AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_procedures') }}
)
SELECT
    p.physician_name,
    t.procedure_code,
    c.procedure_cost
FROM physician_data p
INNER JOIN trained_in_data t ON p.employee_id = t.physician_employee_id
INNER JOIN procedure_costs c ON t.procedure_code = c.procedure_code
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/_intermediate_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: int_physician_procedures

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_most_expensive_procedure_physician.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base_data AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('int_physician_procedures') }}
)
SELECT
    physician_name AS top_physician
FROM base_data
ORDER BY procedure_cost DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:

      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per physician with highest cost procedure"
    columns:
      - name: top_physician
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: What is the name of the activity that has the most faculty members involved in?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Faculty_participates_in (actID VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Activity (activity_name VARCHAR, actID VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_faculty_participates_in.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT actID FROM {{ source('raw', 'Faculty_participates_in') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_activity.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Faculty_participates_in
      - name: Activity
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_faculty_participates_in
    columns:
      - name: actID
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_activity

        tests: [not_null]
      - name: actID
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_most_faculty_activity.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH activity_participation AS (
  SELECT
    T1.activity_name,
    COUNT(*) AS faculty_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_activity') }} AS T1
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_faculty_participates_in') }} AS T2 ON T1.actID = T2.actID
  GROUP BY T1.activity_name, T1.actID
)
SELECT activity_name FROM activity_participation
ORDER BY faculty_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

    columns:
      - name: activity_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: find the name and age of the pilot who has won the most number of times among the pilots who are younger than 30.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE MATCH (winning_pilot VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE pilot (name VARCHAR, age INTEGER, pilot_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_match.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT winning_pilot FROM {{ source('raw', 'MATCH') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_pilot.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    name,
    age,
    pilot_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'pilot') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: MATCH
      - name: pilot
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_match
    description: "Staging model for match data."
    columns:
      - name: winning_pilot
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_pilot
    description: "Staging model for pilot data."
    columns:
      - name: name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: age
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: pilot_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_youngest_winning_pilot.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH winning_pilots AS (
    SELECT
        p.name,
        p.age
    FROM {{ ref('stg_pilot') }} AS p
    INNER JOIN {{ ref('stg_match') }} AS m ON p.pilot_id = m.winning_pilot
    WHERE p.age < 30
),
pilot_wins AS (
    SELECT
        name,
        age,
        COUNT(*) AS win_count
    FROM winning_pilots
    GROUP BY name, age
)
SELECT
    name,
    age
FROM pilot_wins
ORDER BY win_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_youngest_winning_pilot
    description: "The name and age of the youngest pilot who has won the most number of times."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per youngest winning pilot"
    columns:
      - name: name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: age
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH cis_220_professors AS (

    FROM {{ ref('stg_employee') }} e

qm_261_professors AS (

Business question: Find the name of bank branch that provided the greatest total amount of loans.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE loan (branch_id VARCHAR, amount INTEGER); CREATE TABLE bank (bname VARCHAR, branch_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: loan
      - name: bank
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_loan
    description: "Staging model for loan data."
    columns:
      - name: branch_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: amount
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_bank
    description: "Staging model for bank data."
    columns:
      - name: bname
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: branch_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_loan.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
  branch_id,
  amount
FROM {{ source('raw', 'loan') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_bank.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
  bname,
  branch_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'bank') }}
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_top_loan_branch
    description: "Bank branch with the greatest total loan amount."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per top branch"
    columns:
      - name: bname
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_top_loan_branch.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH loan_data AS (
  SELECT
    branch_id,
    amount
  FROM {{ ref('stg_loan') }}
),
bank_data AS (
  SELECT
    bname,
    branch_id
  FROM {{ ref('stg_bank') }}
),
joined_data AS (
  SELECT
    b.bname,
    l.amount
  FROM bank_data b
  INNER JOIN loan_data l ON b.branch_id = l.branch_id
)
SELECT
  bname
FROM joined_data
GROUP BY bname
ORDER BY SUM(amount) DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

Business question: Which address holds the most number of students currently? List the address id and all lines.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Addresses (address_id VARCHAR, line_1 VARCHAR, line_2 VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Students (current_address_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_addresses.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    address_id,
    line_1 AS address_line_1,
    line_2 AS address_line_2
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Addresses') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_students.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    current_address_id AS student_current_address_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Students') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Addresses
      - name: Students
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_addresses
    columns:
      - name: address_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_students
    columns:
      - name: student_current_address_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_address_with_most_students.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        a.address_id,
        a.address_line_1,
        a.address_line_2,
        COUNT(s.student_current_address_id) AS student_count
    FROM {{ ref('stg_addresses') }} a
    INNER JOIN {{ ref('stg_students') }} s ON a.address_id = s.student_current_address_id
    GROUP BY a.address_id, a.address_line_1, a.address_line_2
)
SELECT
    address_id,
    address_line_1 AS line_1,
    address_line_2 AS line_2
FROM base
ORDER BY student_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_address_with_most_students
    description: "Address with the highest number of current students."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per address with most students"
    columns:
      - name: address_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: line_1
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: line_2
</file>

{{ config(materialized='table') }}

),
many_outcome_tasks AS (

)

<file path="models/staging/stg_owners.sql">

    last_name

FROM {{ source('raw', 'Dogs') }}
</file>

version: 2

  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Owners
      - name: Dogs
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_owners
    description: "Staging model for owners data."
    columns:
      - name: owner_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: first_name

      - name: last_name

    description: "Staging model for dogs data."

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_top_dog_owner.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH owner_with_dogs AS (
    SELECT
        o.owner_id,
        o.first_name,
        o.last_name,
        COUNT(d.owner_id) AS dog_count
    FROM {{ ref('stg_owners') }} o
    INNER JOIN {{ ref('stg_dogs') }} d ON o.owner_id = d.owner_id
    GROUP BY o.owner_id, o.first_name, o.last_name
)
SELECT
    owner_id,
    first_name,
    last_name
FROM owner_with_dogs
ORDER BY dog_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

      - name: owner_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: first_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: last_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: What is the average gpa of the students enrolled in the course with code ACCT-211?

<file path="models/staging/stg_enroll.sql">

<file path="models/staging/stg_class.sql">

</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_student.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH enroll_data AS (

),
student_data AS (

)

Business question: What is the name of the most common genre in all tracks?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE GENRE (Name VARCHAR, GenreId VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE TRACK (GenreId VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_genre.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_track.sql">

</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_most_common_genre.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH track_genres AS (
  SELECT t.genre_id, g.genre_name

  INNER JOIN {{ ref('stg_genre') }} AS g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id

LIMIT 1

Business question: What are all the policy types of the customer that has the most policies listed?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE customers (customer_id VARCHAR, customer_name VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE available_policies (policy_type_code VARCHAR, policy_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE customers_policies (customer_id VARCHAR, policy_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_customers.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT customer_id, customer_name FROM {{ source('raw', 'customers') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_available_policies.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT policy_type_code, policy_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'available_policies') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_customers_policies.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT customer_id, policy_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'customers_policies') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: customers
      - name: available_policies
      - name: customers_policies
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_customers
    columns:
      - name: customer_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_available_policies
    columns:
      - name: policy_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_customers_policies
    columns:
      - name: customer_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: policy_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_customer_policy_mapping.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH customers AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_customers') }}),
customers_policies AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_customers_policies') }}),
available_policies AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_available_policies') }})
SELECT
    c.customer_id,
    c.customer_name,
    ap.policy_type_code,
    ap.policy_id
FROM customers_policies cp
JOIN customers c ON cp.customer_id = c.customer_id
JOIN available_policies ap ON cp.policy_id = ap.policy_id
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/_intermediate_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: int_customer_policy_mapping
    description: "Joined customer, policy, and available policies data."
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_policy_types_by_top_customer.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('int_customer_policy_mapping') }}),
customer_policy_counts AS (
    SELECT
        customer_id,
        COUNT(*) AS policy_count
    FROM base
    GROUP BY customer_id
),
top_customer AS (
    SELECT customer_name
    FROM base
    JOIN customer_policy_counts cpc ON base.customer_id = cpc.customer_id
    GROUP BY customer_name
    ORDER BY SUM(cpc.policy_count) DESC
    LIMIT 1
)
SELECT DISTINCT policy_type_code FROM base
JOIN top_customer tc ON base.customer_name = tc.customer_name
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_policy_types_by_top_customer
    description: "Policy types of the customer with the most policies."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per policy type of top customer"
    columns:
      - name: policy_type_code
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Which film has the most copies in the inventory? List both title and id.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE film (title VARCHAR, film_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE inventory (film_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_film.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT title, film_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'film') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_inventory.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT film_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'inventory') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: film
      - name: inventory
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_film
    columns:
      - name: film_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_inventory
    columns:
      - name: film_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_film_with_most_copies.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH film_inventory AS (
  SELECT f.title, f.film_id
  FROM {{ ref('stg_film') }} AS f
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_inventory') }} AS i ON f.film_id = i.film_id
  GROUP BY f.film_id
)
SELECT title, film_id
FROM film_inventory
ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_film_with_most_copies
    description: "Film with the most copies in inventory."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per film with most copies"
    columns:
      - name: title
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: film_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: Show the names of products that are in at least two events.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Products (Product_Name VARCHAR, Product_ID VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Products_in_Events (Product_ID VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_products.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    Product_Name AS product_name,
    Product_ID AS product_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Products') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_products_in_events.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    Product_ID AS product_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Products_in_Events') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Products
      - name: Products_in_Events
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_products
    columns:
      - name: product_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_products_in_events
    columns:
      - name: product_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_products_in_multiple_events.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        p.product_name,
        p.product_id,
        COUNT(e.product_id) AS event_count
    FROM {{ ref('stg_products') }} p
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_products_in_events') }} e ON p.product_id = e.product_id
    GROUP BY p.product_name, p.product_id
)
SELECT
    product_name
FROM base
WHERE event_count >= 2
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_products_in_multiple_events
    description: "Products that appear in at least two events."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per product"
    columns:
      - name: product_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: Which film actor (actress) starred the most films? List his or her first name, last name and actor id.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE film_actor (actor_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE actor (first_name VARCHAR, last_name VARCHAR, actor_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_film_actor.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT actor_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'film_actor') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_actor.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    actor_id,
    first_name,
    last_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'actor') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: film_actor
      - name: actor
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_film_actor
    columns:
      - name: actor_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_actor
    columns:
      - name: actor_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: first_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: last_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_top_actor.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH actor_films AS (
    SELECT
        a.actor_id,
        a.first_name,
        a.last_name,
        COUNT(*) AS film_count
    FROM {{ ref('stg_actor') }} a
    INNER JOIN {{ ref('stg_film_actor') }} fa ON a.actor_id = fa.actor_id
    GROUP BY a.actor_id, a.first_name, a.last_name
)
SELECT
    first_name,
    last_name,
    actor_id
FROM actor_films
ORDER BY film_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_top_actor
    description: "Top actor who starred in the most films."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per top actor"
    columns:
      - name: first_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: last_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: actor_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: Find the number of routes for each source airport and the airport name.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE airports (name VARCHAR, apid VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE routes (src_apid VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_airports.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT name, apid FROM {{ source('raw', 'airports') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_routes.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT src_apid FROM {{ source('raw', 'routes') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2

  - name: raw

      - name: airports
      - name: routes
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_airports

        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_routes

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_routes_by_source_airport.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_airports') }}),
routes AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_routes') }})
SELECT COUNT(*) AS route_count, T1.name

GROUP BY T1.name

    columns:

        tests: [not_null]
      - name: name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: What is the id and name of the browser that is compatible with the most web accelerators?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE browser (id VARCHAR, name VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE accelerator_compatible_browser (browser_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_browser.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT id AS browser_id, name FROM {{ source('raw', 'browser') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_accelerator_compatible_browser.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT browser_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'accelerator_compatible_browser') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: browser
      - name: accelerator_compatible_browser
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_browser
    columns:
      - name: browser_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_accelerator_compatible_browser
    columns:
      - name: browser_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_most_compatible_browser.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH browser_with_accelerators AS (
  SELECT
    b.browser_id,
    b.name,
    COUNT(a.browser_id) AS accelerator_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_browser') }} b
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_accelerator_compatible_browser') }} a ON b.browser_id = a.browser_id
  GROUP BY b.browser_id, b.name
)
SELECT
  browser_id AS id,
  name
FROM browser_with_accelerators
ORDER BY accelerator_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_most_compatible_browser
    description: "Browser with the highest number of compatible web accelerators."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per browser"
    columns:
      - name: id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_degrees.sql">

</file>

Business question: Which countries have at least 3 cities?

CREATE TABLE country (country VARCHAR, country_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE city (country_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_country.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    country AS country_name,
    country_id

</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_city.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

    country_id

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">

models:

    columns:

        tests: [not_null]

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_countries_with_many_cities.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH cities AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_city') }}
),
countries AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_country') }}
)
SELECT
    c.country_name,
    COUNT(c.city.country_id) AS city_count
FROM countries c

HAVING COUNT(ci.country_id) >= 3
</file>

Business question: Show the id and details of the investor that has the largest number of transactions.

<file path="models/staging/stg_transactions.sql">

Business question: For each Orange county campus, report the number of degrees granted after 2000.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE campuses (campus VARCHAR, id VARCHAR, county VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE degrees (degrees INTEGER, campus VARCHAR, year VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_campuses.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    campus AS campus_name,
    id AS campus_id,
    county AS county_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'campuses') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_degrees.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    degrees AS degree_count,
    campus AS campus_id,
    year AS degree_year
FROM {{ source('raw', 'degrees') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: campuses
      - name: degrees
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_campuses
    columns:
      - name: campus_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: county_name
  - name: stg_degrees
    columns:
      - name: campus_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_orange_county_degrees.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH orange_campuses AS (
    SELECT
        campus_id,
        campus_name
    FROM {{ ref('stg_campuses') }}
    WHERE county_name = 'Orange'
),
post2000_degrees AS (
    SELECT
        campus_id,
        SUM(degree_count) AS total_degrees
    FROM {{ ref('stg_degrees') }}
    WHERE degree_year >= 2000
    GROUP BY campus_id
)
SELECT
    oc.campus_name,
    pd.total_degrees AS degrees_granted_after_2000
FROM orange_campuses oc
JOIN post2000_degrees pd ON oc.campus_id = pd.campus_id
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_orange_county_degrees
    description: "Number of degrees granted after 2000 for each Orange County campus"
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per Orange County campus"
    columns:
      - name: campus_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: degrees_granted_after_2000
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Find the organisation ids and details of the organisations which are involved in

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Grants (organisation_id VARCHAR, grant_amount INTEGER); CREATE TABLE Organisations (organisation_id VARCHAR, organisation_details VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_grants.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    organisation_id,
    grant_amount
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Grants') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_organisations.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

    organisation_id,
    organisation_details
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Organisations') }}

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">

  - name: raw

      - name: Grants

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2

  - name: stg_grants
    columns:

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_organisations_with_high_grants.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH grants AS (
    SELECT
        organisation_id,
        SUM(grant_amount) AS total_grant_amount
    FROM {{ ref('stg_grants') }}
    GROUP BY organisation_id
),
organisations_with_high_grants AS (
    SELECT
        o.organisation_id,
        o.organisation_details
    FROM grants g
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_organisations') }} o ON g.organisation_id = o.organisation_id
    WHERE total_grant_amount > 6000
)
SELECT
    organisation_id,
    organisation_details
FROM organisations_with_high_grants
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_organisations_with_high_grants
    description: "Organisation IDs and details of organisations involved in grants with total amount greater than 6000."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per organisation with high grant amount"
    columns:

Business question: Find the name and address of the customers who have both New and Pending orders.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE customers (customer_name VARCHAR, customer_address VARCHAR, customer_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE customer_orders (customer_id VARCHAR, order_status_code VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_customers.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    customer_id,
    customer_name AS name,
    customer_address AS address
FROM {{ source('raw', 'customers') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_customer_orders.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    customer_id,
    order_status_code AS status
FROM {{ source('raw', 'customer_orders') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: customers
      - name: customer_orders
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_customers
    columns:
      - name: customer_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_customer_orders
    columns:
      - name: customer_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_customer_orders_status.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH customers AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_customers') }}
),
orders AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_customer_orders') }}
)
SELECT
    c.customer_id,
    c.name,
    c.address,
    o.status
FROM customers c
INNER JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/_intermediate_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: int_customer_orders_status
    description: "Joined customers with their orders including status."
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_customers_with_new_and_pending_orders.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT * FROM {{ ref('int_customer_orders_status') }}
),
new_customers AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT customer_id, name, address
    FROM base
    WHERE status = 'New'
),
pending_customers AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT customer_id, name, address
    FROM base
    WHERE status = 'Pending'
)
SELECT
    nc.name,
    nc.address
FROM new_customers nc
INNER JOIN pending_customers pc ON nc.customer_id = pc.customer_id
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_customers_with_new_and_pending_orders
    description: "Customers who have both New and Pending orders."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per customer with both statuses"
    columns:
      - name: name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: address
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: How many countries does each continent have? List the continent id, continent name and the number of countries.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE COUNTRIES (Continent VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE CONTINENTS (ContId VARCHAR, Continent VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_countries.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT Continent AS continent FROM {{ source('raw', 'COUNTRIES') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_continents.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT ContId AS continent_id, Continent AS continent_name FROM {{ source('raw', 'CONTINENTS') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: COUNTRIES
      - name: CONTINENTS
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_countries
    description: "Cleaned countries data."
    columns:
      - name: continent
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_continents
    description: "Cleaned continents data."
    columns:
      - name: continent_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: continent_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_countries_by_continent.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    c.continent_id,
    c.continent_name,
    c2.continent
  FROM {{ ref('stg_continents') }} AS c
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_countries') }} AS c2 ON c.continent_id = c2.continent
)
SELECT
  continent_id,
  continent_name,
  COUNT(*) AS country_count
FROM base
GROUP BY continent_id, continent_name
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">

models:

      - name: continent_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]

        tests: [not_null]
      - name: country_count
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: What is the salary and name of the employee who has the most number of certificates on aircrafts with distance more than 5000?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Aircraft (aid VARCHAR, distance INTEGER); CREATE TABLE Employee (name VARCHAR, eid VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Certificate (eid VARCHAR, aid VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_aircraft.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT aid AS aircraft_id, distance FROM {{ source('raw', 'Aircraft') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_employee.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT name, eid AS employee_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Employee') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_certificate.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT eid AS employee_id, aid AS aircraft_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Certificate') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Aircraft
      - name: Employee
      - name: Certificate
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_aircraft
    columns:
      - name: aircraft_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: distance
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_employee
    columns:
      - name: employee_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: name
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_certificate
    columns:
      - name: employee_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: aircraft_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_employee_certificates.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH aircraft AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_aircraft') }}),
employee AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_employee') }}),
certificate AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_certificate') }})
SELECT
    e.employee_id,
    e.name,
    c.aircraft_id,
    a.distance
FROM employee e
INNER JOIN certificate c ON e.employee_id = c.employee_id
INNER JOIN aircraft a ON a.aircraft_id = c.aircraft_id
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/_intermediate_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: int_employee_certificates
    description: "Employee certificates with aircraft distance information."
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_employee_certificates.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('int_employee_certificates') }})
SELECT
    name,
    COUNT(*) AS certificate_count
FROM base
WHERE distance > 5000
GROUP BY employee_id, name
ORDER BY certificate_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_employee_certificates
    description: "Employee with the most certificates on aircrafts with distance more than 5000."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per employee"
    columns:
      - name: name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: certificate_count
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: What is the structure of the document with the least number of accesses?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE document_structures (document_structure_description VARCHAR, document_structure_code VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE documents (document_structure_code VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_document_structures.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    document_structure_description AS document_structure_description,
    document_structure_code AS document_structure_code
FROM {{ source('raw', 'document_structures') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_documents.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    document_structure_code AS document_structure_code
FROM {{ source('raw', 'documents') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: document_structures
      - name: documents
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_document_structures
    description: "Staging model for document structures."
  - name: stg_documents
    description: "Staging model for documents."
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_least_accessed_structure.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH document_access_counts AS (
    SELECT
        t2.document_structure_description,
        COUNT(*) AS access_count
    FROM {{ ref('stg_documents') }} AS t1
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_document_structures') }} AS t2 ON t1.document_structure_code = t2.document_structure_code
    GROUP BY t1.document_structure_code, t2.document_structure_description
)
SELECT document_structure_description AS least_accessed_structure
FROM document_access_counts
ORDER BY access_count ASC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_least_accessed_structure
    description: "The document structure with the least number of accesses."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per least accessed structure"
</file>

),
ranked_artists AS (

)

Business question: Which claim incurred the most number of settlements? List the claim id, the date the claim was made, and the number.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Claims (claim_id VARCHAR, date_claim_made VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Settlements (claim_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_claims.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    claim_id,
    date_claim_made
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Claims') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_settlements.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    claim_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Settlements') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Claims
      - name: Settlements
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_claims
    description: "Staging table for claims data."
    columns:
      - name: claim_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: date_claim_made
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_settlements
    description: "Staging table for settlements data."
    columns:
      - name: claim_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_top_claim.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH claims AS (
    SELECT
        claim_id,
        date_claim_made
    FROM {{ ref('stg_claims') }}
),
settlements AS (
    SELECT
        claim_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_settlements') }}
),
claim_settlements AS (
    SELECT
        c.claim_id,
        c.date_claim_made,
        COUNT(s.claim_id) AS settlement_count
    FROM claims c
    JOIN settlements s ON c.claim_id = s.claim_id
    GROUP BY c.claim_id, c.date_claim_made
)
SELECT
    claim_id,
    date_claim_made,
    settlement_count
FROM claim_settlements
ORDER BY settlement_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_top_claim
    description: "Top claim by number of settlements."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per claim"
    columns:
      - name: claim_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: date_claim_made
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: settlement_count
        tests: [not_null, greater_than_zero]
</file>

Business question: which gender got the highest average uncertain ratio.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE candidate (people_id VARCHAR, unsure_rate INTEGER); CREATE TABLE people (sex VARCHAR, people_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_people.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    people_id AS people_id,
    sex AS sex
FROM {{ source('raw', 'people') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_candidate.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    people_id AS people_id,
    unsure_rate AS unsure_rate
FROM {{ source('raw', 'candidate') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: people
      - name: candidate
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_people
    description: "Staging model for people data."
    columns:
      - name: people_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: sex
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_candidate
    description: "Staging model for candidate data."
    columns:
      - name: people_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: unsure_rate
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_gender_average_unsure_rate.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH joined_data AS (
    SELECT
        p.sex,
        c.unsure_rate
    FROM {{ ref('stg_people') }} p
    INNER JOIN {{ ref('stg_candidate') }} c ON p.people_id = c.people_id
)
SELECT
    sex,
    AVG(unsure_rate) AS avg_unsure_rate
FROM joined_data
GROUP BY sex
ORDER BY AVG(unsure_rate) DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_gender_average_unsure_rate
    description: "Fact table showing the gender with the highest average uncertain ratio."

      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per gender"
    columns:
      - name: sex
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: avg_unsure_rate
        tests: [not_null]
</file>
