Business question: Which service id and type has the least number of participants?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Participants_in_Events (Participant_ID VARCHAR, Event_ID VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE services (Service_Type_Code VARCHAR, service_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE EVENTS (service_id VARCHAR, Event_ID VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE participants (Participant_ID VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_participants_in_events.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    Participant_ID AS participant_id,
    Event_ID AS event_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Participants_in_Events') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_services.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    Service_Type_Code AS service_type_code,
    service_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'services') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_events.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    service_id,
    Event_ID AS event_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'EVENTS') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_participants.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    Participant_ID AS participant_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'participants') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Participants_in_Events
      - name: services
      - name: EVENTS
      - name: participants
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_participants_in_events
    columns:
      - name: participant_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: event_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_services
    columns:
      - name: service_type_code
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: service_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_events
    columns:
      - name: service_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: event_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_participants
    columns:
      - name: participant_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_participant_events_services.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH participant_events AS (
    SELECT
        p.participant_id,
        pie.event_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_participants') }} p
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_participants_in_events') }} pie ON p.participant_id = pie.participant_id
),
event_services AS (
    SELECT
        e.service_id,
        e.event_id,
        s.service_type_code
    FROM {{ ref('stg_events') }} e
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_services') }} s ON e.service_id = s.service_id
)
SELECT
    es.service_id,
    es.service_type_code,
    pe.participant_id
FROM participant_events pe
JOIN event_services es ON pe.event_id = es.event_id
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/_intermediate_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: int_participant_events_services
    description: "Combined participant, event, and service data for analysis."
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_service_participation.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH participation_counts AS (
    SELECT
        service_id,
        service_type_code,
        COUNT(DISTINCT participant_id) AS participant_count
    FROM {{ ref('int_participant_events_services') }}
    GROUP BY service_id, service_type_code
)
SELECT
    service_id,
    service_type_code,
    participant_count
FROM participation_counts
ORDER BY participant_count ASC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_service_participation
    description: "Service with the least number of participants."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per service with minimal participants"
    columns:
      - name: service_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: service_type_code
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: participant_count
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: What is name of the country that speaks the largest number of languages?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE countrylanguage (CountryCode VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE country (Name VARCHAR, Code VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_countrylanguage.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT CountryCode AS country_code FROM {{ source('raw', 'countrylanguage') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_country.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT Name AS name, Code AS code FROM {{ source('raw', 'country') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: countrylanguage
      - name: country
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_countrylanguage
    columns:
      - name: country_code
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_country
    columns:
      - name: code
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_country_languages.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT c.name, COUNT(*) AS language_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_country') }} c
  INNER JOIN {{ ref('stg_countrylanguage') }} cl ON c.code = cl.country_code
  GROUP BY c.name
)
SELECT name AS country_name, language_count
FROM base
ORDER BY language_count DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_country_languages
    description: "Country with the largest number of spoken languages."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per country"
    columns:
      - name: country_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: language_count
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_staff.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    staff_address_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Staff') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_addresses.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    city,
    address_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Addresses') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Staff
      - name: Addresses
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_staff
    columns:
      - name: staff_address_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_addresses
    columns:
      - name: city
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: address_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_city_lives_staffs_city.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT T1.city, COUNT(*) FROM {{ ref('stg_addresses') }} AS T1 JOIN {{ ref('stg_staff') }} AS T2 ON T1.address_id = T2.staff_address_id GROUP BY T1.city ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1
)
SELECT *
FROM base
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">

models:
  - name: fct_city_lives_staffs_city
    description: "Which city lives most of staffs? List the city name and number of staffs."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per result"
    columns:
      - name: city
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: count_count
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Find the title of courses that have two prerequisites?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE prereq (course_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE course (title VARCHAR, course_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_prereq.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT course_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'prereq') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_course.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT title, course_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'course') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: prereq
      - name: course
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_prereq
    columns:
      - name: course_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_course
    columns:
      - name: title
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: course_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_courses_with_two_prereqs.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH prereq_courses AS (
  SELECT T2.course_id
  FROM {{ ref('stg_prereq') }} AS T2
  GROUP BY T2.course_id
  HAVING COUNT(*) = 2
)
SELECT T1.title AS course_title
FROM {{ ref('stg_course') }} AS T1
INNER JOIN prereq_courses ON T1.course_id = prereq_courses.course_id
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_courses_with_two_prereqs
    description: "Courses that have exactly two prerequisites."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per course with two prerequisites"
    columns:
      - name: course_title
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: Of all the contestants who got voted, what is the contestant number and name of the contestant who got least votes?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE votes (contestant_number VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE contestants (contestant_number VARCHAR, contestant_name VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_votes.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT contestant_number FROM {{ source('raw', 'votes') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_contestants.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT contestant_number, contestant_name FROM {{ source('raw', 'contestants') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: votes
      - name: contestants
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_votes
    columns:
      - name: contestant_number
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_contestants
    columns:
      - name: contestant_number
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: contestant_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_least_voted_contestant.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH voted_contestants AS (
  SELECT
    c.contestant_number,
    c.contestant_name,
    COUNT(v.contestant_number) AS vote_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_contestants') }} c
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_votes') }} v ON c.contestant_number = v.contestant_number
  GROUP BY c.contestant_number, c.contestant_name
)
SELECT contestant_number, contestant_name FROM voted_contestants ORDER BY vote_count ASC LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_least_voted_contestant
    description: "Contestant with the least votes."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per contestant with least votes"
    columns:
      - name: contestant_number
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: contestant_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_faculty.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT fac_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'faculty') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_student.sql">

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_faculty_with_students.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH faculty AS (SELECT fac_id FROM {{ ref('stg_faculty') }}),
students AS (SELECT advisor FROM {{ ref('stg_student') }})
SELECT f.fac_id

INNER JOIN students AS s ON f.fac_id = s.advisor
GROUP BY f.fac_id
HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2

Business question: How many activities does Mark Giuliano participate in?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Faculty_participates_in (facID VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Faculty (facID VARCHAR, fname VARCHAR, lname VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_faculty_participates_in.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT facID AS faculty_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Faculty_participates_in') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_faculty.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
  facID AS faculty_id,
  fname AS first_name,
  lname AS last_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Faculty') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Faculty_participates_in
      - name: Faculty
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_faculty_participates_in
    columns:
      - name: faculty_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_faculty
    columns:
      - name: faculty_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: first_name
      - name: last_name
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_mark_giuliano_activity_count.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    T1.faculty_id,
    T1.first_name,
    T1.last_name
  FROM {{ ref('stg_faculty') }} AS T1
),
participation AS (
  SELECT faculty_id FROM {{ ref('stg_faculty_participates_in') }}
)
SELECT COUNT(*) AS activity_count
FROM base
INNER JOIN participation ON base.faculty_id = participation.faculty_id
WHERE base.first_name = 'Mark'
  AND base.last_name = 'Giuliano'
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: rpt_mark_giuliano_activity_count
    description: "Count of activities Mark Giuliano participates in."
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per query"
    columns:
      - name: activity_count
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: How many faculty lines are there in the university that conferred the least number of degrees in year 2001?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE campuses (id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE faculty (faculty VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE degrees (Id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_campuses.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT id FROM {{ source('raw', 'campuses') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_faculty.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT faculty, campus FROM {{ source('raw', 'faculty') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_degrees.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT Id, campus, year, degrees FROM {{ source('raw', 'degrees') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: campuses
      - name: faculty
      - name: degrees
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_campuses
    columns:
      - name: id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_faculty
    columns:
      - name: faculty
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: campus
  - name: stg_degrees
    columns:
      - name: id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: campus
      - name: year
      - name: degrees
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_campus_degrees.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH campus_degrees AS (
  SELECT
    T1.id,
    COUNT(T3.Id) AS total_degrees,
    MIN(T2.year) AS min_year
  FROM {{ ref('stg_campuses') }} T1
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_faculty') }} T2 ON T1.id = T2.campus
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_degrees') }} T3 ON T1.id = T3.campus AND T2.year = T3.year
  WHERE T2.year = 2001
  GROUP BY T1.id
)
SELECT * FROM campus_degrees
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/_intermediate_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: int_campus_degrees
    description: "Campus degrees data for year 2001"
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_campus_degrees.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT * FROM {{ ref('int_campus_degrees') }}
)
SELECT
  id AS campus_id,
  total_degrees,
  min_year
FROM base
ORDER BY total_degrees ASC
LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_campus_degrees
    description: "Faculty lines by campus with least degrees in year 2001"
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per campus with least degrees"
    columns:
      - name: campus_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: total_degrees
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: min_year
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_store_locations.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH store_data AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_store') }}),
district_data AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_district') }}),
link_data AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_store_district') }})
SELECT
    s.store_id,
    s.store_name,
    d.headquartered_city,
    l.district_id
FROM store_data s
INNER JOIN link_data l ON s.store_id = l.store_id
INNER JOIN district_data d ON l.district_id = d.district_id
</file>

WHERE store_name = 'Blackville'

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_teachers_without_courses.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH arranged_teachers AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT teacher_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_course_arrange') }}
),
unassigned_teachers AS (
    SELECT t.teacher_name
    FROM {{ ref('stg_teacher') }} t
    WHERE NOT t.teacher_id IN (SELECT teacher_id FROM arranged_teachers)
)
SELECT
    teacher_name AS name,
    teacher_id AS teacher_id
FROM unassigned_teachers
</file>

models:

    columns:
      - name: name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: teacher_id

Business question: Find the name and nationality of the swimmer who has won (i.e., has a result of "win") more than 1 time.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE record (swimmer_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE swimmer (name VARCHAR, nationality VARCHAR, id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_swimmer.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT id AS swimmer_id, name, nationality FROM {{ source('raw', 'swimmer') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_record.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT swimmer_id, result FROM {{ source('raw', 'record') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: swimmer
      - name: record
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_swimmer
    columns:
      - name: swimmer_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_record
    columns:
      - name: swimmer_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_repeat_winners.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    s.name,
    s.nationality
  FROM {{ ref('stg_swimmer') }} AS s
  INNER JOIN {{ ref('stg_record') }} AS r ON s.swimmer_id = r.swimmer_id
  WHERE r.result = 'Win'
),
aggregated AS (
  SELECT
    name,
    nationality
  FROM base
  GROUP BY swimmer_id, name, nationality
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
)
SELECT
  name,
  nationality
FROM aggregated
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_repeat_winners
    description: "Swimmers who have won more than once."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per repeat-winning swimmer"
    columns:
      - name: name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: nationality
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH class_data AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_class') }}),

Business question: Find the country of origin for the artist who made the least number of songs?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE song (artist_name VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE artist (country VARCHAR, artist_name VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_song.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT artist_name FROM {{ source('raw', 'song') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_artist.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT country, artist_name FROM {{ source('raw', 'artist') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: song
      - name: artist
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_song
    columns:
      - name: artist_name
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_artist
    columns:
      - name: country
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: artist_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_least_prolific_artist.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH joined_data AS (
  SELECT
    a.country,
    s.artist_name,
    COUNT(*) AS song_count
  FROM {{ ref('stg_artist') }} a
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_song') }} s ON a.artist_name = s.artist_name
  GROUP BY a.country, s.artist_name
),
ranked_artists AS (
  SELECT
    country,
    artist_name,
    song_count,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY song_count ASC) AS rank
  FROM joined_data
)
SELECT country
FROM ranked_artists
WHERE rank = 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_least_prolific_artist
    description: "Country of origin for the artist with the least number of songs."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per least prolific artist"
    columns:
      - name: country
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Find the number of funiture types produced by each manufacturer as well as the company names.

SQL schemas:

<file path="models/staging/stg_manufacturer.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

    name,
    manufacturer_id AS manufacturer_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'manufacturer') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_furniture_manufacte.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

FROM {{ source('raw', 'furniture_manufacte') }}

  - name: raw

      - name: manufacturer

version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_manufacturer

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_furniture_types_by_manufacturer.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH manufacturer_data AS (
    SELECT
        name,
        manufacturer_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_manufacturer') }}
),
furniture_data AS (
    SELECT
        manufacturer_id
    FROM {{ ref('stg_furniture_manufacte') }}

FROM manufacturer_data AS t1

GROUP BY t1.manufacturer_id, t1.name
</file>

Business question: What are the average fastest lap speed in races held after 2004 grouped by race name and ordered by year?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE results (fastestlapspeed INTEGER, raceid VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE races (name VARCHAR, year INTEGER, raceid VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_races.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    name,
    year,
    raceid AS race_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'races') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_results.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    fastestlapspeed AS fastest_lap_speed,
    raceid AS race_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'results') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: races
      - name: results
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_races
    columns:
      - name: race_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_results
    columns:
      - name: race_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_average_fastest_lap_speed.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
    SELECT
        r.name,
        r.year,
        r.race_id,
        res.fastest_lap_speed
    FROM {{ ref('stg_races') }} r
    INNER JOIN {{ ref('stg_results') }} res ON r.race_id = res.race_id
    WHERE r.year > 2014
)
SELECT
    name,
    year,
    AVG(fastest_lap_speed) AS average_fastest_lap_speed
FROM base
GROUP BY name, year
ORDER BY year
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_average_fastest_lap_speed
    description: "Average fastest lap speed per race after 2014, grouped by race name and year."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per race (name + year)"
    columns:
      - name: name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: year
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: average_fastest_lap_speed
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Find the id and local authority of the station with has the highest average high temperature.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE weekly_weather (station_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE station (id VARCHAR, local_authority VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_weekly_weather.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT station_id, high_temperature FROM {{ source('raw', 'weekly_weather') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_station.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT id AS station_id, local_authority FROM {{ source('raw', 'station') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2

  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: weekly_weather
      - name: station
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2

  - name: stg_weekly_weather
    columns:
      - name: station_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: high_temperature
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_station
    columns:
      - name: station_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: local_authority
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_station_highest_avg_temp.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    w.station_id,
    s.local_authority,
    AVG(w.high_temperature) AS avg_high_temp
  FROM {{ ref('stg_weekly_weather') }} w
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_station') }} s ON w.station_id = s.station_id
  GROUP BY w.station_id, s.local_authority
)
SELECT
  station_id,
  local_authority
FROM base
ORDER BY avg_high_temp DESC
LIMIT 1
</file>

Business question: Find the average number of followers for the users who do not have any tweet.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE user_profiles (followers INTEGER, UID VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE tweets (followers INTEGER, UID VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_user_profiles.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT followers, UID AS user_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'user_profiles') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_tweets.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT followers, UID AS user_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'tweets') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: user_profiles
      - name: tweets
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_user_profiles
    columns:
      - name: user_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_tweets
    columns:
      - name: user_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_avg_followers_no_tweets.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH non_tweeting_users AS (
  SELECT user_id
  FROM {{ ref('stg_user_profiles') }}
  WHERE NOT user_id IN (SELECT user_id FROM {{ ref('stg_tweets') }})
),
result AS (
  SELECT followers
  FROM {{ ref('stg_user_profiles') }}
  WHERE user_id IN (SELECT user_id FROM non_tweeting_users)
)
SELECT AVG(followers) AS avg_followers_no_tweets FROM result
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2

  - name: raw

      - name: Highschooler
      - name: Friend

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_highschooler
    description: "Raw highschooler data."
    columns:
      - name: id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: student_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_friend
    description: "Raw friend data."
    columns:
      - name: id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: student_id

</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_highschooler.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

<file path="models/staging/stg_friend.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    id AS friend_id,
    student_id AS student_friend_id

</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_students_without_friends.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH highschooler_ids AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT student_highschooler_id

),
friend_student_ids AS (

)

FROM highschooler_ids
LEFT JOIN friend_student_ids ON highschooler_ids.student_highschooler_id = friend_student_ids.student_friend_id
WHERE friend_student_ids.student_friend_id IS NULL
</file>

Business question: What is average lesson price taught by staff with first name as Janessa and last name as Sawayn?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE Lessons (staff_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE Staff (staff_id VARCHAR, first_name VARCHAR, last_name VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_lessons.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT staff_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'Lessons') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_staff.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    staff_id,
    first_name,
    last_name
FROM {{ source('raw', 'Staff') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: Lessons
      - name: Staff
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_lessons
    columns:
      - name: staff_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_staff
    columns:
      - name: staff_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: first_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: last_name
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_avg_lesson_price_janessa_sawayn.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (
  SELECT l.staff_id, s.first_name, s.last_name
  FROM {{ ref('stg_lessons') }} l
  JOIN {{ ref('stg_staff') }} s ON l.staff_id = s.staff_id
  WHERE s.first_name = 'Janessa'
    AND s.last_name = 'Sawayn'
)

FROM base
</file>

    columns:
      - name: avg_lesson_price
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Are the customers holding coupons with amount 500 bad or good?

SQL schemas:

<file path="models/staging/stg_discount_coupons.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}

    CAST(coupon_amount AS INTEGER) AS coupon_amount

</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_customers.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    good_or_bad_customer AS is_good_customer,
    coupon_id AS coupon_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'customers') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2

  - name: raw

models:

      - name: coupon_id

{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (

        c.is_good_customer,

    FROM {{ ref('stg_customers') }} c

Business question: Find the driver id and number of races of all drivers who have at most participated in 30 races?

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE races (raceid VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE results (driverid VARCHAR, raceid VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE drivers (driverid VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_races.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT raceid AS race_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'races') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_results.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT driverid AS driver_id, raceid AS race_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'results') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_drivers.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT driverid AS driver_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'drivers') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: races
      - name: results
      - name: drivers
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_races
    columns:
      - name: race_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_results
    columns:
      - name: driver_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: race_id
        tests: [not_null]
  - name: stg_drivers
    columns:
      - name: driver_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/int_driver_race_counts.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
WITH drivers AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_drivers') }}),
results AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_results') }})
SELECT d.driver_id, COUNT(*) AS race_count
FROM drivers d
INNER JOIN results r ON d.driver_id = r.driver_id
GROUP BY d.driver_id
</file>

<file path="models/intermediate/_intermediate_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: int_driver_race_counts
    description: "Count of races per driver."
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_drivers_with_le_30_races.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('int_driver_race_counts') }})
SELECT driver_id, race_count
FROM base
WHERE race_count <= 30
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_drivers_with_le_30_races
    description: "Drivers who have participated in at most 30 races."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per driver with <= 30 races"
    columns:
      - name: driver_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
      - name: race_count
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

Business question: Find the emails of customers who has filed a complaints of the product with the most complaints.

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE customers (email_address VARCHAR, customer_id VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE complaints (customer_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_customers.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    email_address,
    customer_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'customers') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_complaints.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT
    customer_id
FROM {{ source('raw', 'complaints') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: customers
      - name: complaints
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_customers
    columns:
      - name: email_address
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: customer_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_complaints
    columns:
      - name: customer_id
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/fct_most_complaining_customers.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH customer_complaints AS (
    SELECT
        c.email_address,
        COUNT(cp.customer_id) AS complaint_count
    FROM {{ ref('stg_customers') }} c
    JOIN {{ ref('stg_complaints') }} cp ON c.customer_id = cp.customer_id
    GROUP BY c.email_address, c.customer_id
)
SELECT email_address FROM customer_complaints ORDER BY complaint_count DESC LIMIT 1
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: fct_most_complaining_customers
    description: "Email addresses of customers who filed the most complaints."
    meta:
      model_type: fact
      grain: "1 row per customer with highest complaint count"
    columns:
      - name: email_address
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>

Business question: List the name of all the distinct customers who have orders with status "Packing".

SQL schemas:
CREATE TABLE orders (customer_id VARCHAR, order_status VARCHAR); CREATE TABLE customers (customer_name VARCHAR, customer_id VARCHAR)

<file path="models/staging/stg_customers.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT customer_name, customer_id FROM {{ source('raw', 'customers') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/stg_orders.sql">
{{ config(materialized='view') }}
SELECT customer_id, order_status FROM {{ source('raw', 'orders') }}
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_sources.yml">
version: 2
sources:
  - name: raw
    tables:
      - name: customers
      - name: orders
</file>

<file path="models/staging/_staging_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: stg_customers
    columns:
      - name: customer_name
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: customer_id
        tests: [not_null, unique]
  - name: stg_orders
    columns:
      - name: customer_id
        tests: [not_null]
      - name: order_status
        tests: [not_null]
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/rpt_customers_with_packing_orders.sql">
{{ config(materialized='table') }}
WITH base AS (SELECT * FROM {{ ref('stg_customers') }}),
orders_filtered AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT customer_id
  FROM {{ ref('stg_orders') }}
  WHERE order_status = 'Packing'
)
SELECT DISTINCT b.customer_name
FROM base b
INNER JOIN orders_filtered o ON b.customer_id = o.customer_id
</file>

<file path="models/marts/core/_core_models.yml">
version: 2
models:
  - name: rpt_customers_with_packing_orders
    description: "List of distinct customers who have orders with status 'Packing'."
    meta:
      model_type: report
      grain: "1 row per customer with packing orders"
    columns:
      - name: customer_name
        tests: [not_null, unique]
</file>
